steps.m: to be free of faults/to be perfect

steps.n: This special form creates a noun that declares Allah's complete perfection and freedom from any faults or limitations. In the Quran, it's often used to declare Allah's perfection.
Added kasra vowel mark to show basic form

steps.m: who/which/that (connects to أَسْرَىٰ - took on a night journey)
steps.n: Adding ال changes this from meaning 'possessor' to being a relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English. Here it connects to the verb أَسْرَىٰ (took on a night journey).
The ل gets a shadda for emphasis, and the initial alif becomes a waṣla (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word in pronunciation
The final ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top in the Uthmani script
This root relates to nighttime travel
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show making someone else do the action. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) in this pattern.

steps.m: he made (someone) travel at night (referring to Muhammad ﷺ making the night journey)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show this is a special type of alif at the end. This is because the word ends with the weak letter ي.
Added fatha and sukun to make the noun 'servant' from the basic meaning 'serve'
The preposition بِ is added to indicate 'with' or 'by'
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it's connected to the next word meaning 'his'. It's like saying 'with servant-of' before saying whose servant.
Added هِۦ to show the servant belongs to Allah. The ۦ just extends the sound.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun 'night'. The ي is a weak letter that stays silent with a sukun.

steps.m: at night (describing when أَسْرَىٰ/took on journey happened)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word answers 'when?' about the journey. Like saying 'I traveled at night' in English.
This is the basic form of the particle meaning 'from'. It shows where something starts or comes from.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending in ن or م sound, making the م sound stronger.
The fatha (َ) is added at the end to connect this word to the next word الْمَسْجِدِ (the mosque), showing what something is from.

steps.m: to prostrate, to bow down in worship

steps.n: Added مَ at start to show 'place where' something happens, like how a mosque is where people pray

steps.m: the mosque (connected to الْحَرَامِ - the Sacred)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it definite (the). The ِ ending shows it's connected to the word الْحَرَامِ (the Sacred) that comes after it, forming 'the Sacred Mosque'
The added alif makes it a noun describing something that is permanently forbidden or sacred

steps.m: the sacred (part of المسجد الحرام - the Sacred Mosque)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it belongs to/describes المسجد
This is a preposition showing where Allah took His servant - from one mosque to another. It connects the word 'His servant' (بِعَبْدِهِۦ) with 'the mosque' (ٱلْمَسْجِدِ)

steps.m: to prostrate, bow down in worship

steps.n: Adding مَ at the beginning and ِ after ج turns this into a noun meaning 'the place where prostration happens'

steps.m: the place of prostration (referring to the Sacred Mosque)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific and ِ at the end shows it's connected to another word. This refers to the Sacred Mosque (ٱلْحَرَامِ) mentioned right after it.
Added vowels to connect the letters and the weak letter و changes to ا at the end
Added أ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'most distant/farthest'
Added ال to specify this is referring to a particular farthest mosque mentioned in the ayah
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to connect clauses. It refers to a singular masculine subject.

steps.m: The one who (refers to Allah in أَسْرَىٰ)
steps.n: Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it definite. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who took His servant on the night journey.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes the blessing more extensive/intensive
The alif is written as a small line above the letter (ٰ) and the last vowel is removed with sukun
Adding نَا at the end shows that 'we' did the blessing
Adding fatha creates a noun meaning the space or area around something
Adding sukun makes it a complete noun meaning 'surroundings' or 'area around'

steps.m: around it (referring to Al-Masjid Al-Aqsa which was blessed)
steps.n: The هُ suffix shows this refers to Al-Masjid Al-Aqsa (the Farthest Mosque). This word tells us what area Allah blessed - the area around the mosque.
Added نُ at start to mean 'we' and changed middle vowel to make it mean 'show to someone' instead of just 'see'
لِ at the start means 'so that' or 'in order to'
The هُ suffix shows who is being shown to - in this case 'him' referring to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ who was taken on the night journey
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it shows where the night journey began - from the Sacred Mosque.
Added sukun (ْ) on the ن to show the word stops here
Changes the root into a noun meaning 'sign' or 'miracle' - something that shows or makes clear
Makes the word plural by adding ات - this is a common way to make feminine nouns plural
Changes to show something owns or possesses these signs
Adds 'our' to show these are Allah's signs being shown to the Prophet. The final alif is lengthened to show emphasis.
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it more certain and important. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English.
Added هُ at the end to show who is being talked about. This connects to the next statement where it says 'He is the All-Hearing'.
The small ۥ mark shows how to pronounce the هُ with a longer 'u' sound at the end

This is a pronoun that refers back to Allah through His attribute السَّمِيعُ (The All-Hearing) mentioned right after it in the ayah. The هُ shows it's talking about one male/masculine thing, and the وَ makes it a separate word instead of attaching to another word.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who hears perfectly (attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Changed the word to show someone who hears in a perfect, complete way - this form is often used for divine attributes
Added ال and merged the س with a shadda to show this is THE All-Hearing One (Allah)
Added the ُ ending to show this is describing Allah in this ayah
Added ي between second and third root letters with vowels to show someone who has this quality very strongly
Adding ال makes it definite and specific. When used as one of Allah's names, it means He sees and knows everything completely. The ُ shows this word describes Allah in this ayah.
Root أتي became past tense verb 'gave' by adding alif at start and changing last letter to alif
Changed the final ى to ي with sukun to prepare for suffix
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word shows what we gave to Moses.
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew. While it ends with an alif maqsura (ى), this is part of the borrowed name rather than an Arabic morphological process.
Added damma (ُ) on م and fatha (َ) on س to show correct pronunciation. This word is the object (receiver) of the verb ءَاتَيْنَا (We gave) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

The pattern فِعَال is commonly used to create nouns from verbs, especially for things that result from the action. This word is in accusative case because it is the direct object (the thing being given) of the verb ءَاتَيْنَا (We gave).
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being given. The alif is written as ٰ (dagger alif) in this word. The hamza on the definite article is written as ٱ.

steps.m: to make or set something as something else

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added sukun to prepare for attachment of نا
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show what received the action. This connects to هُدًى (guidance) in the ayah - we made it into guidance.
Added و to connect this with the previous statement about giving Moses the Book
Changes from verb 'to guide' into the noun 'guidance'

steps.m: a way of guidance (that Allah gave to the Children of Israel)
steps.n: Adding the two dots (tanwin) at the end makes it mean 'a guidance' instead of 'the guidance'
Adding vowel marks to create basic past tense verb form
Added لِ (meaning 'for/to') at the start to show it's directed towards the children of Israel
Added shadda on ل and elongation mark (ى with ٓ) at the end for pronunciation

steps.m: Israel (name of Prophet Yaqub عليه السلام)
steps.n: This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew meaning 'one who struggles with God'. It refers to Prophet Yaqub (Jacob) عليه السلام who was given this name by Allah.

steps.m: Israel (name of Prophet Yaqub عليه السلام)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and modified spelling to match Quranic orthography. The ٰٓ (alif with madda) replaces the ا, and the ء replaces the ئ. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.
This is a particle that introduces a new clause
When أَنْ combines with لا, the ن merges into the ل making it doubled (with a shadda). This word connects to تَتَّخِذُوا۟ (you take) to mean 'that you do not take'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The ت is doubled (has shadda) to show this is done with more effort than just 'taking'
The ت at the start shows this is either 'she' or 'you' doing the action
The ُ at the end shows this is a normal present tense verb (not changed by any other words)

steps.m: you (all) take for yourselves (as وَكِيلًا - a guardian/protector)
steps.n: Added plural marker وا to show multiple people are being addressed. This word is followed by وَكِيلًا which specifies what is being taken.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with the word دُونِى (besides me/other than me) to show what not to take as protectors.

steps.m: to be lower or beneath something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'beneath/lower than'
Added ِى at the end to show this means 'besides me'. This word appears in a phrase warning against taking others besides Allah as protectors.

steps.m: to take care of or manage something for someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: one who takes care of things (guardian/trustee)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third letters with kasra on middle letter to show someone who does this action as their role

steps.m: a guardian/trustee (as the object being appointed)
steps.n: Added tanween fatHa to show this word is receiving the action of being appointed or taken as something

steps.m: to spread out or scatter (like seeds or children)

steps.n: Changes the root into a concrete noun referring to descendants
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being described by what comes after it in the ayah
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to the people who were carried with Noah in the ark. In this ayah, it's specifically referring to the descendants of those people.
Added the three fatha marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he carried'
Added sukun to prepare for adding 'we' ending

steps.m: we carried (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. In this ayah, 'we' refers to Allah speaking in the royal plural form.

steps.m: with (connects نُوحٍ/Noah to those who were carried)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows who was together with someone else. In this ayah, it shows who was carried along with Noah.
This is the proper name Noah, which comes from a root meaning related to lamenting or mourning. The name became a proper noun.

steps.m: Noah (proper name in genitive case, possessed by مَعَ)
steps.n: The double kasra (tanwin) at the end shows this is in the genitive case because it comes after the word مَعَ (with) in the phrase 'with Noah'
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed he (emphasizes كَانَ عَبْدًا شَكُورًا - he was a grateful servant)
steps.n: Added هُ which means 'he/him' to show who is being talked about. This connects to the next part of the ayah that tells us 'he was a grateful servant'
Added fathas to make it past tense

steps.m: he was (describing عَبْدًا شَكُورًا - a grateful servant)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle of a past tense verb. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added fatha and sukun to make the noun 'servant'. This word comes after كَانَ (was) in the ayah, so it describes what someone was.
Added tanween (double line marks) to show it's 'a servant' rather than 'the servant'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: very thankful (describing someone who thanks a lot)
steps.n: This form shows someone who does the action very much or habitually

steps.m: very thankful (describing Noah as a servant in كَانَ عَبْدًا)
steps.n: Adding ًا to the end makes it describe Noah as a thankful servant (connects to كَانَ عَبْدًا)
Added fathas to make past tense. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end of a word.
Added نا to show 'we' did the action. The ى changes back to يْ before the suffix.
Added و to connect with previous statement, and lengthened the final alif for emphasis
This is a basic particle that shows direction or connection. Here it connects 'We decreed' with 'Children of Israel'
Added the vowel mark kasra (ِ) under the إ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'i-laa'
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced with a long 'aa' sound at the end

steps.m: to build, establish, or create offspring

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb

steps.m: sons/children (in a possessive or connected state)
steps.n: Changed from 'he built' to 'children/sons of' by changing the middle vowel - this form is used when saying 'children of' someone (like 'children of Israel')
Adding ى at the end shows this word will connect to what comes after it (like in 'children of Israel')
This is a proper noun borrowed into Arabic. It combines two Hebrew words: يسرا (Yisra) meaning 'he struggles/persists' and ايل (El) meaning 'God', so the full meaning is 'he who struggles with God'.

steps.m: Israel (name of Prophet Jacob, object of إِلَىٰ)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and modified spelling to standard Quranic form. This word is the object of إِلَىٰ (to) in this verse.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. It has no root since it's a primitive word.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how it's pronounced
Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words. This word shows the location 'in' for ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The verb 'he wrote' changes to mean the thing that is written (a book). Like how drawing makes a drawing, writing makes a book.
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after the preposition في (in/within) in the ayah
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Changes from past to present tense and adds the doer (you/she)
Adds emphasis - means 'surely/definitely'
Added نَّ at the end to make the meaning stronger and more certain - like saying 'definitely' or 'for sure'
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that something is written or contained in الْكِتٰبِ (the Book/Scripture).
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a proper word
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific earth/ground rather than any earth/ground
The word 'earth' gets a kasra (small diagonal line) under it because it comes after the word في (meaning 'in'). This is like saying 'in the earth' instead of just 'the earth'.

steps.m: to pass, repeat, or go through
steps.n: Basic meaning of passing or going through
Connected the root letters and added a shaddah (double) on ر to make the past tense verb 'he passed'
Added ة to show one specific instance of the action

steps.m: two times (describing لَتُفْسِدُنَّ - you will cause corruption)
steps.n: Added يْنِ to show exactly two instances. This describes how many times the corruption will occur.
وا shows they (plural)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added ت at start to mean 'you' and changed vowels to make it present tense
Added emphatic لَ to show certainty about future action
Added و to connect this phrase with what came before

steps.m: and you will most certainly rise
steps.n: Added نَّ for extra emphasis and certainty
وا shows they (plural)
Changed the verb into an abstract noun by adding damma vowels and doubling the final و

steps.m: with great arrogance (describes how they acted in وَلَتَعْلُنَّ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how they acted arrogantly. This connects to the verb وَلَتَعْلُنَّ (and you will surely act haughtily) in the ayah.
Past tense showing something became great or significant
Changes from action to description by adding ي between second and last root letters
Adds ًا at the end to show this describes how they exalted themselves, connecting to عُلُوًّا (exaltedness) in the ayah
This is a time word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it introduces when something came.

steps.m: then when (introduces the time of جَآءَ/came)
steps.n: Adding فَ at the beginning shows this happens after something else mentioned before. It connects this timing to what happened earlier.
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he came' in the past
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter

steps.m: to promise or make a commitment to do something

steps.n: Root letters were given vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make a noun meaning 'a promise' from the basic verb meaning 'to promise'
The ُ shows this word is doing the action - here, the promise is what came
This is the root meaning first or beginning
Added vowels and special ending to make it feminine. This word is used for describing the first of two things.
Added هُمَا at the end to show it belongs to two things. The small alif (ٰ) is added before هُمَا to connect the words properly.

steps.m: to send forth or raise up (especially with a purpose or mission)
steps.n: Basic meaning of sending or dispatching
Adding fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb form
Adding نا changes the subject from 'he' to 'we'. In this ayah, Allah is speaking about sending powerful servants against the Children of Israel.
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon you all (subject of بَعَثْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to بَعَثْنَا (we sent) in the ayah, showing who was sent against.
Changed the word pattern to make it plural. The alif after ب makes it plural.
Added tanween fatha (ًا) at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb بَعَثْنَا (we sent).
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Added نا which means 'us' or 'our', making it specific to first person plural
The alif is elongated (آ) for emphasis in pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning
This is a special word that doesn't come from a typical root. It's used to show who has something or what kind of people we're talking about.

steps.m: possessors of (followed by بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ - great might)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. In this ayah, it connects to 'great might' to mean 'people of great might'.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the result of being powerful
Adds a special ending to show this word is describing شَدِيدٍ (severe), together meaning 'severe might'
Root meaning strength or intensity
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to make it an intensive adjective
Added tanwin kasra to show this describes the might (بَأْسٍ) mentioned in the ayah
Root meaning to search or explore
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding alif between first two letters and fatha marks
Added وا to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: so they searched through violently (after بَعَثْنَا/We sent)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action happened as a result of the previous action (being sent)
Root meaning penetration or permeation
Changes from root to plural noun meaning spaces or gaps
Makes the word plural by adding alif after second letter
Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon. The word shows where the people went when they 'explored/searched' (فَجَاسُوا).

steps.m: to move around in a place, to inhabit
steps.n: Root meaning dwelling or circling
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the long 'aa' sound to mean 'a place where people live'
Changed the word to its plural form by adding ي after first letter and changing vowels
Added ال (the) at the beginning and doubled the د with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object being moved through in the phrase جَاسُوا۟ خِلٰلَ ٱلدِّيَارِ (they went through the homes)
Root meaning being or existing
Added past tense vowels. The middle و changes to ا in past tense, which is common for these types of words.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Root meaning promise
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to turn the action into a noun meaning 'a promise'
The word changed from 'promise' to 'a promise' by adding the double marks (tanween) at the end. It's like adding 'a/an' in English. This word is affected by كان which puts it in a special form.
Root meaning doing or acting
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show what receives the action instead of what does the action
Added ًا and doubled the م to show this word is the object (something that was fulfilled)
Basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else

steps.m: then (connecting رَدَدْنَا with what came before)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on first letter and doubled the م with shadda (ّ) to strengthen its meaning. This word connects this new part about Allah returning power to the Muslims with what happened before.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb. The sukun (ْ) on the last د shows where the word could take endings.

steps.m: we returned (the power to you against them)
steps.n: Added نَا to show 'we' did the action. This verb connects to لَكُمُ (to you) and عَلَيْهِمْ (against them) later in the ayah, showing who received the return of power.
Basic preposition indicating purpose or direction

steps.m: for you all (subject of رَدَدْنَا - we returned)
steps.n: Added the plural pronoun كُمُ (you all) to show who the action is directed towards. This word connects to رَدَدْنَا (we returned) showing who received the action.
The doubled ر shows intensity in the meaning. This word is used in the context of returning to attack enemies.
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of returning to attack
Added definite article and final fatha. This word refers to the specific instance of returning to attack mentioned in this ayah.
The ى changes to ي when connecting to pronouns

steps.m: upon them/against them (object of رَدَدْنَا - we returned)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them'. This word shows who was affected by the action رَدَدْنَا (we returned) in this ayah.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show causing someone else to do the action. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) mark to show it's not moving.
Changed from basic verb form to 'we' form by adding نا at the end, showing that 'we' did the action
Added وَ (and) to connect this action with what came before it in the verse
Added كُم to show who received the action (you all). This connects to the previous verse mentioning returning power to the Children of Israel.
Root letters م-و-ل become مَال (māl) - a simple noun meaning one piece/unit of wealth
Changes the word to mean 'many types of wealth' using a common plural pattern

steps.m: with wealth (used with وَأَمْدَدْنٰكُم - 'and We aided you')
steps.n: Adds 'with' to show how Allah aided them, and adds tanwin to show it's indefinite. This word connects to وَأَمْدَدْنٰكُم (and We aided you) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
These are two different words - 'banaa' means 'to build' while 'bani' means 'son/children of'. They are not related by transformation.
Added plural ending to show multiple sons
Added conjunction 'and' at beginning

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added نٰ to show 'we' are doing the action
Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous actions in the verse (giving you victory and supporting you)

steps.m: and we made you all (into a more numerous people)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who was made into something (you all). In this verse, it refers to making the Children of Israel into a more numerous group.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: more numerous (describing نَفِيرًا - the number of people)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'more' instead of just 'many'. Like changing 'many' to 'more numerous' in English. This word tells us about the number of people (نَفِيرًا).
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to create a noun meaning 'group'

steps.m: a group (object of جَعَلْنٰكُمْ أَكْثَرَ - we made you more numerous in)
steps.n: Added tanween fatah to show this word is the object being acted upon in the phrase 'we made you more numerous in group'

steps.m: if (introduces the condition أَسَأْتُمْ - you do evil)
steps.n: This word shows that what happens next depends on a condition. Here it introduces 'if you do evil' as the condition.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at start to mean 'to make something' or 'to cause' - changes from 'being good' to 'making good'
Adding تُ at the end changes it from 'he did good' to 'I did good'
Changes from 'I did good' to 'you all did good' - adds م to make it plural you
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at the start changes meaning from 'being good' to 'doing good' (makes it active)
Adding تُ changes it from 'he did good' to 'I did good'
The تم suffix changes the verb to plural 'you all' form in past tense
Basic noun pattern that turns the root into a concrete noun meaning 'soul'
This makes the word plural, like changing 'self' to 'selves'
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose or direction
Added kasra to show this word will be possessed by something
Added كُمْ to show these selves belong to you all

steps.m: if (connects to أَسَأْتُمْ - you did evil)
steps.n: This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it connects to أَسَأْتُمْ (you did evil) to mean 'if you do evil'

steps.m: and if (connects to أَسَأْتُمْ - you did evil)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning changes it from 'being bad' to 'doing something badly'
The long vowel ا changes to hamza (أ) before adding endings
Adding تُمْ shows that multiple people (you all) did the action in the past
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to make it pronounceable
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement (connects to أَسَأْتُمْ - 'you did evil')
Added ها at the end to show this refers back to the evil deed mentioned (أَسَأْتُمْ - 'you did evil')
This is a basic conditional particle that shows when something happens. It's used to connect two events in time, like saying 'when X happens, Y will happen'.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. Here it connects to 'and if you do evil' (وَإِنْ أَسَأْتُمْ) to show what happens next.
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to standard spelling of past tense 'came' by adding alif
Alternative spelling showing the extended sound of 'came'
Root letters turned into noun meaning 'promise' by adding vowel marks
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in جَآءَ (came)

steps.m: to delay or come after something

steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'delay/after' to active participle meaning 'last/final' by adding ا after first letter
Added ة to make it refer to a specific thing (the final world/life) rather than just the general concept of being last
Added ال to make it refer to THE final life specifically (the Hereafter), not just any final thing
Added ِ to show this word belongs to وَعْدُ (promise) in the phrase وَعْدُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ (promise of the Hereafter)
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Adds purpose meaning with لِ prefix

steps.m: so that they harm (وُجُوهَكُمْ - your faces)
steps.n: Changes from singular to plural by adding وا at the end. This word connects to وُجُوهَكُمْ (your faces) which is what they will harm.

steps.m: to face or turn towards something

steps.n: Root meaning 'face/turn' becomes the noun 'face'
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple faces
Added fatha to prepare for possession suffix
Added كُمْ to show the faces belong to multiple people being addressed
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Added final ُ to complete present tense form
Added ل at beginning to show purpose/intent
Added و at beginning to connect with previous clause

steps.m: and so that they enter (to enter المَسْجِدَ - the mosque)
steps.n: Changed from 'he enters' to 'they enter' by adding وا at the end - shows multiple people will enter the mosque
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding مَ at the start and changing the vowels makes it mean 'the place where this action happens'
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's the object being entered in the phrase 'so they may enter the mosque'
This is a comparison word that shows something is similar to something else
Adding ما makes it refer to a whole action or situation instead of just one thing. In this ayah, it refers to how they entered the first time.
Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-a
The suffix وا indicates 'they (masculine)' in past tense verbs

steps.m: they entered it (referring to المسجد/the mosque)
steps.n: The هُ at the end refers back to المسجد (the mosque) mentioned earlier in the verse, showing what they entered
Added fatha and shadda to connect the letters and show emphasis

steps.m: one instance of passing by, a time

steps.n: Adds -in sound at the end because it comes after 'first' (أَوَّلَ)
The doubled ب shows that the destruction is total and intense
Changed from past tense (he destroyed) to present tense (he destroys) by adding يُ at start and changing the vowels inside the word
Added لِ to show purpose/intent

steps.m: and so that he destroys completely
steps.n: Added وَ to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and so that they destroy completely
steps.n: Added plural marker to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: like/just as (connects to دَخَلُوهُ - they entered it)
steps.n: In this ayah, مَا is used with ك to mean 'like/just as', comparing how they will enter to how they entered before
Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters and make it past tense
Added the plural masculine past tense suffix وْا to show multiple people did this action. In this context, it shows their arrogant behavior.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from verb to noun showing intense/complete action

steps.m: with complete destruction (describes how ليتبروا was done)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word describes how the action ليتبروا (they destroyed) was done - with complete destruction
Adds fatHa vowels and ى to make it a complete verb meaning 'perhaps'
Added an alif khanjariyah (ٰ) to show the long 'aa' sound more clearly. This doesn't change the meaning.
The doubled ب shows this is someone's permanent role or quality as a nurturer

steps.m: the nurturer/lord (subject of يَرْحَمَكُمْ - may have mercy on you)
steps.n: The damma shows this word is doing the action of having mercy (يَرْحَمَكُمْ) in this ayah

steps.m: your (all of you) lord (subject of يَرْحَمَكُمْ - may have mercy on you)
steps.n: The word 'lord' becomes 'your lord' by adding كُمْ (kum) at the end, which means 'your' when talking to many people

This is a particle that connects 'عَسَىٰ' (it may be) with 'يَرْحَمَكُمْ' (He will have mercy on you), showing what might happen. It works like 'that' in English phrases like 'I hope that you are well'.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he shows/will show mercy to you all (connected to عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ - 'perhaps your Lord')
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who receives the mercy. This verb is part of what might happen from your Lord (عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ).
This is a basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else happening

steps.m: and if (connects this condition to رَحْمَة/mercy mentioned before)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with Allah's mercy mentioned in the previous part
Command form ('return!') - the و drops out to make it shorter and easier to say
Added تُمْ at the end to show that multiple people are being addressed
The ت and م merged together with a shadda, but meaning stays the same
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb. The middle weak letter و changes to ا.
The middle weak letter is dropped and the first vowel changes to damma when adding certain endings.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This is a past tense verb where 'we' is doing the returning.

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the root letters together, making it a past tense verb meaning 'he made'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. The ْ (sukun) is added before نَا to help pronunciation.

steps.m: and we made/put (connected to عُدْنَا - we returned)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with the previous statement 'we returned' (عُدْنَا). This shows these actions happened together.
This is a proper noun (foreign loanword) that doesn't derive from an Arabic root. The word جهنم (Jahannam) refers to Hell/Hellfire in Islamic terminology.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation and grammatical role. This word is the object (thing being acted upon) of جَعَلْنَا (We made) in this ayah.

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
The word changed from singular (one person who rejects) to plural (many people who reject). The ين ending shows it's plural and comes after the word 'for' (ل)
The preposition ل is added to show purpose/destination, connecting the disbelievers to Hell mentioned earlier

steps.m: for the disbelievers (who Hell is made for)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite ('the'). This word is connected to جَهَنَّمَ (Hell) in the ayah, showing Hell is made for these disbelievers.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that means the place where the action happens
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb 'we made' (جَعَلْنَا). The full meaning in context is 'We made Hell a prison for the disbelievers.'
This word emphasizes the importance of what comes after it. Here it emphasizes 'this Quran'. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show this emphasis.
Basic demonstrative word used to point to something nearby
The small alif (ٰ) above the ه adds emphasis to the pointing action
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added alif at the end to show this is pointing to something masculine and singular (the Quran). This word is followed by ٱلْقُرْءَانَ (the Quran) which it's pointing to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes the verb 'read' into a noun meaning 'that which is read intensively'
Added ال to make it specific - this isn't just any recited thing, it's THE recited book

steps.m: the Quran (as the object of يَهْدِى)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being guided to in the phrase 'guides to'
Added basic past tense form with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Adding ي at start shows it's happening now, not in the past
The يَ tells us it's 'he' doing the action in present tense
This is the basic feminine relative pronoun used to connect clauses, like saying 'which' or 'that' when talking about feminine things
Added shadda on ل and vowel marks to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to that which (feminine) (connects يَهْدِى to هِىَ)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show direction or purpose. This connects the verb يَهْدِى (guides) to what follows (هِىَ - it is), showing what the Quran guides to.

steps.m: it (referring to the way/path mentioned in يَهْدِى)
steps.n: Basic feminine pronoun used here to refer back to the path or way that the Quran guides to. In the ayah, it connects what comes before (that the Quran guides to a way) with what comes after (that this way is most upright).
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb

steps.m: most straight/upright (describing هِىَ 'it' referring to the way)
steps.n: Changed to the comparative/superlative form by adding أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end, with a sukun on the first root letter. This word describes how the way (mentioned as هِىَ) is the most straight/upright.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense and shows that the good news is being given directly to someone

steps.m: he gives good news (intense) (connected to يَهْدِى - guides)
steps.n: Adding يُ shows this happens in the present, like يَهْدِى (guides) earlier in the verse

steps.m: and he gives good news (intense) (connected to يَهْدِى - guides)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to يَهْدِى (guides) - the Quran guides AND gives good news
Adding ُ shows this is a present tense verb meaning 'he gives good news'
Changes the action into the person who does it, like changing 'trust' to 'truster'

steps.m: multiple people who trust/feel secure (object of يُبَشِّرُ - gives glad tidings to)
steps.n: Makes it refer to many people. This word receives the action of يُبَشِّرُ (gives glad tidings to)

steps.m: the specific group of people who trust/feel secure
steps.n: Adds ال to show we're talking about a specific group, not just any trusting people

steps.m: the believers (receiving glad tidings from يُبَشِّرُ)
steps.n: Shows this word is receiving the action of giving glad tidings
Relative pronoun used to describe a group by what they do. The shadda on ل is due to assimilation with definite article
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the past tense verb
Changes from 'did/worked' (past) to 'does/works' (present) by adding يَ at start

steps.m: they do/act/work (referring to the believers mentioned in ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end to match the plural believers (ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ)
Changes from verb 'to be good' to noun meaning 'one who is good'
Changes from singular 'righteous' to feminine plural form to mean multiple good deeds
Makes it definite. This word is what is being done in يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/perform)
The word gets a kasra (i) ending because it's the object being acted upon by the verb يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/perform)

steps.m: that (connecting لَهُمْ to the previous statement)
steps.n: Added shadda to strengthen the word, making it emphasize that what follows is definitely true. Here it connects 'they have' (لَهُمْ) to the previous statement about the believers.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for them/they have (connected to أَجْرًا - a reward)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this is 'for them'. This word connects to أَجْرًا (a reward) in the ayah to mean 'they have a reward'
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun instead of an action
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being affected by أَنَّ (that) in the sentence. This means the reward is what is being talked about or promised.

Added ي between second and third root letters to make it into an adjective that describes how big or great something is. In this ayah, it's describing the reward (أَجْرًا) that the believers will get.
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing the reward (أَجْرًا) that is being received. The ًا ending matches the same ending on أَجْرًا because they go together.
This is a special word used to emphasize what comes after it. It's like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English.

steps.m: and indeed (introduces a new statement after ٱلَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This word introduces what happens to those who don't believe.
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال at beginning to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because ذ is a sun letter that combines with ل. This word is the subject of لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (they do not believe).
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) into 'they do not believe'

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, or believe
to make someone feel safe/secure, to cause to believe
steps.n: Adding alif at start changes meaning to 'cause someone to do the action'
Changed from past tense (he believed) to present tense (he believes) by adding يُ at the start and changing the middle vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This word is what لَا (not) is negating in this ayah.
Adding vowels changes it to mean 'being in a state of lateness'
Adding ة makes it a feminine noun meaning 'the last/end part'
The kasra at the end shows this word is the object of a preposition
Adding بِ makes it mean 'with' or 'by' the thing

steps.m: to prepare, make ready (especially something unpleasant)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to cause/make someone do the action'

steps.m: we have prepared (for them عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا - a painful punishment)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to emphasize 'we' and show completion. This word connects to عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (a painful punishment) which is what was prepared.
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose
Added هُمْ to show who the action is for. This word connects to أَعْتَدْنَا (We have prepared) to show what was prepared for them.

steps.m: punishment (object of أَعْتَدْنَا - we have prepared)
steps.n: Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning 'punishment'. This word is what is being prepared in the phrase 'we have prepared for them'.

steps.m: a punishment (object of أَعْتَدْنَا - we have prepared)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is any punishment, not a specific one. The ًا ending also shows this word is receiving the action of the verb 'we have prepared'.
Added ي between second and third root letters to show intensity of the pain. This pattern is often used for strong qualities.
Added ًا at the end to show this describes the punishment (عَذَابًا) mentioned in the ayah. Both words have the same ending because they're connected.

steps.m: to call upon, pray to, invite

steps.n: The ي at the start shows this is happening now (present tense)
Added و meaning 'and'. The final و is dropped because it comes before another word. This verb connects to بِٱلشَّرِّ (upon evil) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be close and friendly with others

steps.n: Adds إِنْ and ان to the root to make a noun meaning 'human' - humans are social beings who are friendly with others
ٱلْ at the start makes it specific
The ُ shows this is the doer of the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah
Connected the letters and doubled the ر to show strong or intense evil
Added ال to point to a specific evil (the one being called for in this verse)

steps.m: with/for the intense evil (object of يَدْعُ)
steps.n: Added بِ to show how the person is calling/praying. This word is what's being called for in يَدْعُ (he calls/prays)

steps.m: with/for the intense evil (object of يَدْعُ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ
Changes from verb 'to call' into noun meaning 'a call/prayer'
Changed to show this is what is being called/prayed for (object of the verb)
Added هُ with a small و (ۥ) after it to show possession - meaning this is his prayer/calling
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun referring to something good
Added ال to make it specific
Added بِ at the start meaning 'with/by' and ِ at the end because it follows the preposition بِ. This word is what the person is calling for in their supplication.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'
Root meaning 'being social' becomes a noun meaning all humans/mankind
Changes from general mankind to a single human being
Adds the nominative case ending to show this is the subject doing the action in يَدْعُ (calls upon)
Makes it definite by adding the ال prefix, referring to humankind in general
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who does this action as a habit or characteristic
Added tanween alif to show this word is describing mankind (ٱلْإِنسٰنُ) in the verse

steps.m: to make something into something else, to place something somewhere

steps.n: Added fatha (َ) after each letter to make the basic past tense verb. This shows one person did this action in the past.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'. The ْ (sukun) on ل shows the word pauses there before the نَا.

steps.m: and we made (the night and day into signs)
steps.n: Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this to the previous statement. This word starts a new part explaining what Allah made the night and day into.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added ال at the beginning which causes the first letter to get a shadda
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلْنَا (we made)
Root letters connected with a-vowels (fatha) to make a noun meaning 'river/stream'
Added alif to create noun for the time when brightness flows
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of 'We made' (جَعَلْنَا)

steps.m: and the daytime (as object of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect to previous word (night) and changed alif to wasl form

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be a sign

steps.n: Adding ة turns this into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Changed ة to ت to prepare for adding the dual ending
Added يْ to show this means exactly two of something

steps.m: two signs (in grammar case showing possession/after a preposition)
steps.n: The ِ at the end shows this word follows special words like مِن (from) or في (in)
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of something before it
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
The root letters combine with special spelling because of the weak letters. The alif (ا) comes after the hamza (ء) to show length.
Added ة to make it a specific single sign. This word is what Allah made (the object of جَعَلْنَا meaning 'We made').
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a basic noun
Added ال with shadda on the first letter to make it definite
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb جَعَلْنَا (we made)

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word connects to two signs (ءَايَتَيْنِ) that are mentioned later.

steps.m: and we made (something into something else)
steps.n:
1. Added وَ (and) at beginning to connect this action with what came before
2. Changed the final alif to a long alif (ا → آ) to show we're pausing here when reading
The root letters combine with vowels to form the base noun. The hamza (ء) replaces the first root letter (أ), and the weak letter (ي) changes to alif (ا).
Adds feminine marker to create the common singular noun meaning 'sign'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object. This word is the direct object (what is being erased) of the verb محونا (we erased).
This form turns the flowing of bright light into the noun 'daytime'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific daytime rather than any daytime

steps.m: of the daytime (connected to ءَايَةَ - sign)
steps.n: Shows this word is connected to 'sign' (ءَايَةَ) in the phrase 'sign of the daytime'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form showing something that does the action of giving light
Added feminine marker to match with آية (sign) which is feminine

steps.m: giving light (feminine, as a description of آية/sign)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is describing the word آية (sign) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at start to mean 'you', and extra letters in middle to show seeking something for yourself
Changed ending to show multiple people doing the action
Adds 'so that/in order to' meaning to the verb
Has 'li' at start to mean 'so that' or 'in order to'

steps.m: to give extra or do more than required

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning the extra thing given. This word is what's being sought in the phrase 'that you may seek'.
Added ًا to show this is any favor (not a specific one) that's being sought from your Lord (رَّبِّكُمْ).
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or its source. Here it connects to رَّبِّكُمْ (your Lord) to show the favor comes from Him.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending in ن or ل, making the م sound stronger. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger and turns it into a noun meaning 'one who does this a lot'
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call or plea, like saying 'O my Lord!'
Adding كُمْ (kum) changes 'my Lord' to 'your Lord' when speaking to many people
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at start and changed vowels to show present tense for 'you'
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: so that you all may know
steps.n: Added لِ to show this is the purpose or reason for something

steps.m: and so that you may know
steps.n: Added conjunction وَ ('and') at the beginning to connect with previous statement
Added fathas to create a noun meaning 'number'. This word is what is being known in the phrase 'so that you may know the number'.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'year' by adding vowels and ة at the end
Changed to plural form by changing vowels to kasra (ِ) and adding ين at the end
Makes the noun definite by adding the ال prefix
Adds a fatha mark to show this word is the object of something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the first vowel to kasra (ِ) and added alif after second letter to make it mean 'the act of counting'
Adding ال makes the noun definite, changing it from 'a calculation' to 'the calculation'

steps.m: and the calculation (object of لِتَعْلَمُوا - so that you may know)
steps.n: Added 'and' (وَ) at the start. The word gets a fatha (َ) at the end because it's something being learned/known.
The doubled ل with damma makes this a word meaning 'everything without exception'. This word is what we explained in detail, as shown by the next word فَصَّلْنٰهُ (we explained it in detail).
Adding the conjunction و connects this to the previous phrase
The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of the verb فَصَّلْنٰهُ (we explained it in detail)
Root letters ش-ي-أ got vowels (َ ْ) to make the noun 'thing'
Changed the hamza (أ) to (ء) because it comes after a sukun
Changed the ي to ى because it comes before hamza
Added tanwin kasra to show this word belongs to كُلَّ (every/all) that comes before it
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb

steps.m: to explain in detail, to break down thoroughly
steps.n: The doubled letter (ّ) makes the meaning more intense - not just to separate, but to explain thoroughly
The نٰ at the end means 'we' did the action. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.

steps.m: we explained it thoroughly (object of تَفْصِيلًا)
steps.n: Adding هُ means 'it' - refers to what was explained. This verb is connected to the word تَفْصِيلًا (explanation) that comes after it in the ayah.
Added shadda to show intensive meaning of explaining thoroughly
This form makes a noun that describes the action itself

steps.m: a detailed explanation (object of فَصَّلْنٰهُ - we have explained it)
steps.n: Adds -an ending to show this word receives the action (it is what was explained)
The doubled ل shows this means absolutely everything with no exceptions. This word is the subject of the verb أَلْزَمْنٰهُ (we have fastened) in this ayah.
The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb أَلْزَمْنٰهُ (we have fastened) in this ayah
The و at the start connects this to what came before
Creates the word for 'human' - humans are social beings who are close to others
Adds genitive case ending to show possession or after certain prepositions
The letter ن is dropped and replaced with a small alif (ٰ), and the word gets kasra tanwin to show it receives the action of أَلْزَمْنٰهُ (We have fastened)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Adding أ at the start makes the action causative. This verb takes طائر (deeds/fate) as its object in the accusative case, showing what is being made necessary for each person.
Adding نا suffix changes the subject to 'we', making it clear that Allah is speaking in the first person plural form

steps.m: we made it bind to him (طٰٓئِرَهُۥ - his record/fate)
steps.n: The هُ refers to إِنسٰنٍ (person) mentioned earlier, and طٰٓئِرَهُۥ (his record/fate) is what's being made to bind

steps.m: to fly or move through air

steps.n: In pre-Islamic Arabia, birds were used to tell fortunes, so this word came to mean fate or destiny. Added alif after first root letter to show who/what does the action.

steps.m: fate/destiny (object of أَلْزَمْنٰهُ - We have fastened)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is receiving the action of the verb أَلْزَمْنٰهُ (We have fastened)

steps.m: his fate/destiny (object of أَلْزَمْنٰهُ - We have fastened)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show this fate belongs to him. The whole phrase means 'We have fastened his fate (to him)'.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that something is in/attached to someone's neck.
Added damma vowels to make the basic noun form
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something and follows the preposition في (in)
Added هِۦ to show this neck belongs to him. This word is connected to طٰٓئِرَهُۥ (his deeds/fate) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes to 'we' form and adds meaning of causing/making something happen

steps.m: and we bring out (for لَهُ - for him)
steps.n: Adds 'wa' (and) at start + changes ending to match present tense 'we bring out'
Added fatha to show the basic preposition sound
Added هُ which means 'him' to show who the preposition is directed to

steps.m: for him/to him (connected to طٰٓئِرَهُۥ - his fate/deeds)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) which helps with pronunciation when connecting words. This word is connected to طٰٓئِرَهُۥ (his fate/deeds) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و becomes silent with sukun.
Adding fatha at the end shows this word tells us when something happens. Here it tells when نُخْرِجُ (We will bring forth) happens - on the day of resurrection.
Changed و to ي and added ا to show it's about the action itself, not just doing it
Added fatha to prepare for next change
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific instance of rising - the resurrection
Added ٱلْ to show we mean THE resurrection (the Day of Judgment), and ِ because it comes after فِى (in) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the vowels and added alif to make it into a noun for the thing that is written

steps.m: a book (that will be brought forth)
steps.n: Added tanween (double fatha) at the end to show this is the object being brought forth in the verse (نُخْرِجُ means 'We will bring forth')
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start. The middle vowel changed from ـِ to ـْ. The final ي became ى when the verb became present tense.
Added alif maksura marker (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound

steps.m: he meets/finds it (referring to كِتٰبًا - the book)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what is being met/found. This refers to the book (كِتٰبًا) mentioned earlier that each person will receive on the Day of Judgment.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: spread out (something that was acted upon)
steps.n: Changed to show something that had the action done to it
Added ًا to show this describes the book (كِتٰبًا) that will be brought forth on the Day of Judgment
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by adding ٱ at the start and changing the vowels. This is telling one person to read their book, as shown in the next word كِتٰبَكَ (your book).
Adding the vowels changes this from an action to a thing. The kasra-fatha pattern makes it a noun.
Adding alif makes it specifically mean a book. This is what is being commanded to be read in اقْرَأْ (read!).
Adding fatha at the end shows this is the object being acted upon by the verb 'read'

steps.m: your book (that you are commanded to read)
steps.n: Adding ك at the end makes it 'your book'. The alif is written as ٰ when followed by a suffix.
Connected the root letters with fathas to show this happened in the past. This word relates to كِتٰبَكَ (your book) in this ayah, showing the book is sufficient.
Adding the ى with small alif (ٰ) above it at the end because the word ends with a weak letter ي
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'self'
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'by means of' or 'with' something
Added كَ at the end to show this means 'your'. The ِ before ك shows this word follows the preposition ب. This word is being acted upon by كَفَىٰ (is sufficient)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و gets a sukun (ْ) because it's a weak letter.
Added a small line (fatha) above the last letter to show this word receives the action of 'sufficed'
Added ال to make it specific, referring to a particular day (the Day of Judgment) mentioned in this context
This is a basic preposition showing something is above or over something else
The ى changes to ي before adding attachments at the end
Adding كَ at the end shows this applies to 'you'. This word is connected to كَفَىٰ (sufficient) in the ayah, showing what is sufficient.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to show someone who does this action as their role or characteristic
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of being sufficient in the phrase 'sufficient as a reckoner'
This is a standalone relative pronoun used to refer to people. It introduces a conditional clause in this ayah, connecting to ٱهْتَدَىٰ (is guided).
Adding a shadda makes the 'm' sound stronger, adding emphasis to the word.
The kasra at the end connects this word grammatically to the verb that follows it (ٱهْتَدَىٰ - is guided).

steps.m: to guide or show the right path

steps.n: The root letters were connected with vowels (َ) and the last letter changed to ى to show 'he guided' in the past tense
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing the action to/for oneself
Added alif maqsurah (ى) at end to complete the past tense form
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added مَا at the end to restrict the meaning to 'only' or 'just'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing the action to oneself
Added ي at start to make it present tense 'he'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun form meaning 'soul/self'
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it's connected to another word, like 'of the soul'
Adding لِ (li) at the start changes it to mean 'for/to the soul'
Adding هِۦ (hi) at the end makes it mean 'his soul'
This is a question word that became a conditional particle meaning 'whoever'. It introduces the person or thing we're talking about.
Added و at the beginning to connect this part to the previous statement. This word is the subject of ضَلَّ (went astray) in this ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels to show who did the action and when it happened
When two identical letters come at the end of a word, they combine with a shadda (ّ). This doesn't change the meaning, just makes it easier to say.
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added مَا at the end which restricts the meaning to 'only' or 'just'

steps.m: to lose the right path/to go wrong

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he completely lost the right path
steps.n: The middle letter is doubled with shadda to show the action is complete or intense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ and end vowel to ُ. This word comes after فَإِنَّمَا (then indeed) in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows something is physically or metaphorically above something else
When attaching pronouns, على changes to عَلَيْ
The ها suffix shows this word is feminine and refers back to نَفْس (soul) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation of the negation particle
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word negates the verb تَزِرُ (bears) that comes after it.

steps.m: to carry or bear a heavy burden

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ and changing vowels

steps.m: to carry a heavy load or burden

steps.n: Changed the word to show who does the action by adding alif after first letter
Added ة with tanween to make it feminine and indefinite
Changed the root into a noun by adding specific vowel patterns
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being carried. This word is what's being carried in the phrase 'no bearer of burdens shall bear...'
Added damma and sukun to create the base form for comparison
Added ىٰ at the end to make it feminine and show it's describing something else in the sentence
Basic negative particle ما that negates the verb كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form
Changed from past tense 'was' to a simple command 'be!' by dropping the middle letter and changing vowels
Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' did this action in the past
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added shadda to make the punishment more intense
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'one who' and changed vowels to show it's the doer of the punishing
Added ين at the end to show multiple punishers. This word is the subject complement of كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah, showing what 'we were not'.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Added shadda to emphasize the middle letter, making the meaning stronger
Added alif maqsura (ىٰ) at the end to complete the particle. This word connects to نَبْعَثَ (we send) in the ayah, showing the action continues until we send something.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ن at beginning to show 'we' as the doers, and the word comes after حَتَّى (until) which requires this specific ending
Changed the word pattern to show someone who does the action. The و after second letter shows this is their role or job.

steps.m: a messenger (object of نَبْعَثَ - we send)
steps.n: Added tanween fathatain (ًا) to show this is the object being sent in the phrase 'until we send a messenger'

steps.m: when (introduces the time condition for أَرَدْنَآ)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition. It connects to the next word أَرَدْنَآ (we intend) to show when something happens.

steps.m: and when (introduces the time condition for أَرَدْنَآ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it

steps.m: and when (introduces the time condition for أَرَدْنَآ)
steps.n: The final alif is lengthened to help with pronunciation at the end of the word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Adding أَ at start changes meaning to 'cause to want'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and put sukun on د because it's followed by two letters
This is a connecting word that introduces what Allah wants to do. It connects أَرَدْنَآ (We intended) with نُّهْلِكَ (we destroy), showing that the destruction is what was intended.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added نُ at start to mean 'we', and changed vowels to make it present tense 'destroy' instead of past tense 'destroyed'
Added shadda on ن because it comes after أَن in the ayah
Root letters connected with a fat-ha (a) and sukoon (stop mark) to make a noun meaning 'settlement'
Added ة to make it a specific place. This word is what is being destroyed in the phrase 'destroy a town'
Added tanween (ً) to show this is the direct object being acted upon in the phrase 'destroy a town'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding sukun (the little circle) on the last letter to show it's a complete past tense verb
Adding نا changes it from 'he commanded' to 'we commanded'

steps.m: to live in ease and luxury

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added مُ at the start to show who does the action, and changed vowels

steps.m: of the one who lives in luxury
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is being described by another word

steps.m: its wealthy ones (object of أَمَرْنَا - we commanded)
steps.n: Added ها to show it belongs to the town (قَرْيَةً). This word is what receives the action of the verb أَمَرْنَا (we commanded).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The وا shows multiple people did the action

steps.m: so they broke rules (result of أَمَرْنَا/we commanded)
steps.n: The فَ shows this happened as a result of the previous action (we commanded)
This shows location or being inside something

steps.m: in it (referring to قَرْيَةً - the town)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the town (قَرْيَةً) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The ها is feminine because قَرْيَةً (town) is feminine.
This root carries the basic meaning of something being true, right, or becoming necessary
The shadda (double letter) makes the meaning stronger and more definite. In this ayah, it shows something becoming absolutely necessary as a result of previous actions.

steps.m: so it became necessary (as a consequence of فَفَسَقُوا۟)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is a direct result of the previous action (their disobedience فَفَسَقُوا۟). The word connects to عَلَيْهَا (upon them) which follows it.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or affecting something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change

steps.m: upon it/her (referring to قَرْيَةً - the town)
steps.n: The ها refers back to the town (قَرْيَةً) mentioned earlier in the verse. Since 'town' is feminine in Arabic, we use the feminine 'it/her'.
Root meaning 'speak' becomes a noun meaning 'speech' by adding the vowels a and silent w
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action in the sentence. This word is what 'became due' in the verse.

steps.m: to destroy utterly, to demolish completely

steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) on middle letter to show complete destruction. The double letter makes the meaning stronger.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: we utterly destroyed it/her (referring to قَرْيَةً - a town)
steps.n: Added هَا to show what was destroyed (referring back to the town mentioned earlier)

steps.m: so we utterly destroyed it/her (as a consequence of فَفَسَقُوا۟)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this destruction happened as a result of their wrongdoing (فَفَسَقُوا۟)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that names the action itself
Added tanween to show this describes how thoroughly the destruction was done. This word explains how 'we destroyed it' (دَمَّرْنٰهَا) was carried out.
This is a question word used to ask about quantity

steps.n: The و connects this to what came before, making it mean 'and how many'. In this ayah, it's asking a rhetorical question about how many generations Allah destroyed, connecting to the verb أَهْلَكْنَا (We destroyed) that follows it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at beginning makes someone cause the action instead of just doing it. Also added sukun on middle letter.
Adding نَا at the end shows 'we' did the action
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from' in English
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلْقُرُونِ (the generations). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better.

steps.m: a generation (group of people living at the same time)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the basic singular noun form
Changed vowels and added ون to make regular plural form
Added ٱلْ to make it specific
Added kasra to show this word comes after مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to 'the generations' (ٱلْقُرُونِ) to show which generations were destroyed.
Added a small mark (called a 'small noon') at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word

steps.m: to be far away from something

steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks (fatha on first letter, sukun on middle letter). The idea of being 'far from' something developed into meaning 'after' in time - like how something after is far from the beginning.
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to نُوحٍ (Noah) that comes after it, showing it means 'after Noah'
This is a proper name, not derived from a root
The tanwin kasra (ٍ) shows this word belongs to or is connected to something before it. Here, it's connected to the word بَعْدِ (after), showing we're talking about 'after Noah'
Added fathas to make it past tense. The final ى is because this is a weak verb ending in ي
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) as the standard spelling for weak verbs ending in ي

steps.m: and he was sufficient (connected to أَهْلَكْنَا - we destroyed)

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda combines the repeated letters and makes this a noun for someone who does this action intensively
Added كَ at the end to show possession (your) and ِ before it because it follows the preposition بِ
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'by/with'. This word connects to كَفَىٰ (sufficient is) in the ayah.

steps.m: to commit a sin or fault

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning one sin
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple sins
Added kasra to show this word is connected to 'His servants' (عِبَادِهِ) in the ayah
Added بِ at beginning to show these sins are being used as a means or reason
Changed from root meaning 'to worship' into noun meaning 'one who worships' by adding vowels
Changes single servant into plural form meaning multiple servants
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by what comes after it
Added هِۦ (hi) at the end to show these are Allah's servants/worshippers. The word connects to 'sins' later in the verse.

steps.m: to inform or be aware of something deeply

Changed to فَعِيل pattern to show someone who deeply knows/is aware of things
Added an alif and tanween (double dots) at the end to show this word is being used as a description - here it's describing Allah as being All-Aware
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: All-Seeing (as a descriptive complement of كَفَىٰ)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is completing the description in the phrase كَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ (Your Lord is sufficient)
This is a basic word used to refer to people in questions or statements, like saying 'who' or 'whoever' in English.

The shadda appears because this word combines with the next letter in the ayah, making the sound stronger. It's still the same word meaning 'whoever', just written slightly differently because of how it connects.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb

Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowels to match present form, and added ُ at end to show it's doing the action. This word is connected to عَجَّلْنَا (We hastened) in the ayah.

steps.m: to rush or seek something quickly

steps.n: Basic root letters got vowels to make 'he rushed' in past tense
Added ا after ع to make it mean 'one who rushes'
Added feminine ending to create a noun meaning 'the worldly/immediate life'
Added the definite article to specify this particular worldly life

steps.m: the worldly life (as object of يُرِيدُ - wants)
steps.n: Added fatHa to show this is the object of the verb يُرِيدُ (wants) in the ayah
Added sukun at the end to prepare for adding 'we'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added ه with damma to mean 'him'

Combination of preposition في (in) and pronoun ها (it/her)

steps.m: to want or wish for something

Combination of preposition ل (for) and relative pronoun من (who)

The middle letter و changes to ي because it's between two vowels
Added يُ at the beginning to show 'he' is doing the action, and ُ at the end to show it's happening now

A connecting word that shows one thing happens after another with a time gap in between. In this ayah, it connects 'We hastened' (عَجَّلْنَا) with 'We have made' (جَعَلْنَا) to show these actions happened one after the other.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb meaning 'he made/set'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and put a sukun (ْ) on the last root letter
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

Added the prolongation mark ۥ to show the extended pronunciation. This word is receiving the action of عَجَّلْنَا (we hastened) in this ayah.

steps.m: Hell (as the object being entered in يَصْلَىٰهَا - he will enter it)

steps.m: he will enter it (referring to the fire)
steps.n: Added ا to lengthen the word and ها to show 'it' (feminine object)

steps.m: to blame or find fault with
steps.n: The root relates to criticism or blame

Changes the word to show what receives the blame rather than who does the blaming

steps.m: blamed/criticized (as a description of how someone ended up)
steps.n: Adds ًا to show this word is describing the state someone ended up in
The root means to push away or drive out forcefully

Changed from 'he drove away' to 'one who is driven away' - like going from pushing to being pushed
Adding double letter (shadda) makes the meaning stronger - like saying 'really driven away'
Added tanween to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence

The و connects this condition to what came before it, showing this is an additional case being discussed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'to want/intend something'

steps.m: to strive or work hard towards a goal

steps.n: Added fathas to show past tense. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end. This word connects to سَعْيَهَا (his striving) later in the verse.
Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what came before (wanting the Hereafter)
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Added fatha to show the basic preposition sound

steps.m: for it/her (referring to الْءَاخِرَةَ - the Hereafter)
steps.n: Added the feminine pronoun ها to show what the preposition is referring to. Here it refers back to 'the Hereafter' (الْءَاخِرَةَ) which is feminine in Arabic.

steps.m: to work hard towards a goal

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of striving'. This word shows what kind of effort was made.

steps.m: a believer (subject of وَهُوَ - and he is)
