steps.m: those (who did what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to people or things that are far away. Here it refers back to the believers mentioned who wanted the Hereafter and strived for it.

steps.m: so/then those (who wanted and strived for the Hereafter)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is a result of what came before - because they wanted the Hereafter and strived for it while being believers, therefore (as a result) they are the ones whose efforts will be appreciated.

steps.m: to be or exist in a state/condition

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he was' in the past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.

steps.m: to work hard towards a goal
effort/striving (the thing being done in سَعَىٰ)
steps.n: This changes the action into a noun showing the actual effort or work being done. It is what's being referred to by the verb سَعَىٰ (strove/made effort) in this ayah.
Added ُ to show this is the main subject being talked about
Added هُم which means 'their' and shows this belongs to a group of people
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he thanked'
Changes from 'he thanked' to 'being thanked' - like changing from doing the action to receiving it
The shadda makes the meaning stronger
The ًا at the end shows this describes how something turned out
The doubled ل (shadda) shows completeness, while the damma (ُ) makes it into a noun. This word is what receives the extending/providing mentioned in نُّمِدُّ (we extend/provide).

steps.m: all/each one (as the object receiving Allah's provision)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of extending/providing (نُّمِدُّ). It refers to both groups mentioned after it (هٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَهٰٓؤُلَآءِ - these and those) who receive from Allah's provision.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this is happening to something rather than someone doing it
Added shadda to show this is happening with intensity
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing this
The ُ at the end shows this is a normal statement, not a command or wish
Starting with the basic pointing word ها which shows something near
Added ؤُ to strengthen the pointing meaning and make it specifically for plural
Added لَآءِ to complete the plural demonstrative form. This word points to multiple things that are nearby.
This is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'these'. It's used to point to multiple things that are near.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: from (connects عَطَآءِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects 'the giving/gift' (عَطَآءِ) to what was mentioned before.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed the final و to اء to make it a noun meaning the act or result of giving
Added kasra at the end to show this gift belongs to someone (your Lord in this ayah). The alif is written as آ which is just a spelling variation.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (shadda) combines with the noun pattern to create the meaning of 'master/lord' - someone who provides complete nurturing and care
The kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else (in this case, it's connected to عَطَآءِ meaning 'gift/bounty')
Adding كَ shows this belongs to 'you'. The whole phrase عَطَآءِ رَبِّكَ means 'the gift/bounty of your Lord'
This is a negative particle that negates the verb كَانَ (was) that comes after it in the ayah, changing its meaning to 'was not/never was'
The و at the start connects this phrase to the previous statement, while مَا negates the verb كَانَ that follows
Added basic past tense vowels to show this happened in the past
The و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two fathas. This word tells us about Allah's giving in the past.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the final و to اء to make it a noun meaning the result of giving
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing the giving. The long alif (آ) makes the 'aa' sound stronger.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and guide to completion

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures in a complete and perfect way

steps.m: master who nurtures completely (possessed by عَطَآءِ)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word belongs to or comes after عَطَآءِ (bounty/gift)

steps.m: your master who nurtures completely (possessed by عَطَآءِ)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows this master belongs to you. The whole phrase عَطَآءِ رَبِّكَ means 'the bounty of your master'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he prevented) to a passive participle (something prevented) by adding مَ at the start and changing the vowels
Added ًا to show this word is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's describing what Allah's gift was not (it was not forbidden/restricted)

steps.m: to look, observe, or think about something carefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by adding u- at start and changing ending - makes it a command to one person
Changed initial hamza-damma to alif to help pronunciation
This is a question word used to ask about how something happens or what state something is in
Adding vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce this question word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled ض shows doing something with extra effort or making something happen
Adding نا changes it from 'he' to 'we'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun. This word is what we 'preferred' (فَضَّلْنَا) in this ayah.
Added هُمْ at the end to show we're talking about 'them'. This whole phrase describes who Allah preferred over others.
This is one of the most common Arabic prepositions. It shows one thing being physically or metaphorically above another.
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce the word - 'a' sound after ع and after ل
Added small alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's pronounced with a long 'aa' sound at the end
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the basic noun form
The word gets two dots under it (ٍ) to show it's receiving the action of favoring/preferring
Changes to an adjective meaning 'being in a state of lateness'
Makes the word feminine by adding ة, changing it to mean 'the last/end part'
Adds 'wa' (and) at the beginning to connect it to what came before
Adds emphasis particle لَ to strengthen the meaning

steps.m: and surely the hereafter (subject of أَكْبَرُ)
steps.n: Adds definite article ال and nominative case ending ُ to show this is the subject of أَكْبَرُ (greater)
Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make it mean 'it became big/great' in the past
Added أ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'more/greater than'
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: he went up step by step
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Forms a concrete noun meaning 'a single step/level' from the root meaning of stepping
Changes from one step/level to many steps/levels by adding ات at the end
The alif is written as a small alif (ٰ) above the letter when followed by certain endings
Added tanwin kasra to show this word is being compared to something else in the sentence
Connected the root letters with vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'became big' to 'bigger/greater' by adding أ at start - this makes it compare two things
Adds a 'u' sound at the end to show this word is doing the action (subject)

steps.m: and greater/larger (than ranks in this world)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this to previous phrase. This word is comparing the ranks of the Hereafter (درجات) with those mentioned before.
Added shadda (double letter) to show someone actively choosing to prefer something over another
Changed from verb to noun showing the act itself
Added tanween alif to make it an adverb (tells us 'how' - with excellence/greatly) describing the action of preferring
This is a negative particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's telling us not to do the action in تَجْعَلْ (make/set up)
Adding the shadda (ّ) and fatha (َ) makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'absolutely not' instead of just 'not'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at the start changes 'he made' to 'you make'
This is a preposition that connects two parts of the sentence, showing that something is being placed alongside Allah
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic word for 'deity'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
This forms the noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Root meaning 'worship' changed to mean 'one who is worshipped' by adding vowels
The alif after second letter makes the 'a' sound longer
Added tanwin alif to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb تَجْعَلْ (make/take)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the first letter to hamza with fatha and added a long alif to make it mean 'another'. This word is describing إِلٰهًا (god) in the phrase 'don't make with Allah another god'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense form

steps.m: you sit/would sit (result of لَّا تَجْعَلْ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he sat' to 'you sit' - shows what would happen as a result of doing something (making another god). Like saying 'if you do X, then Y will happen'

steps.m: then you would sit/end up (as a result of لَّا تَجْعَلْ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the start to show this happens as a result of something else
This forms a passive participle meaning 'blamed/criticized'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed the verb 'blamed' into a passive participle (something that is blamed) by adding مَ at start and و after second letter
Added ًا at the end to show this describes what receives the action in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive participle form showing something that was abandoned
Added emphasis with shadda on م to show complete abandonment
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Added small alif (ٰ) after ى to show proper pronunciation
Added وَ (and) to connect this decree with previous statements
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares for others in a complete way

steps.m: one who nurtures completely (subject of قَضَىٰ/decreed)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing the action of decreeing (قَضَىٰ) in this verse
Adding 2nd person masculine singular possessive pronoun suffix. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb قَضَىٰ (decreed).
These are two separate words that often come together: أَنْ (that) and لا (not)
When أَنْ and لا come together, the ن combines with ل to make a stronger ل sound (shown by ّ). This word connects to تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ (you worship) to mean 'that you do not worship'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added وا to make it plural 'you all' and changed the word to show it's a command - meaning 'worship!' instead of just 'worship'
Starting with the basic negation particle لا
Adding إِ at the beginning and doubling the ل changes this from a simple negation to an exception particle. This connects to the verb تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ (worship) to mean 'worship none except...'
The alif at the end is written as a long alif (آ) in the Quran. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
This is a special emphatic pronoun base that requires an attached pronoun to be complete. It's used to give extra emphasis to pronouns.
Added هُ (him) to complete the emphatic pronoun. This word is the object of تَعْبُدُوا (you worship) in this ayah, emphasizing that Allah alone should be worshipped.

steps.m: to give birth, to produce offspring

steps.n: The root means 'to give birth' and adding alif makes it mean 'one who gives birth' (like mother/father). Think of it like changing 'teach' to 'teacher'
Adding َيْن shows we're talking about exactly two parents and puts the word in genitive case (used after prepositions)
Adding بِ shows we're talking about doing something with or by the parents
Adding الْ makes the word specific - we're talking about particular parents
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic word
Changed the word to show the action of doing good rather than just the quality of being good
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of the verb قَضَىٰ (decreed). In this ayah, Allah is decreeing kindness to parents.
Starting with the conditional particle إِن which shows something depends on something else
Added ما which makes the condition more general, like changing 'if' to 'if ever' or 'whenever'
The ن combines with م to make a stronger م sound (shown by the ّ mark). This word introduces options in the following phrase about reaching old age.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this happens in present/future. The ْ (sukun) and ُ (damma) are part of this pattern.

steps.m: he will surely reach (referring to parents reaching old age)
steps.n: Adding نَّ with shadda adds strong emphasis. In this ayah, it refers to parents definitely reaching old age while with their children.
Changes from root meaning to a noun showing location/position

steps.m: with you (referring to the parents mentioned in إِمَّا يَبْلُغَنَّ)
steps.n: Added كَ to show this is happening 'with you'. In this ayah, it's talking about when parents reach old age while with you (in your care).

steps.m: to grow, become big or old

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to grow old' to noun meaning 'old age'

steps.m: the old age (that the parents reach)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and a fatha at the end because this word is the object of يَبْلُغَنَّ (reach)
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing something
Added هُ to show possession or reference to someone
Added مَآ to show it refers to two people (dual form)
This is a connecting word that shows options. In this ayah, it connects 'one of them' with 'both of them' when talking about elderly parents.
Changes the word into a noun meaning 'both'
Adds alif to make it refer to two things
Basic negation particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's telling us not to say something disrespectful to parents.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this command follows from the previous statement about parents deserving good treatment.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form
Changed from past tense 'he said' to command 'say!' by changing the vowels

steps.m: you say (addressing someone in this ayah)
steps.n: Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' and dropped the و because it comes before a command marker (لا)
Added fatha to show basic vowel sound
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added هُمَا to show this refers to both parents (mentioned earlier as الوالدين)
Changed the final alif to alif madd for pronunciation
The damma (ُ) and doubled letter (ّ) make this into a strong expression of feeling, like saying 'ugh!' when you're really annoyed

steps.m: ugh! (showing strong dislike to parents, which is forbidden)
steps.n: This word appears in a verse teaching us not to say even 'ugh!' to our parents out of respect. The tanween (ٍ) makes it general, meaning any kind of disrespectful sound or word
This is a negative particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's connected to تَقُل (say), telling us 'do not say'
Added و at the beginning to connect this command with what came before it
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
ت at start shows 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: you scold harshly both of them (referring to parents)
steps.n: Added the dual pronoun هُمَا to show this refers to both parents mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added vowels to make it 'he said' in the past tense
Added و (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous command فَلَا تَقُل (do not say)

steps.m: and say! (command to say قَوْلًا كَرِيمًا - kind words)
steps.n: Changed from past tense 'he said' to a command 'say!' by dropping the middle letter and adding a dot on top. This tells you to speak kindly to parents, connecting to قَوْلًا كَرِيمًا (kind words) that follows.
This is a preposition particle that shows who the action is directed towards. Here it connects to the verb تَقُل (say) to show who is being spoken to.
Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: surely for both of them (connects to تَقُل)
steps.n: The هُمَا at the end shows this refers to exactly two people (in this case, both parents)
Connected the root letters together to form the basic word structure
Added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun meaning the thing itself

steps.m: a saying/speech (as the object of قُل)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being said (the object). In this ayah, Allah commands us to speak to our parents with this kind/noble speech.
Added ي between the second and third letters with vowels to show this describes someone or something with this quality
Adding ًا at the end shows this describes how to do something - in this case, it describes how to speak to parents
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to an imperative (command) form by adding alif and changing vowels

steps.m: and lower! (as a command to lower wings of humility to parents)
steps.n: Added 'and' at beginning. This word is connected to لَهُمَا جَنَاحَ (to them the wing) in the ayah, forming the metaphor of lowering one's wings of humility to parents.
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its basic meaning

steps.m: for both of them (parents mentioned in وَٱخْفِضْ)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show this refers to both parents. This word connects to وَٱخْفِضْ (and lower) to say 'lower [something] for both of them'

steps.m: to bend down or lean towards something

steps.n: Root letters connected with short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make a verb meaning 'to bend'
Added alif to make it into a noun meaning 'wing'

Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb 'lower' (اخْفِضْ). In the ayah, it's used metaphorically to mean 'lower your wing of humility to them' - a beautiful image of a bird lowering its wing gently.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun (quality) by shortening the word and adding emphasis mark (shadda). Shows the gentle/humble way to treat parents.
Added ال to make it specific gentleness
Added kasra at end because this word follows مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its source
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلرَّحْمَةِ (mercy). This is a helper vowel that makes the words flow together better when speaking.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy
Changed the verb into a noun meaning one instance or type of mercy
Added ال to make it definite. The ر gets doubled (shadda) because it combines with the ل. The ة gets kasra because it follows مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah.
Changed to a command form by adding ُ on first letter and dropping the weak middle letter و. This tells one person to do the action.
Added وَ at the start to connect this command with the previous command وَٱخْفِضْ (and lower/humble yourself)
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter ب to show the complete and perfect nature of the nurturing
Added kasra at the end to show this is being called upon or addressed
Added shadda on first letter to emphasize the call, showing strong emotion in calling out to the Lord
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he was merciful' to 'show mercy!' by making it a command - like telling someone 'be merciful!'
Added ٱ at start to help pronounce the command form

steps.m: show mercy on both of them! (command)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show the command applies to both parents
This is a basic comparison particle that shows something is similar to something else
Added fatha to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next part
Added ما which makes the comparison more specific. In this ayah, it connects how the parents raised the child (رَبَّيَانِى) to how the child should treat them now.
Changed from basic root to past tense verb by adding vowel marks and doubling middle letter
Added alif at end to show two people did the action
Added ي to show 'me' as the object of the verb
Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make the root into a past tense verb meaning 'became small'
Changes the verb into an adjective describing the quality of being small

steps.m: small (describing me when I was raised as a child)
steps.n: Adds the accusative case ending to show this describes the state of being raised. Related to رَبَّيَانِى (they both raised me) in the ayah.

steps.m: to care for, raise, nurture to completion

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship

steps.m: lord (as the subject of أَعْلَمُ - knows best)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing something - here, your Lord is the one who 'knows best'

steps.m: your Lord (as the subject of أَعْلَمُ - knows best)
steps.n: كُمْ means 'your' when talking to multiple people. The whole phrase means 'your Lord knows best what is in your souls'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: most knowing (of what is in your souls)
steps.n: Changed from 'he knew' to 'most knowing' by adding أَ at the start and changing the vowels to make it a superlative (meaning the highest degree). Like saying 'most' in English.
This is a preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with what (is in نُفُوسِكُمْ - your souls)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to ask about or refer to something. Here it refers to what is in their souls, mentioned right after this word in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it's used with نُفُوسِكُمْ (your souls) to mean 'in your souls/hearts'.
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This is the standard spelling when the word stands alone or at the end of a phrase.
Connected the root letters together to make a noun meaning one soul or self
Changes the singular 'soul' into plural 'souls' by adding damma marks and و
Added كُمْ at the end to show these souls belong to multiple people being spoken to (you all)
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects with تَكُونُوا۟ (you are) to mean 'if you are'
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and ُ after ك to show present tense
Added وا at the end to show we're talking to multiple people

steps.m: to make something good or right

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed from a verb (doing something) to describing someone who has this quality
Added ين to show this refers to many righteous people. This word follows تَكُونُوا (you all be) in the sentence, describing what the people are.
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds certainty to what comes after it

steps.m: so/then indeed (connects to previous statement about being righteous)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before about being righteous

steps.m: then indeed He (referring to Allah in this context)
steps.n: Adding هُۥ (hu) means 'He/him' - it attaches to the end to show we're talking about Allah
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he was' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter in the past tense. This is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle.

steps.m: one who returns often in repentance
steps.n: Added shadda (double mark) on middle letter and alif after it to show someone who does this action a lot

steps.m: those who return often in repentance
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people who do this

steps.m: for those who return often in repentance
steps.n: Added لِ at start to mean 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for the ones who return often in repentance (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the beginning and َ at the end because it's the object of كَانَ (was)

steps.m: to forgive or cover up faults completely

steps.n: Added vowels (َ) to make it past tense 'he forgave'
Changed the pattern to show someone who forgives as a constant quality
Added tanween alif to show this describes how Allah was (connected to كَانَ in the ayah)
Changed root letters and added vowels to make it 'he gave' in past tense
Changed the ending vowel to kasra to make it a command

steps.m: and give/bring! (command to give to ذَا ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this command with what came before. This command relates to giving to relatives (ذَا ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ) mentioned right after.
This is a special word that means 'possessor of' or 'one who has' when followed by another noun
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Changed from a basic root meaning 'to be close' into a noun meaning 'closeness' by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ
The ى at the end changes the abstract concept into a word for people related to that concept

steps.m: the relatives (object of ءَاتِ - give to)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to specific relatives. This word is receiving the action of giving (ءَاتِ) in this ayah.
Adding fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb

steps.m: his rightful share (what is being given)
steps.n: The root حَقق (truth/right) became حَقَّ (right/due) and هُ was added to mean 'his right/due'. Connected to the command 'give' (ءَاتِ)
Added ۥ after هُ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be still or settle in one place

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed the middle vowel and added ي to make it mean 'someone who is very much this way'

steps.m: poor person (one who is settled in need)
steps.n: Added مِ at the start to show someone who experiences something (like being poor), similar to how مريض means someone who is sick
Added ٱلْ to specify a particular poor person
Added fatha at end to show this is the object receiving the action of giving (وَءَاتِ)

steps.m: and the poor person (object of give)
steps.n: Added و at start to connect this with the previous phrase about relatives
The root meaning 'to build' becomes a noun meaning 'son' - like how parents build/create their children
Added و to connect this word with the previous word (المسكين - the needy) in the ayah

steps.m: and the son (object of ءَاتِ - give)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is receiving the action of 'give' in the ayah. Also changed alif to hamzat wasl since it's in the middle of speech.

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تُبَذِّرْ (squander) into 'do not squander'
Added وَ to mean 'and'
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger - showing excessive or wasteful scattering
ت at start shows this is about 'you' doing the action
The sukun at the end makes it a command or prohibition

steps.m: to scatter or spread things wastefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun (the concept of the action)

steps.m: wastefully (describes how not to give in وَلَا تُبَذِّرْ)
steps.n: Added -an ending to make it describe 'how' something is done (like saying 'wastefully' instead of just 'waste')
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the spendthrifts were (كَانُوٓا۟) brothers of devils. It's like saying 'Listen up, this is important!'
Added shadda to show intensive meaning of wasteful behavior
Changes from verb (to waste) to noun meaning 'a wasteful person'
The definite article ٱلْ (al) makes the noun specific. The hamza is written as ٱ because it's at the start.
The kasra ending shows this word is affected by إِنَّ (indeed/verily) which comes before it
The ينَ ending makes the word plural and shows it's the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). Added past tense vowels.

Adding ال makes it refer to a specific promise/covenant
Adding the fatha shows this is what is being fulfilled in the command 'fulfill the covenant'
Added vowels to make it past tense 'he was'
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

steps.m: to ask or be responsible for

steps.n: Added مَ at the start to show who receives the action, and added ُ after the second letter to show it's happening to someone. This word is used in this ayah to show that promises and agreements will be asked about.
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing what will be asked about. In this ayah, it refers to the covenant (العَهْد) that was mentioned before.
Added hamza and alif to make the basic past tense form. The weak letter ي changes to ى at the end.
Added وا to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: and fulfill! (you all) (command related to الْكَيْلَ - the measure)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement. This command is about fulfilling/giving full measure when measuring things.

steps.m: to measure (especially amounts of grain or liquid)
measurement (the act or result of measuring)
steps.n: This changes the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of measuring' or 'the amount measured'

steps.m: the measurement (object of أَوْفُوا - give in full)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific - 'the measurement' instead of just any measurement. This word is being acted on by the verb أَوْفُوا (give in full)
The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is receiving the action of the verb 'give in full'

steps.m: when (introducing the condition of weighing in وَزِنُوا۟)
steps.n: This is a conditional word that shows when something will happen. In this ayah, it connects the action of weighing (كِلْتُمْ) with what should be done (وَزِنُوا۟). It's like saying 'when you measure, then weigh...'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The weak letter ي is dropped, kasra added to ك, and تُ added at the end to show past tense for you/I

steps.m: you (all) measured (referring to merchants measuring goods)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to show that multiple people are being addressed. This matches the context of the ayah which is giving instructions to merchants about fair trading.

steps.m: to weigh or measure the weight of something

steps.n: The root و-ز-ن becomes a command زِنُوا by adding وا at the end to make it a command for a group of people
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this command to the previous one about giving full measure

steps.m: to weigh or measure with justice

steps.n: Changes from basic root into noun meaning tool for measuring
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it a specific scale rather than any scale
Adds بِ to show how the weighing should be done. This connects to the command وَزِنُوا۟ (weigh!) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The root word gets extra letters (م س ت) at the start and the middle letter changes to ي to make a word meaning 'straight path' or 'one who stays straight'

steps.m: the straight one (possessed by/connected to something)
steps.n: Added 'al' (the) at start and changed ending to show it belongs to/connects with another word
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for pointing to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a special mark (alif khanjariya) to show distance
Added ل with kasra to change from 'this' to 'that', showing more distance
Added ك at the end to show we're pointing to something specific that was just mentioned
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic comparative adjective pattern
Added double dammah to make the word indefinite and show it's the subject
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: better/more beautiful (than what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This form is used to compare things. Here it's comparing this interpretation/outcome with what was mentioned before.

steps.m: and better/more beautiful (than what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: to interpret or explain the meaning of something

steps.n: Adding the basic vowel marks to make it readable
Changed from a verb (to interpret) into a noun (interpretation) by adding تَ at the start and ي in the middle - like changing 'explain' to 'explanation'
Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon by أَحْسَنُ (better/best) in the verse
This is a negative particle that tells us something should not happen. In this ayah, it's telling us not to follow something.
The و at the start connects this prohibition with previous commands in the Quran
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding تَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you follow (as a command not to do)
steps.n: The و at the end drops to make it a command (don't follow), similar to how English changes 'you follow' to 'follow' for commands
This is a basic word that shows what thing is being referred to. In this ayah, it's the object of the verb تَقْفُ (follow/pursue), so it means 'do not follow what...'

steps.m: is not (negates لَكَ بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ - you have knowledge of it)
steps.n: This is a special negative verb that always means 'is not'. In this ayah, it negates the phrase 'you have knowledge of it', showing that you don't have knowledge of something.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: for you/to you (speaking to one male)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha at the end to show this is directed to one male person. This connects to the word عِلْمٌ (knowledge) in this ayah, showing you don't have knowledge of something.
Starting with the basic pronoun and adding kasra to show it's being acted upon
Adding the preposition ب at the beginning to show how something is done. In this ayah, it's talking about not following what you don't have knowledge of.
Adding the small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide to show the long vowel sound
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the result of knowing. This word is connected to بِهِ (with it) in the ayah, showing what you shouldn't follow without having.
Added two damma marks at the end to make it 'a knowledge' instead of 'the knowledge'
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to show how it's pronounced

Added shadda (ّ) to make the word stronger. This particle emphasizes what comes after it (in this case السَّمْعَ وَٱلْبَصَرَ وَٱلْفُؤَادَ - hearing, sight and heart) and makes those words take a special ending.
Changed from basic root letters to the noun meaning 'hearing' by adding the vowel marks fatHa and sukoon
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the hearing' instead of just 'hearing'
Changed the spelling of ال to ٱل which is a common way to write it in the Quran
Added َ at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Changed the root letters into a simple noun meaning 'eyesight' by adding short 'a' vowels (fathas)
Added fatha at the end to show this is one of the objects of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) along with hearing (ٱلسَّمْعَ) and heart (ٱلْفُؤَادَ)
The word 'sight' becomes 'the sight' by adding ال (al) at the start

steps.m: and the sight (object of إِنَّ along with hearing and heart)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous object (hearing/ٱلسَّمْعَ) in the list of things that will be questioned about
Forms the common noun meaning heart/mind from the root letters
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added 'and' to connect it to the previous words (hearing and sight) in the verse
Added fatha at the end to show this is one of the objects that إِنَّ is talking about, along with hearing and sight
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
The doubled ل makes the meaning stronger, showing it means absolutely everything with no exceptions
This word is the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah, showing what was asked about
This is the base form that means 'those'
Adding ا shows these people are far away
Adding ئِ shows we're talking about multiple people

steps.m: those (far away, plural, being talked about)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows these are the specific people being discussed in this ayah - the ones who follow what they don't have knowledge of
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
This is a preposition that shows what something is about or concerning. In this ayah, it connects to the pronoun that follows it.

steps.m: about it/concerning it (referring to something male)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (meaning 'it' for masculine things) to show what is being talked about

steps.m: to ask or question someone about something

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning and ُو in the middle to make it mean 'the one being asked/questioned' instead of just 'to ask'
Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon. This word is the predicate (خبر) of كَانَ in this ayah, describing what these things (hearing, sight, and heart) will be questioned about.
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'do not' in Arabic. In this ayah, it's used with تَمْشِ (walk) to say 'do not walk'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This و means 'and' and connects different instructions together.
Root letters م-ش-ي became a past tense verb 'he walked' by adding the vowels
Added ت at start to show 'you', changed vowels to show present tense

Basic preposition showing location or place. Here it connects 'walking' with 'the earth' to show where the walking happens.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowel marks to make it a concrete noun referring to the physical ground
Added 'the' at beginning and kasra at end because it follows the word فِي (in)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English.
Added كَ at the end to show who is being addressed. This word connects to لَن تَخْرِقَ (you will never pierce) in the ayah, showing who won't be able to pierce the earth.

Added the fatha mark (َ) above ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'lan'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.n: Changed to show 'you' doing the action in the present tense. This word shows what someone is trying to do to الْأَرْضَ (the earth) but won't succeed because of لَن (will never) before it.

steps.m: the earth (object being walked upon in تَمْشِ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific ('the earth') and fatḥa at the end shows it's receiving the action of walking (from تَمْشِ - walking)
This is a special word that shows something definitely won't happen in the future
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word connects to تَبْلُغَ (reach) to say something 'will never reach'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as the doer. Changed middle vowel to damma and added sukun on first letter. The word is negated by لَن which means it won't happen in the future.

Changed first vowel from fatha to kasra to make it plural, showing many mountains
Added ال for 'the' and َ at the end because it's the object of the verb تَبْلُغَ (reach/attain)

The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show the pointing extends further away
Adding لِ makes it point to something far away instead of nearby
Adding كَ completes the word and makes it point to something specific. In this ayah, it's describing كُلُّ (all of that)

The weak letter و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show intensity

The هُ shows possession - this evil belongs to something mentioned before
Changed into a location word showing nearness or presence. In this ayah, it shows the location 'near/with your Lord' where something is disliked.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does this action completely and perfectly
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Adding كَ shows this belongs to the person being spoken to. This whole word comes after عِندَ (with/at) in the ayah.

The ًا at the end shows this word is being acted upon. It's describing what was hateful in the statement 'All of that was hateful to your Lord'
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a special mark (alif khanjariya) that extends the pointing distance
Adding ل with kasra changes it to point to something far away
Adding ك with fatha makes it more specific and emphatic in pointing to something far
Combined the two words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together
The ن changes to م and gets a shadda because it's easier to say when the words combine
The final alif is written long to show it's at the end of the word
Root letters connected with vowels to make past tense 'he revealed'
Added أَ at beginning to show causative meaning (making something happen)
Added elongation mark (ٰ) and pause mark (ٓ) for recitation
This is the basic preposition that shows direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added the pronoun كَ (you) to show who is being addressed. This word connects to أَوْحَىٰٓ (he revealed) to show what was revealed to.
Added fatha to first letter to make it a noun. The doubled middle letter shows intensity, indicating complete mastery.
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Added كَ at the end to show possession (meaning 'your')
Basic preposition indicating source or origin
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْحِكْمَةِ (wisdom)

steps.m: to judge wisely, make wise decisions

steps.n: Changes from the basic root meaning into the noun form meaning 'wisdom'
Added ال (pronounced 'al') at the beginning to mean 'the', like in English

steps.m: of the wisdom (from what your Lord has revealed)

Basic negation particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's telling us not to do the action تَجْعَلْ (make/set up)
Added و at the beginning to connect this command with the previous wisdom mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'

Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
This word is an adjective describing إِلٰهًا (god/deity). The hamza and long alif at the beginning make it into an adjective meaning 'another'.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: then you will be thrown (consequence of previous actions)

steps.m: to blame or find fault with

Shows who receives the blame rather than who does the blaming
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing what's receiving the action in the sentence
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to passive participle form showing who receives the action
Added an -an ending to show this word describes how something was driven away
Added shadda for emphasis, showing the complete nature of being driven away
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to choose specially'. The sukun and final ى show it's this specific form.
Adding فَ makes it mean 'so/then'

steps.m: Did He then choose specially for you all?
steps.n: Added second person plural object pronoun كُمْ to show who was chosen for

steps.m: to care for, develop, raise up

steps.m: Lord (subject of أَفَأَصْفَىٰكُمْ - did He choose for you)

steps.m: your Lord (subject of أَفَأَصْفَىٰكُمْ - did He choose for you)
steps.n: Adding كُم shows this Lord belongs to all of you (plural)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Different word - not related to 'build'. This is the plural of 'son' (ابن) made by adding ين
Added the definite article ال to specify these particular sons
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) at the beginning
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: and he took for himself (as a claim about اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous statement. This describes what some people falsely claim about Allah.
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ (the angels)
Changed root letters into a noun meaning 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a
The singular 'angel' becomes plural by adding alif after first letter and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added feminine ending to show complete group
Added definite article to specify these particular angels
Added kasra at end because it follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Changed the root letters to make the plural word for 'females' by adding إِ at the start and ا in the middle

steps.m: females (as the object being acted upon)

Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed'. This emphasizes what comes after it.
Added كُمْ at the end to show who is being addressed - 'you all'. This word connects to لَتَقُولُونَ (you are saying) in the next part of the ayah.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form. The و changes to ا because it has a fatha before it.
Changed from 'he said' to 'you say' in present tense. The middle letter و appears in present tense.
Adds masculine plural subject marker to indicate plural 'you'

steps.m: indeed you (all) say (a statement that is عَظِيمًا/mighty)

Changed from basic root 'say' into 'a saying' by adding vowels (َ and ْ)

steps.m: a mighty statement (that you are saying)

Adding ي between ظ and م with the vowels makes this describe something that has this quality strongly
The ًا at the end shows this word is describing the word قَوْلًا (statement) that comes just before it in the ayah

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or repeatedly
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word connects to 'في هذا القرآن' (in this Quran) which comes after it.
Basic preposition showing location or position

steps.m: in (connects to هٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ - this Quran)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows that something is happening 'in' the Quran.
Basic pointing word that shows something is near
Added ذا to make it specifically point to a masculine thing that's near
Added the alif mark (ٰ) and fatha marks to help with pronunciation. In this ayah, it's pointing to 'the Quran' which comes right after it.
The root 'read' becomes a noun meaning 'that which is read' by adding ان to make it into Quran
Added ال to make it refer to the specific divine book
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word في (in) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Doubled the ذ letter to show the action is done with more intensity
Added لِ to show purpose - this is explaining why Allah varied examples in the Quran
Added plural ending to show this refers to all people reading the Quran
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَزِيدُهُمْ (increases them) in this ayah
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Changes from past tense (he increased) to present tense (he increases) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel

steps.m: he increases them (referring to their aversion/turning away)
steps.n: The هُمْ shows who is being increased - in this case referring to their aversion mentioned in the next word نُفُورًا (turning away/aversion)
Starting with the basic negation particle and adding alif to complete its basic form
Adding shadda to emphasize the meaning

steps.m: except, only (negating what came before in وَمَا يَزِيدُهُمْ)

steps.m: to run away from something disliked

Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَزِيدُهُمْ (it increases them)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form

Basic particle form that introduces hypothetical situations that didn't happen
Added a shadda (double letter mark) at the start to make the word stronger when spoken. The sukun mark shows where to stop.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it mean 'he was' in the past tense

steps.m: he was (describing what was with مَعَهُ)

Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added attached pronoun هُ meaning 'him' to show who the preposition refers to
Added elongation mark (ۥٓ) to indicate prolonged pronunciation of the final syllable

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple deities
Added tanween dammah to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was/were) in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows similarity or comparison

steps.m: like what/as (connects to يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Adding ما makes the comparison more general or conditional. Here it connects to what 'they say', showing how something is similar to what they claim.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the past tense form
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added ونَ at the end to show that multiple people are doing the action
This is the basic form meaning 'when' or 'at that time'

steps.m: then/in that case (showing result of لَّوْ كَانَ - if there were)
steps.n: Adding ً (tanween) at the end changes this from just 'when' to showing a result or consequence. Here it shows what would happen if there were other gods.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create past tense verb

Added وْا to show multiple people doing the action
Added لَ to show this is part of a conditional statement (if...then)
Added shadda on ل and changed ا to ٱ for pronunciation, plus small high rounded zero

Added small alif (ٰ) above final ى to show it's pronounced with a long 'aa' sound
This is a special word that starts with just the letter ذ. It's used to show ownership or possession.
Added kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it (الْعَرْشِ - the Throne)
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This makes it ready to connect to the next word (الْعَرْشِ - the Throne).

steps.m: to build something high or elevated

steps.m: the throne (possessed by ذي - owner of)

steps.m: to make a path or way
