Adding ي makes this a permanent path/way rather than temporary state

steps.m: a way (object of لَّٱبْتَغَوْا۟ - they would seek)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the object being sought in the phrase 'they would seek a way'

steps.m: to declare perfection, to praise as perfect

steps.n: The ان ending makes it more emphatic, showing complete perfection
The هُۥ at the end means 'His', showing this glorification belongs to Allah. Like saying 'Glory be to Him'
Changed root letters into past tense verb meaning 'he became high' by adding a-vowels
Added تَ at the beginning to show doing something to yourself (like 'made himself high'), and the ى ending shows it's a special form of the past tense
Added و at beginning to connect this word to the previous word سُبْحٰنَهُۥ
The ت shows the action is done to oneself - Allah exalts Himself
Starting with the two separate words that will combine. عَنْ is a preposition meaning 'away from' or 'about', and مَا means 'what'
When عَنْ combines with مَا, the ن assimilates into م with a shadda, and they become one word
Added basic past tense form first, which is needed before making present tense
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now
The ونَ at the end shows many people are doing it
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the quality of being high

steps.m: greatly/to a great height (describing how Allah is exalted above what they say)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word describes how much Allah is above what they say. This connects to وَتَعٰلَىٰ (and He is exalted) in the ayah.
Added ي between the second and third root letters to show this is a strong quality. Also added vowel marks to show how to pronounce it.
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing how Allah is exalted (عُلُوًّا). This ending makes it work like an adverb in English (telling 'how' something happens).

steps.m: to float/glide through space in praise

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows this is done with extra effort or repeatedly
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'she/it' by replacing يُ with تُ at start
Added fatha to show this is connecting to something
Added هُ at the end to show this means 'him'. This word shows who is receiving the glorification in تُسَبِّحُ (glorify).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (rose up) to a noun (sky) by adding ء at the end
Added وات to make it plural, showing multiple heavens
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action in تُسَبِّحُ (glorifies)
Added ٱل to specify these particular heavens, and doubled the س with shadda
Added vowel marks to connect the letters. This word describes 'the heavens' (السَّمٰوٰتُ) in the ayah.
Added the definite article 'the' and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject describing 'the heavens'
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form the noun
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject of يُسَبِّحُ (glorifies)
Added وَ to connect this with ٱلسَّمٰوٰتُ (the heavens) mentioned before it
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to those who are in the seven heavens.

steps.m: and who/whoever (referring to those in السَّمٰوٰتُ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it in the ayah, showing that this is an additional group that also glorifies Allah.
This is a preposition indicating location or containment
Added the kasra vowel mark to show proper pronunciation
Added pronoun هِ meaning 'it/him' to show what is being referred to

steps.m: in them (feminine plural, referring to السَّمٰوٰتُ the heavens)
steps.n: Added نَّ to make the pronoun feminine plural, referring back to the heavens which is treated as feminine plural in Arabic

steps.m: if (introduces a condition in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition 'if there is anything' in this ayah

steps.m: and if (connects to previous statement about heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this condition to the previous statement about how the seven heavens and earth glorify Allah
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of
Added kasra vowel mark under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel sound, making the م sound stronger. This مِّن connects to the word شَىْءٍ (thing) in this ayah.
Adding vowels to make the word readable

The ٍ (tanween kasra) shows this word is indefinite (any thing) and is being possessed or belongs to something. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase 'there is not from any thing' (وَإِن مِّن شَىْءٍ).

This is a complete particle used for exclusion that connects two parts of the sentence. In this ayah, it connects 'glorifies' (يُسَبِّحُ) with 'with His praise' (بِحَمْدِهِ), showing that everything glorifies specifically with His praise.

steps.m: to glorify, praise by declaring perfection

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter makes the action more intense or repeated
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start. Shows ongoing/repeated glorification of Allah. Links to تُسَبِّحُ (glorifies) later in the verse.

steps.m: to praise, commend, or express gratitude

steps.n: Adding the fatha (َ) mark helps show this is a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (doing praise) to a noun (the act of praise) by adding a stop mark
Added the possessive pronoun هِ (His) which refers to Allah in this context, and the kasra (ِ) that connects it
Added بِ (with/by) to show this praise is the means by which the glorification (تُسَبِّحُ) happens
This is a particle that shows contrast between ideas. Here it connects the previous statement about everything praising Allah with the following statement about people not understanding this praise.

steps.m: but (connects to previous statement while showing contrast)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the beginning connects this contrasting statement to the previous one about everything praising Allah
Basic negative particle used to negate what comes after it
Added shadda to emphasize the negation. This emphasis shows up because the word إِلَّا appears in the ayah, where the initial إِ has merged with the لا to create the emphasized لَّا
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'to understand'
Changed from 'he understood' in the past to 'you understand' in the present by adding تَ at the start
Added ونَ to show that many people are doing this action. This word connects to تَسْبِيحَهُمْ (their glorification) which is what they don't understand.

steps.m: to glorify or praise by declaring perfection

steps.n: Added shadda to show intensive glorification
Changed verb into noun showing the act itself
Added هُمْ to show possession and َ to connect it. This word is what you don't understand according to تَفْقَهُونَ (you don't understand) in the ayah.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' in English
Added هُۥ at the end to show who is being talked about. This word connects to كَانَ (was) in the ayah, showing who 'was' forgiving and forbearing.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ي between ل and م to create intensive adjective pattern فَعِيل, with vowels َ and ِ to match the pattern. This creates the meaning of someone who has this quality intensely.
The word got a small mark (tanween) at the end to show it's describing كَانَ (he was) - like saying 'He was patient'

steps.m: to forgive or cover up faults

steps.n: Added vowels and و to show this describes someone who does a lot of forgiving

steps.m: one who forgives a lot (describing كَانَ - was)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is describing the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah

steps.m: when (introduces the time of قَرَأْتَ - you recited)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces when something happens. Here it introduces when 'you recited' (قَرَأْتَ) the Quran.

steps.m: and when (connects this time clause to what came before)
steps.n: The و connects this new time situation to whatever came before it in the previous ayah

steps.m: to read or recite (especially sacred texts)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a complete word showing something that happened in the past
Added تَ at the end to show this is addressing 'you' (masculine singular). In this ayah, Allah is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ about when he recites the Quran.

steps.m: to read or recite something aloud
that which is recited (the divine revelation)
steps.n: Changed the word from an action into a special noun form that means 'the thing that is recited'. This is a special pattern used for important religious concepts.

steps.m: the Quran (object of قَرَأْتَ - you recited)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific (the Quran), and َ at the end shows it's receiving the action of reciting in this ayah

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way

steps.n: Added fatha (َ) after each letter to show this happened in the past. The last fatha is hidden because of the next change.
Changed from 'he made' to 'we made' by adding نا at the end
Added vowel marks to make the word mean 'between'. This word is what was placed by جَعَلْنَا (we placed).

steps.m: between you (object of جَعَلْنَا - we placed)
steps.n: Added كَ to show this is happening between 'you'. This whole phrase is what Allah placed.
Added fatha and sukun to form the preposition 'between'
Adding و connects this to the previous 'between' (بَيْنَكَ) in the phrase 'between you and between those who...'
This is a relative pronoun used to connect clauses
Added ال with shadda to make it specific
Changed to plural form by adding ين and using hamzat wasl. This word describes the people who don't believe in the hereafter.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe), making it mean 'they do not believe'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Added an alif at the start changed it from 'trust' to 'have religious faith/believe'
Changed to present tense with yu- prefix and internal vowel changes

steps.m: they believe (those who لَا do not)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show multiple people. This word refers to those who do not believe, as indicated by لَا (not) before it in the ayah.
Adding آ at start makes it mean 'more/most last'
Adding ة makes it feminine and changes meaning to 'the afterlife'
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Adding preposition changes meaning to 'in/with/by'
Adding kasra ending to show genitive case after preposition
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Forms a noun meaning 'thing that blocks'. This word is what Allah says He placed between the Prophet and those who don't believe.

steps.m: a barrier (object of جَعَلْنَا - We made/placed)
steps.n: Shows this is what was made/placed (like saying 'We made a barrier' instead of 'We made the barrier')

steps.m: hidden/concealed (as a result of being acted upon)
steps.n: Added مَ at the start and و before the ر to make it mean 'something that was hidden' instead of 'to hide'
Adding shadda (double mark) on م makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: completely hidden/concealed (describing the barrier mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing the حِجَابًا (barrier) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. The ْ (sukun) on ل shows the word is pausing before the نَا.

steps.m: and we made/put (connected to previous statement)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the verse
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else

Added an alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is a small alif written above the ى. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it shows what they turned away upon - their backs.
Changed to noun form meaning 'heart'. The heart is named this way because it can turn emotions and thoughts.
Changed to broken plural form meaning multiple hearts by adding و and changing vowels to ُ
Added هِمْ with kasra (ِ) connector to show the hearts belong to them
The doubled ن (shown by ّ) makes the meaning stronger
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific covering, and the vowel change to ِ is common with this pattern
Adding أَ at the beginning makes it a broken plural (multiple coverings), like changing 'box' to 'boxes'

steps.m: coverings (as the object being placed on their hearts)
steps.n: The ً shows this word is receiving the action of جَعَلْنَا (we placed/made)

steps.m: that (connects to يَفْقَهُوهُ - they understand it)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two ideas together. Here it connects the previous part about 'coverings on their hearts' with what happens because of those coverings - 'they cannot understand it'.
Connected the root letters together with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now or in the future. The vowels change to match this new form.
Adding و at the end changes it from 'he' to 'they' (for a group of men)

steps.m: they understand it deeply (object of أَن)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows what is being understood. This word comes after أَن (that) in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows the deafness is in their ears.

steps.m: and in (connects to ءَاذَانِهِمْ - their ears)
steps.n: Added وَ at the start to connect this phrase to what came before it, showing this is another thing Allah did to their hearts

steps.m: and in (connects to ءَاذَانِهِمْ - their ears)
steps.n: Added an elongation mark (ٓ) to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word
Root word for 'ear' becomes plural by adding alif at start and stretching middle letter
Added هِمْ to show possession by a group

steps.m: to be deaf or heavy (especially in ears)

steps.n: Changes from action to the condition itself. This word describes what Allah placed in their ears to prevent them from hearing.

steps.m: deafness/heaviness (being placed in their ears by جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word is receiving the action of جَعَلْنَا (we placed). Allah is saying He placed this deafness in their ears.

steps.m: when (introduces what happens when you mention Allah in the Quran)
steps.n: This is a word that introduces a condition or time. In this ayah, it specifically introduces what happens when the Prophet mentions Allah in the Quran.

steps.m: and when (connects to previous statement about their hearts having covers)
steps.n: The وَ connects this condition to the previous statement about their hearts having covers, showing these are related ideas
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the final vowel to sukun to prepare for adding the subject marker

steps.m: you remembered/mentioned (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added تَ at the end to show that 'you' (masculine singular) did the action. In this ayah, this is referring to when Prophet Muhammad ﷺ mentions/remembers Allah.

steps.m: to take care of, raise, develop

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does this action perfectly and completely

steps.m: your Lord (object of ذَكَرْتَ - you mentioned)
steps.n: Adds كَ to show it belongs to 'you'. This word is what is being mentioned in the phrase 'when you mentioned your Lord'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that something (وَقْرًا - deafness) is in their ears.

steps.m: to read or recite something carefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: recitation, that which is recited abundantly
steps.n: Changes from verb 'read' to noun meaning 'recitation' by adding special pattern that shows something done a lot
Added ٱلْ to make it specific (the Quran), and ِ because it follows the word فِى (in)
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding vowel marks (َ and ْ) to describe being alone

steps.m: Him alone (referring to Allah in القرءان)
steps.n: The word 'alone' gets هُۥ (meaning 'Him') attached to it, changing it from just 'alone' to specifically 'Him alone'
Basic past tense pattern (fatha vowels) added to make 'he turned away' from the root meaning 'to turn away'
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ل makes the action more intense or repeated

steps.m: they turned away intensively (subject of نُفُورًا)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to نُفُورًا (in aversion) in the next ayah.

steps.m: upon/on (preposition showing position of أَكِنَّةً on قُلُوبِهِمْ)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is positioned on top of or over something else. Here it shows that covers (أَكِنَّةً) are placed upon their hearts (قُلُوبِهِمْ).

steps.m: upon/on (preposition showing position of أَكِنَّةً on قُلُوبِهِمْ)
steps.n: Added an alif symbol (ٰ) to show the long 'aa' sound at the end. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: upon/on (preposition showing position of أَكِنَّةً on قُلُوبِهِمْ)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) above to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to turn back or away from

steps.n: Changed to broken plural form by adding أ at start and ا after د - means 'backs' instead of just 'back'
Changed ending to show it belongs to something (like saying 'backs of')

steps.m: their backs (referring to those who turn away when the Quran is recited)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show the backs belong to them. In this ayah, it refers to those who turn away when the Quran is mentioned.

steps.m: to run away in fear or aversion

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels and added و to make it into a noun describing the action itself

steps.m: in aversion (describing how they turned away in وَلَّوْا۟)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the action وَلَّوْا۟ (they turned away) was done - they turned away in a state of aversion/fleeing
This is a standalone pronoun that doesn't come from a root. Adding basic vowel marks to show correct pronunciation.
Adding shadda for emphasis. This emphasizes that Allah is specifically referring to Himself with majesty and power.
Basic root meaning showing the concept of knowing or understanding
Basic past tense verb meaning 'to know' - the middle kasra vowel is common for verbs about knowing things
Changed عَلِمَ (he knew) to أَعْلَمُ (most knowing) by adding أَ at the start to make it mean 'most knowing'. Like saying 'We are the ones who know best' in English.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what'
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how they do the listening mentioned in يَسْتَمِعُونَ (they listen)
Adds يَ at the start to make it 'he' + present tense, like changing 'hear' to 'he hears'

steps.m: he seeks to listen/tries to hear
steps.n: The ست combination shows actively seeking or trying to do the action

steps.m: they seek to listen/try to hear (to you)
steps.n: The ون ending shows multiple people (men or mixed group) are doing the action. In this ayah, they are listening to the Prophet ﷺ, shown by إِلَيْكَ (to you) after this word.
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or used
Added pronoun ه to show who/what the preposition refers to
Added pronunciation helpers (small و and elongation mark) that don't change the meaning
This word shows when something happened in the past. Here it introduces what the wrongdoers said.

steps.m: to hear or listen to something

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he heard'
Added است at the beginning to show actively seeking or deliberately doing the action
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they seek to listen/pay attention (to مَا)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This verb relates to مَا (what) that they are listening to in the ayah.
This is a preposition showing direction or destination
The ى changes to ي before adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.

steps.m: to you (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is directed to 'you' (masculine singular). In this ayah, it's specifically addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
This is a time word that points to something that happened in the past. In this ayah, it introduces what the wrongdoers said.

steps.m: and when (connecting to what the disbelievers were listening to)
steps.n: Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about the disbelievers listening.
Basic pronoun meaning 'they'. This word is the subject of يَسْتَمِعُونَ (they listen) in this ayah.
Added damma (ُ) on ه and sukun (ْ) on م to show correct pronunciation
Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'private talk' by adding vowel marks
Added ى (alif maqsurah) at the end to make it mean 'secret whispering' specifically
Added alif extension mark (ٰ) and lengthening mark (ٓ) to show proper pronunciation
This is a basic word used to talk about something that happened in the past. It's like saying 'at that time' or 'when' for past events.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly: 'i' sound under the إ and a stop mark on ذ. In this ayah, it's used to talk about the time when they listen to you.
Changed vowels to make it a present tense base. The و becomes long with sukun.

steps.m: he says (describing what الظَّالِمُونَ/the wrongdoers do)
steps.n: Added يَ at the start to show it's happening now. This word tells us what the wrongdoers (الظَّالِمُونَ) are doing.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: one who wrongs (subject of يَقُولُ - says)
steps.n: Shows who does the action - like adding '-er' in English (wrong → wronger). These are the people speaking in the ayah.
Adding the definite article makes the word definite
Changes from singular (one wrongdoer) to plural (many wrongdoers) by adding ون at the end
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'if' or 'not' depending on context. Here it's used with the verb يَتَّبِعُونَ (they follow) to negate it, making the meaning 'they do not follow'

steps.m: to follow behind or go after something

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'to follow'
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels for present tense
Added shadda to intensify the meaning, showing persistent or close following

steps.m: you all follow closely/persistently (referring to the wrongdoers/الظَّالِمُونَ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to the wrongdoers (الظَّالِمُونَ) who are speaking.
This is a basic word that means 'except'. In this ayah, it connects to تَتَّبِعُونَ (you follow) to show what is being excluded.
The vowels make it a noun meaning 'man'
Added ًا (tanween fatha) to show this is 'a man' when it's the object being described in the sentence

steps.m: to cast magic or enchant someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added مَ at the start and changed vowels to make it passive - shows someone receiving magic rather than casting it
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound
Added ًا at the end to show this describes the man (رَجُلًا) they are talking about in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to 'u' to make it a command to one person
Added helper alif at start to make the command easier to pronounce
Added final sukun to show end of word. This word is connected to كَيْفَ (how) in the ayah, telling us to look at how something happened.
Adding vowel marks to make the question word 'how'

steps.m: to hit, strike, or set forth (examples/parables)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form. In this ayah, it's used to mean 'set forth' rather than physical hitting.

steps.m: they set forth (examples for لَكَ - for you)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This connects to لَكَ (for you) in the ayah, showing they set forth examples specifically for the Prophet.
Starting with the basic particle ل which shows direction or purpose

steps.m: to/for you (object of ضَرَبُوا - they struck)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is directed at 'you'. This word shows who the examples were struck/made for in the phrase 'how they struck examples for you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (resembled) to a noun meaning an example or model
Changed to plural by adding hamza at start and changing middle vowels
Added ال to make it definite (specific examples)

steps.m: the examples (object of ضَرَبُوا - they made)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb ضَرَبُوا (they made/struck)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Doubled the middle letter to show intensity of going astray
Added وا to show many people did this action

steps.m: so they went far astray (result of ضَرَبُوا۟)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of them making up false comparisons (ضَرَبُوا۟) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is a connecting particle that shows one thing follows another, like saying 'so' or 'then' in English
Added the negative particle لا which prevents something from happening. In this ayah, it connects to يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (they are able) to say they cannot do something.
Combining 'and then' with 'not' creates a flowing negative connection
Connected the root letters with basic past tense voweling
Adds يَسْتَ at the start to mean 'being able to' do something
Adds a 'u' sound at the end to show this verb is in its basic form
Adds ون at the end to change from 'he' to 'they' (for a group of men)

steps.m: to make a path or way

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from verb to noun meaning 'path'
Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object being acted upon in the phrase 'they cannot find a way'
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed the middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Added وَ to connect this to what came before. In this ayah, they are questioning how they could be resurrected after becoming bones and dust.
This is the basic word meaning 'when' that refers to a future time
Added أَ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question
The إِ changes to ءِ to show extra emphasis in the question, expressing strong doubt or disbelief about what is being asked
Changed root letters into a noun form by adding the vowel 'u' and lengthening the middle letter
The weak letter و drops out between the damma and sukun
Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' did this action in the past

steps.m: to be massive or great in size

steps.n: Root meaning 'great/massive' becomes the noun 'bone' by adding vowels - like how bones are big strong parts of the body
Changes singular 'bone' to plural 'bones' by adding ا in middle - common way to make plural for body parts
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of كُنَّا (we were). The people are asking if they will be brought back to life after becoming bones.

steps.m: to crumble or break into tiny pieces

Starting with the basic word that shows emphasis
Added نا at the end to show this is about 'we/us'

steps.m: is it really that we? (questioning what they say in كُنَّا)

Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'sent/raised' to 'one who is sent/raised' by adding مَ at start and changing vowels
Adding ون makes it plural - more than one being raised

steps.m: surely ones who are raised up
steps.n: Adding ل adds emphasis/certainty to the meaning

Changed the pattern to make it plural, showing many stones

This is a coordinating particle that connects two choices
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and make it into a noun meaning the result of creating

steps.m: from what/from that which (emphasized) (referring to something that يَكْبُرُ - grows big)

steps.m: your chests (referring to the disbelievers being addressed)
steps.n: Added ِكُمْ at the end to show these chests belong to the group being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at the start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Added سَ to show this will happen in the future
Added فَ to show this happens as a result of something

نَا means 'us' - shows who is being returned

steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show indefiniteness and connection to other words
