steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about calling upon false gods, showing the result - that they have no power.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they possess/own (referring to those you claimed as gods besides Allah)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show multiple subjects. In this ayah, it refers to the false gods that people claimed besides Allah, showing they don't have power to remove harm.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to a verbal noun (the act of removing) which is the object of يَمْلِكُونَ (they possess/have power over) in this ayah
Root meaning 'harm' becomes a noun showing the actual harm/hardship by adding damma and combining the two ر letters
Added ال to specify a particular harm being discussed
The word gets a kasra (i-sound) at the end because it's connected to 'removal of' - like saying 'removal of THE HARM'
This is a preposition that shows something moving away or being separated from something else
Added كُمْ to show who is being moved away from - in this case, 'you all'
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word negates يَمْلِكُونَ (they possess/have power), saying 'and they do not have power'

steps.m: to change or turn something from one state to another

steps.n: Added shadda to show intensive meaning - doing something thoroughly or repeatedly
Changes the verb into a noun that describes the action itself
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. This word is what they 'do not possess/control' (لَا يَمْلِكُونَ)
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to things or people
Adding ى shows distance - this indicates we're pointing to something far away

steps.m: those (pointing to people far away) (subject of يَدْعُونَ)
steps.n: أُولَى becomes أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ when we want to point to people who are far away. Like saying 'those people over there'. It's the group doing the action يَدْعُونَ (calling) in this sentence.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ with shadda to make it specific

steps.m: those who/the ones who (subject of يَدْعُونَ)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word is the subject of يَدْعُونَ (they call/invoke) in this ayah.

steps.m: to call upon, invite, or pray to

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and form past tense. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels, and restored original و at end
Added نَ to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to يَبْتَغُونَ (they seek) in the ayah, showing what these people do.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added ا at start and ت after first letter to show doing something with extra effort
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing final ى to ي
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word continues the meaning of يَدْعُونَ (they call upon) from earlier in the verse.

This is a basic preposition that shows movement or direction towards something. In this ayah, it connects يَبْتَغُونَ (they seek) with رَبِّهِمُ (their Lord), showing they are seeking towards their Lord.
The alif at the end is written with a small mark (ىٰ) to show it's a long vowel sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Added fatha on first letter and shadda on second letter to create the noun meaning 'lord'
The word 'Lord' gets 'their' added to it by attaching هم (meaning 'their') at the end
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (seeking) to a noun (one who seeks)

steps.m: a specific means of seeking closeness
steps.n: Added ة to make it a specific instance or concrete example
Added ال to make it specific and known

steps.m: the means of seeking closeness (object of يَبْتَغُونَ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb يَبْتَغُونَ (they seek)
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added pronoun هم to show 'of them'. This word is asking which of them is closest (أَقْرَبُ) in the ayah.
This is the basic concept of nearness or closeness
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing a state of being near
Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it a superlative, showing the most near/closest among them. The ُ at the end shows this describes who/what is being talked about.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The ا changes back to و.
Adding نَ at the end makes the verb for a group of females

steps.m: and they hope (for رَحْمَتَهُۥ - His mercy)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous phrase. This word connects to رَحْمَتَهُۥ (His mercy) which comes after it in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form the basic noun meaning 'mercy'
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of mercy
Changed ة to ت to prepare for next change
Added هُۥ to show this is His mercy
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing last vowel to ُ
Changed from singular to plural by adding ون at the end
Added و at beginning to connect this action with the previous one. In this ayah, they fear Allah's punishment specifically.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to create a noun meaning the result of the action
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being feared in يَخَافُونَ (they fear)
Added pronoun suffix to show possession - this is what they fear in the verse
Added kasra to show this is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger. It's connected to عَذَابَ رَبِّكَ (the punishment of your Lord) in this ayah.
Added shadda to make the emphasis even stronger. This shows that what comes after it (about the punishment) is very certain and important.
This root represents the basic concept of punishment or torment
Connected the root letters and added alif after ذ to make it a noun. This word is what is being feared (object of يَخَافُونَ - they fear) in this ayah.
This word is being acted on - it's what they fear (connected to يَخَافُونَ/they fear)
Connected the root letters and added fatha on first letter. The middle letter is doubled with shadda to show intensity, meaning complete or perfect lordship.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something (in this case, it will be possessed by 'you' in the next step)
Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to you (masculine singular). This whole phrase 'your Lord' is possessed by the word عَذَابَ (punishment) in the phrase 'punishment of your Lord'.
This root represents the basic concept of being or existing
Added fathas to make the past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to be cautious or guard against
steps.n: This is the basic concept of being cautious or guarding against something
Added vowels to make it a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'being cautious' to 'something we should be cautious of' - like changing 'fear' to 'fearsome thing'

steps.m: something to be cautious of (as object)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
This is a compound particle made up of و (and) + إن (if)
Basic preposition that shows where something starts or what it's made of
Added shadda to emphasize the word because it comes after a word ending in ن (إِن). This is a common Arabic rule to make pronunciation easier.

steps.m: to gather or settle in a place

steps.n: Added vowels and ة to create a noun meaning 'town/village' - a place where people gather and settle
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is any town/village that is being acted upon in the verse (being destroyed or punished, as mentioned in مُهْلِكُوهَا - destroying them)
This is a basic exception word that shows something is different from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it connects to قَرْيَةٍ (town/village) to show no town is excluded from what follows.
The alif at the end helps connect this word smoothly to what comes after it in the sentence.
This is the base form of the first person plural pronoun meaning 'we'

steps.m: we (as the doers of مُهْلِكُوهَا - destroyers of it)
steps.n: Added نُ at the end to show this is the subject form of 'we'. In this ayah, it shows we are the ones doing the destroying (مُهْلِكُوهَا)

steps.m: to destroy, to cause to perish

steps.n: Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who does the action of destroying
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action
Changed from singular to plural by adding و

steps.m: those who will destroy it (subject of نَحْنُ/we)
steps.n: Added هَا meaning 'it' referring to the town/city (قرية). This word tells us what 'we' (نحن) will do.

steps.m: to face or be in front of

steps.n: Changes verb 'to face' into noun meaning 'before'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is possessed by يَوْمِ (day) that comes after it
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
The ِ at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to the word after it (الْقِيٰمَةِ). Together they mean 'Day of Resurrection'.
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding fathas and changing middle letter و to ا
Changed the verb into a noun showing the action itself. The middle letter و changed to ي because of the kasra before it
Added ة to specify this particular type of rising - the resurrection
Added ال to make it refer to THE resurrection specifically

steps.m: the resurrection (being described by يَوْمِ/day of)
steps.n: Added kasra at end because it's being described by the word يَوْمِ (day of). The alif changed to a long alif (ٰ) in writing
Basic coordinating conjunction showing alternatives. In this ayah, it connects 'destroying them' with 'punishing them severely', showing these are two possible outcomes.
Added مُ at start and doubled the middle letter with ِّ to show who does the action. This word connects to نَحْنُ (we) in the ayah.

steps.m: those who punish (plural doers of نَحْنُ/we)
steps.n: Added و and ُ at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: those who will punish it (referring to قَرْيَةٍ/town)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what is being punished - refers back to قَرْيَةٍ (town) mentioned earlier

steps.m: to punish severely or cause pain

steps.n: Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning the result of punishment
Adding ًا (tanween) at the end makes it 'a punishment' instead of just 'punishment'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to make it an adjective showing intensity
Added tanween alif to show this describes how the punishment (عَذَابًا) is done

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state or condition)

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to show something happened in the past

steps.m: it was (describing something in the ayah)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in the past tense. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) to show distance
Adding ل with kasra (لِ) helps emphasize the distance
The ك at the end is used for addressing or drawing attention to something. Here it points to what is written in the book.
Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else. Here it shows something is written 'in' the book (الْكِتٰبِ).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he wrote' changes to mean the thing that was written (book) by changing the vowels and adding an alif. Like how drawing makes a drawing.
Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the book') and ِ at the end because it follows the word في (in/within) in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Changed from 'wrote' to 'written' by adding مَ at start and changing vowels - makes it passive

steps.m: written (as something acted upon in كَانَ ذٰلِكَ - that was)
steps.n: Added -an ending (tanween) to show this is describing 'that' in 'that was written'
This is a particle that can either ask questions (what?) or make negatives (not). In this ayah, it's used with the verb نُرْسِلُ (we send) to say 'we do not send'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. The و means 'and'
Added fathas to make it a past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show who was prevented (us)
Changed the ending نا to نآ to show it's a longer sound
This is a connecting word that joins نُّرْسِلَ (we send) to what came before it. It works like saying 'that' in English when you explain what someone did or will do.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together to make a past tense verb
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed the vowels to show it's happening in the present
Added shadda on ن and changed the final vowel to fatha because it comes after the word أَن in the ayah, which requires these changes

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident
steps.n: This root relates to signs and clear evidence
Root letters changed to make a noun meaning 'sign' by adding a long aa sound at start
Adds feminine plural suffix to create plural noun
Added ال to show these are specific signs being referred to

steps.m: with/by the signs (connected to نُرْسِلُ - we send)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show these signs are the means by which something is sent. This word connects to نُرْسِلُ (we send) in the verse.
This is the basic form of the word used to make exceptions. In this ayah, it connects to تَخْوِيفًا (warning/frightening) to say 'except as a warning'.
The alif at the end is written as a long alif (آ) instead of regular alif (ا). This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects 'prevented us' (مَنَعَنَآ) with 'we send' (نُّرْسِلَ), showing what was prevented.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this was done with force or intensity. In this ayah, it refers to how previous peoples strongly rejected Allah's signs/miracles.
Added kasra to the preposition ب to make it pronounceable

steps.m: with it/her (referring to ٱلنَّاقَةَ - the she-camel)
steps.n: Added the feminine pronoun ها to show what/who the preposition refers to. In this ayah, it refers back to the she-camel (ٱلنَّاقَةَ) that was given to Thamud as a sign.
The doubled و (shown by ّ) makes the meaning stronger, showing these people or things came very first before others
The ُ shows this word is doing something or being described

steps.m: first ones (plural, referring to previous nations)
steps.n: Adding ون shows we're talking about many people (specifically previous nations that came before)

steps.m: the first ones (referring to specific previous nations)
steps.n: Adding ال (written as ٱلْ) points to specific previous nations that rejected signs, not just any first ones
Changed meaning from 'come' to 'give/bring' by making someone/something come to someone else
Changed to past tense and added sukun to prepare for suffix
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before

steps.m: Thamud (the name of an ancient Arab tribe)
steps.n: This is the basic form of the name Thamud, referring to an ancient Arabian people mentioned in the Quran who rejected their prophet Salih

steps.m: Thamud (as the object being given the she-camel)
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) at the end to show that Thamud is receiving something (the she-camel) in this sentence
Forms a concrete noun from the root meaning of excellence/choice
Added ال to point to a specific she-camel mentioned in the story of Thamud
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object that received the action of giving in ءَاتَيْنَا (We gave)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show something that makes things clear or visible
Added ة to make it feminine, since it's describing the sign/miracle (آية) which is feminine

steps.m: as something clear and visible (describing الناقة - the she-camel)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this describes how Allah sent the she-camel - as a clear sign

steps.m: to wrong or be unjust to someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: and so they wronged (consequence of كَذَّبَ 'they denied')
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this action happened as a result of them denying the signs mentioned earlier in the verse
Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning with/by
Added هَا at the end which refers back to الْءَايٰتِ (the signs) mentioned earlier in the verse. هَا is used because الْءَايٰتِ is feminine plural.
Basic particle ما that can mean 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it's used with the verb نُرْسِلُ (we send) to negate it, meaning 'we do not send'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added نُ at start to mean 'we', changed vowels to show it's happening now (present tense)
Root meaning relates to something being clear or evident
Root letters combine to make a noun meaning 'something that shows or makes clear' - like a sign or verse
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs
Adds 'with the' to show these signs are being used as tools or means
This is a basic particle that creates exceptions or restrictions. It cannot be broken down further since it's a fundamental tool in Arabic.
Adding the long alif at the end makes this particle connect with what follows it. Here it introduces the exception that the only thing preventing sending signs was that earlier people denied them.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes the word to show doing the action of frightening a lot or making others frightened

steps.m: as a warning/frightening (object of نُرْسِلُ - we send)
steps.n: The added -an ending (tanwin) makes this word describe how or why something is done - in this case, how the signs are sent
This is a time particle that refers to past events. It introduces a specific moment when something happened, connecting to the verb قُلْنَا (we said) that follows it.

steps.m: and when (connecting to قُلْنَا - we said)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to what came before it in the previous ayah, showing this is an additional event being mentioned
Root letters changed to make a command - 'You say!' (like when a teacher tells you to speak)
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action in the past. This word connects to لَكَ (to you) in the ayah.
This is a preposition showing who something is for

steps.m: to/for you (object of قُلْنَا - we said)
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows this is directed to one male person. This word connects to قُلْنَا (we said) to show who is being spoken to.
This is the basic conditional particle that shows uncertainty about something happening
Adding the shadda changes this from a word about uncertainty to one that shows certainty and emphasis. Here it emphasizes that 'your Lord' (رَبَّكَ) did something.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up

steps.n: The doubled ب (with shadda) shows this is someone who does a lot of nurturing and caring

steps.m: your Lord (the one who أَحَاطَ/surrounds all people)
steps.n: Added كَ (ka) at the end to mean 'your Lord' - like adding 'your' in English. The Lord (رَبّ) is described as surrounding/watching over everyone in this verse.

steps.m: to surround or protect something completely

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding an alif between first two letters and putting vowels

steps.m: he fully encompassed (the subject of رَبَّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show complete mastery of the action. In this ayah, it shows how Allah completely encompasses all people with His knowledge and power.

steps.m: people (those who are close/familiar with each other)
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for 'people' by adding alif after first letter
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the people' instead of just 'people'
Added kasra to show this word follows a preposition
Added بِ at beginning to show how something is done or the means by which it's done
This is a particle used to connect to the verb جَعَلْنَا (We made/placed) in this ayah
The و connects this phrase to what came before it in the ayah

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to place

steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did this action. The ْ (sukun) on ل shows the word pauses before the نَا. This نَا shows who did the action (like 'we' in English).
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'a vision' by adding damma on first letter and alif at end

steps.m: the vision (that أَرَيْنٰكَ we showed you)
steps.n: Added 'the' to specify this particular vision. The ر gets doubled (shadda) because it's a 'sun letter' that combines with the ل of ال.
This is the basic feminine demonstrative form
Adding ٱلَّ changes this from a demonstrative (this/that) to a relative pronoun (which/that). It connects this word to what comes after it in the sentence.

steps.m: which/that (feminine, referring to ٱلرُّءْيَا - the vision)
steps.n: The ى with ٓ (alif khanjariyah) is just a spelling variation of ي. This word refers back to 'the vision' (ٱلرُّءْيَا) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at the beginning to make it mean 'cause to see' or 'show'
Changed ى to ي to prepare for adding suffixes
نا means 'we' when added to past tense verbs
Adding ك at the end means 'you' (masculine singular) as the object

steps.m: except (what comes after it from طُغْيٰنًا)
steps.n: This is an exception particle that excludes what comes after it (طُغْيٰنًا meaning 'transgression') from what came before. In this ayah, it means that Allah's warning only increases them in transgression.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he tested) to a noun (a test) by changing the vowels and adding ة at the end
Added tanween to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلْنَا (we made/placed)

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others
steps.n: Basic meaning relates to human companionship and social interaction
Changed to mean 'people' - those who are social and live together. The أ is dropped because it's not needed in this form.
Added ال to mean 'the people' specifically. The ن gets a shadda (double mark) when ال is added.
Added لِ at the beginning to mean 'for' or 'to'. The اَل changes to لّ (with shadda) when combined with لِ.
The laam (ل) is doubled with a shaddah and has a kasra. The word ends with kasra because it follows a preposition
Basic meaning relates to plant growth and branching
This is not a verb - it's the noun 'tree' with basic vowel marks added
Adding ة makes it mean one single tree instead of trees in general
Adds 'and' to connect this word to what came before it
Adds a fatha mark to show this is the object of the sentence
Basic meaning relates to cursing or condemning
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to show something that receives the action of being cursed
Added feminine marker to show this refers to something feminine (in this case, the tree الشَّجَرَةَ mentioned in the ayah)

steps.m: the cursed one (feminine) (describes الشَّجَرَةَ - the tree)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning and case ending to show this describes the specific cursed tree mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: in (connects to ٱلْقُرْءَانِ - in the Quran)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows location or position within something. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱلْقُرْءَانِ to show what something is 'in'.
These three letters together represent the basic idea of reading or reciting
Changed from basic root 'read' to noun 'reading' by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ
The suffix ان makes it mean 'that which is frequently/intensely read'
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific to THE Quran
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word في (in) in the ayah
Basic meaning relates to fear or fright
Doubling of middle root letter (و) intensifies meaning from 'to fear' to 'to frighten'
Changed to 'We' form and present tense - added نُ at start and ُ at end
Added و which connects this action to the previous statements in the verse
Added attached pronoun هم as direct object
Basic interrogative word meaning 'what'

steps.m: and so what (connects to نُخَوِّفُهُمْ - We frighten them)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about frightening them
Basic meaning relates to increase or addition
Connected the root letters and added past tense vowels. The middle letter ي changes to ا in the past tense because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle ا back to ي, and adding ُ at end

steps.m: he increases them (object of وَنُخَوِّفُهُمْ - and We frighten them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who receives the action. This word describes what happens to the same 'them' that are being frightened in وَنُخَوِّفُهُمْ.

steps.m: except (what follows is excluded from فِتْنَةً/trial)
steps.n: This is a common exception particle that shows what comes after it is excluded from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it shows that the vision being a trial is the only purpose - no other purpose.

steps.m: to cross boundaries, to go too far
steps.n: The root conveys the idea of going beyond proper bounds or limits
Changed the verb 'to cross boundaries' into a noun meaning 'crossing boundaries' by adding marks (damma and sukun)
Added ان makes it mean 'a lot of' or 'extreme' crossing boundaries, like when someone really goes too far

steps.m: in an extremely rebellious way (describes يَزِيدُهُمْ - increases them)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this describes how something happens. In this ayah, it describes how Allah's signs only increase the disbelievers' rebellion.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ي between second and third root letters makes it into a strong quality
Adding ًا at the end shows this word is describing something in the sentence
This is a time word that shows when something happened in the past. Here it introduces when Allah told the angels to bow to Adam.

steps.m: and when (referring to قُلْنَا - We said)
steps.n: The و connects this story to previous stories in the Quran, showing it's part of a series of reminders
Command form - just say 'say!' The root letters get shorter and simpler when giving a command.
Adding نا at the end changes it from 'say!' to 'we said'
The alif after ل changes it from a verb (to own/rule) into a noun meaning 'one who has authority'
Changed from singular 'angel' to plural 'angels' by adding ا and ئِ in the middle - this is a special plural form for this word
Adding feminine ending for this special plural form
Added لِ prefix meaning 'to/for' - this connects the angels to the verb قُلْنَا (we said) as the ones being addressed

steps.m: to/for the angels (being addressed by قُلْنَا)
steps.n: Added ال prefix for definiteness ('the')

steps.m: to the angels (being commanded by قُلْنَا)
steps.n: Adds kasra at end to mark genitive case after preposition لِ. The word is the indirect object of the verb قُلْنَا (we said) through the preposition لِ.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to 'u' to make it a command (like 'do this!')
Added ٱ at beginning to make it a proper command

steps.m: prostrate! (all of you) (command to angels in قُلْنَا لِلْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to make it a command for a group. This is Allah commanding all the angels to prostrate to Adam.
This is a proper name
Forms proper name from root meaning 'brown/dark-skinned'

steps.m: to Adam (the one the angels were commanded to prostrate to)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show direction/purpose (to/for) and َ at end to show this is what the action is happening to. This connects to اسْجُدُوا (prostrate) in the ayah, showing who they were commanded to prostrate to.

steps.m: to prostrate (bow down with forehead touching ground)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks. This word shows the angels' response to Allah's command to prostrate (اسْجُدُوا)
The وا ending shows that multiple beings (the angels) did this action together

steps.m: so they prostrated (showing the angels' immediate response)
steps.n: The فَ at the beginning shows this happened right after Allah's command. The ٓ and ۟ marks are pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning.

steps.m: except (introduces إِبْلِيسَ as exception to previous command)
steps.n: Basic exception particle that excludes something from what came before it. Here it shows that Iblis was the exception to the angels who prostrated.

steps.m: except (introduces إِبْلِيسَ as exception to previous command)
steps.n: The alif is written with a special elongation mark (آ) for Quranic writing style. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a proper name borrowed from Greek 'diabolos' meaning 'slanderer/accuser'
The َ (fatha) at the end shows Iblis is being acted upon by the word إِلَّا (except) that comes before it. This means everyone prostrated EXCEPT Iblis.
Added vowels to make the past tense form. This shows someone (he) did this action in the past.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed vowel marks to switch from past to present tense
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action

steps.m: Should I prostrate? (questioning tone used by إِبْلِيسَ)
steps.n: Added ءَ at beginning to make it a question, showing Iblis's defiant questioning

steps.m: who/whom (asking about the object of أَسْجُدُ)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about people. Here it's asking about who/whom Iblis is being told to prostrate to.

steps.m: to whom (object of أَسْجُدُ - I should prostrate)
steps.n: The لِ shows direction - to whom the prostration should be done. This forms part of Iblis's questioning response.

steps.m: to create, form, or make something from nothing

steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: you created (addressing Adam in إِبْلِيسَ's question)
steps.n: The تَ at the end shows this is addressing 'you' (masculine singular). The sukun (ْ) is added before تَ to maintain proper pronunciation. This is part of إِبْلِيسَ's question to Allah about Adam.
Adding the kasra vowel mark turns this into a proper noun meaning clay
The ًا shows this is what was created, as the object of Allah's creation in the phrase 'You created from clay'
Connected the root letters ق-و-ل with fathas to make the past tense verb 'he said'
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added تَ to show 'you' did the action
Added ء (hamza) between vowels to make it easier to pronounce

steps.m: have you seen? (object of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added كَ (meaning 'you') at the end to show who is being asked 'have you seen?'
This is the basic pointing word that shows something is near. The alif on top (ٰ) is a special mark that shows it's stretched out when saying it.
Adding ذَ makes it specifically point to something masculine and nearby
The ا at the end helps show this is a complete pointing word. In this ayah, it's pointing to something Iblis (Satan) is referring to in his speech.

steps.m: who/which/that (referring to كَرَّمْتَ - the one whom You honored)
steps.n: This word connects two parts of the sentence by pointing back to something mentioned before. Here it connects 'this one' with 'whom You honored'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing special honor or dignity being given
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did this action in the past. Changed last vowel to sukun before the ending
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for adding the pronoun
The word 'upon' joins with 'me' (ي), making the last letter stronger (with a shadda)
This is the basic conditional word that shows something depends on something else

steps.m: if indeed (introducing what Allah swore about)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'indeed' or 'truly' before 'if'. This combination is often used in oaths or strong statements.
The doubled خ shows this isn't just a simple delay, but an intensive or repeated delaying
The تَ at the end shows that 'you' did this action in the past

steps.m: you delayed me (speaking to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The نِ at the end shows that 'me' is receiving the action of being delayed. In this ayah, this is Iblis speaking to Allah.
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to يَوْمِ (day) to show 'to the Day'
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show the long 'aa' sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'day'
Added kasra at the end to show this day belongs to or is connected to القيامة (the resurrection). This is like saying 'day of' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he stood) to a noun meaning 'the act of standing'
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance - the Day of Resurrection
Added the definite article to specify this is THE resurrection
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by يَوْمِ (day), forming يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ (Day of Resurrection)
Added emphatic لَ prefix to indicate emphasis. The لَ (lām) adds certainty/emphasis to the verb's meaning.
Added 'I' by changing the first letter to أ (hamza) - this shows I am the one doing the action

steps.m: surely I will definitely take complete control
steps.n: Added double noon (نَّ) for extra emphasis, showing Shaytan's arrogant certainty in this threat
Changed the ة to ت because it will be connected to a pronoun
Added هُ at the end to show possession. This word is what Iblis is saying he will take control of, as the object of لَأَحْتَنِكَنَّ (I will surely take control of).
This is the base form of the exceptive particle that excludes what comes after it from what was mentioned before
Added alif at the end to complete the particle. In this ayah, it's excluding 'his offspring' from those that will be misled.
The ي makes it into an adjective meaning 'having the quality of being little'
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this word is being acted upon. Here it's an exception after إِلَّا (except/but)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is about something that already happened. This word introduces the speech that follows.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
Added sukun on first letter and fatha on middle letter with sukun at end to make basic command form

steps.m: go away! (command from Allah to Iblis)
steps.n: Added alif at beginning to form emphatic command. This is Allah commanding Iblis (Satan) to leave/go away after he refused to bow to Adam.
This is a question word that became a conditional word meaning 'whoever'. In this ayah, it's the subject (doer) of the verb تَبِعَكَ (follows you).
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. It connects this part to Allah's command 'go!' (ٱذْهَبْ) in the previous part.

steps.m: to follow closely behind or pursue

steps.n: Added vowels to make a past tense verb. The َ (fatha) on تَ and عَ with ِ (kasra) on بِ shows this is about something that already happened.
Added كَ at the end to show who was followed. In this ayah, Allah is addressing Iblis/Satan, so the 'you' refers to him.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'
Added هُمْ (them) to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, 'them' refers to those who follow Iblis/Satan, as mentioned in تَبِعَكَ (follow you).
This is an emphasis particle that strengthens statements

steps.m: then indeed (connects to ٱذْهَبْ - go forth)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of the previous command 'go forth'. It connects the consequence to the action.
This is a proper noun referring to Hell, likely of foreign origin. Since it's a foreign word, we start with the letters directly rather than an Arabic root.
Added vowel marks and shadda to show correct pronunciation. This word is receiving the action of جَزَآؤُكُمْ (your recompense) in this ayah.

steps.m: to give what someone deserves (good or bad)

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from action to the thing itself (like changing 'pay' to 'payment')
The word changed to show it's the main subject (doer) of the sentence
Adds 'your' (plural) by attaching كُمْ at the end

steps.m: to pay back or give what is due

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel 'a' between letters
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action

steps.m: a repayment/reward (as the object being given)
steps.n: Shows this is what is being given as repayment in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed to passive participle pattern showing something that was made full/complete
Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda
Added tanween to show this describes how complete the recompense (جَزَآءً) will be
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added است at beginning to show trying/seeking to do the action
Changed from 'he tried' to 'you try!' by putting a stop mark (sukun) at the end and changing middle vowel to 'i' sound
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'
This is a relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence by referring to people. Here it connects to ٱسْتَطَعْتَ (you are able), showing 'whoever you are able [to stir up]'
Added kasra at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb that follows it (ٱسْتَطَعْتَ - you are able)

steps.m: to be able, to have capability

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the start to mean 'seeking/trying to' - like asking for the ability to do something
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action, and changed initial alif to ٱ for connection with previous word
Added sukun to show the word ends here. This is a preposition meaning 'from'.

steps.m: from them (referring to those who follow Shaytan)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show this applies to 'them'. In this ayah, it refers to those who follow Shaytan's misleading.
Added fatha and sukun to create the noun form of the word, changing it from an action (making sound) to a thing (a sound)
Added بِ at the beginning to show this sound is being used as a tool or method

steps.m: with your voice (tool used by الشيطان)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is your voice. This word describes how الشيطان (Satan) should use his voice as a tool, like in English 'with your voice'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed to a command by adding 'u' at the start and changing middle letter's vowel to 'u'

steps.m: and gather/drive! (command to Shaytan in this ayah)
steps.n: Added و (and) at start and changed vowels to match surrounding context. This is part of a series of commands given to Shaytan.
The base preposition عَلَى changes its final ى to ي when connecting to pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِم at the end to show this applies to 'them' (a group of people)
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the collective noun
Added instrumental preposition ب meaning 'with'
Added second person singular possessive pronoun كَ with kasra connector ِ. This word is part of Allah's command to Shaytan to use his cavalry against humans.
Added vowel pattern to create noun for person who walks/goes on foot
Added 'and' (وَ) at the start and changed the ending to show it connects to another word

steps.m: and your foot soldiers (object of بِ)
steps.n: Added possessive suffix كَ to show ownership. This word follows the preposition بِ (with/by) in the phrase بِرَجِلِكَ (with your foot soldiers).

steps.m: to share or join with others in something

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun describing person who does the action
Adding the sukun (little circle) at the end changes this from 'one who shares' to 'share!' - making it a command to one person
Added هُمْ (them) at the end to show who to share with

steps.m: and share with them! (command to Shaytan in this ayah)
steps.n: Added و to connect this command with the previous commands given to Shaytan
This is a helper word that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to الْأَمْوٰلِ (wealth/money) to show 'in wealth'.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic singular noun form
Changed from single to plural by adding أ at start and changing middle letters - this is a common plural pattern in Arabic
Added ال to make it specific
Added kasra at end to show possession
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to the root letters to make it mean 'child' instead of 'to give birth'
Changed to broken plural form by adding hamza at start and alif after second root letter
Added the definite article ٱلْ to specify these particular children
Added وَ to connect this word to what came before it in the verse
Added kasra at the end to show these children belong to or are connected to something
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make past tense verb
Changed from 'he promised' to 'promise!' by adding a stop mark (sukun) at the end - this makes it a command

steps.m: promise them! (command to one person to promise multiple people)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُمْ to show who is being promised
Word is complete - command to one person to make a promise to multiple people
Basic negative/relative particle ما with fatha added for pronunciation. In this ayah it acts as a negative particle that negates the verb يَعِدُ (promises).
Added conjunction و (and) to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by: 1) adding يَ at start, 2) changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra), 3) adding ُ (damma) at end, and 4) dropping the weak letter و
Added هُمُ at the end to show who receives the promise (them)
Root letters got stretched out with extra letters to make a noun meaning 'one who does this a lot'
Added ال to make it specific. The ش gets doubled (shadda) because it's a sun letter

steps.m: Satan (as the doer of the action)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
This is the base form of the exception particle that excludes what follows from what precedes.

steps.m: except (what follows غُرُورًا/deception from what precedes)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to lengthen the final sound. In this ayah, it excludes 'deception' (غُرُورًا) from all of Satan's promises mentioned before.
Root letters got vowel marks (ُ) and a و was added in the middle to make it mean 'deception/false promises'

steps.m: empty tricks and false promises (that Satan promises)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end because this word is receiving the action of يَعِدُ (he promises) in this ayah

Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphasis word that makes what comes after stronger and more certain. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English. Here it emphasizes the statement about عِبَادِى (My servants).
Forms plural showing multiple servants using the عِباد pattern
Added ى at the end to show these are MY servants
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
This special word is used to say something is not true in the present, even though it looks like a past tense word. In this ayah, it's saying that something is not true for someone.
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun 'you' (كَ) to show who the action is directed towards
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'over'. The ى at the end shows it's a preposition.
The ى changes to يْ when we're going to add something to the end of the word. This is just a spelling change.
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being acted upon - 'them'.

steps.m: to dominate or have authority over

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowel marks ُ (u) and ْ (sukoon) to mean 'authority'
Added ٰن at the end to show the authority is intense or complete
The double-damma (ٌ) shows this is talking about authority in general, connecting to the context where Shaytan is being told he has no authority over Allah's servants
Added fatha vowels to make past tense verb. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's at the end of a past tense verb.

steps.m: and it was enough (subject of يَكُونُ)
steps.n: Added و conjunction prefix meaning 'and'
Added dagger alif (ٰ) to show the long /aa/ sound at the end without writing a full alif. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.
The doubled letter shows this is someone who does the nurturing perfectly and completely
The kasra shows this lord belongs to or is connected to something
Adding كَ shows the lord belongs to the person being spoken to

steps.m: by your lord (used as an oath)
steps.n: The بِ at the start is used to make an oath, like saying 'I swear by your lord'

steps.m: to protect/take care of/be responsible for

steps.n: Added ي after first root letter and kasra on first letter to create a noun meaning someone who does this action as their role
Added tanween fatHa (ًا) to show this word is receiving the action of كَفَىٰ (is sufficient). Like saying 'Allah is sufficient as a protector'

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and guide to completion

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and constantly
The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject or is after 'this is...'
Adding كُمُ shows this means 'your' for a group of people
The shadda (double letter) was added to make it stronger after joining with previous word - still means 'your Lord'
Added kasra to specify direction or reference

steps.m: the one which/who (subject of يُزْجِى - drives)
steps.n: Added ال to transform it from a demonstrative to a relative pronoun. This word connects to يُزْجِى (drives/steers) to tell us who is doing the steering.

steps.m: the one which/who (subject of يُزْجِى - drives)
steps.n: Changed the writing style to use waṣla (ٱ) and added emphasis (shadda) on ل. This doesn't change the meaning, just how it's written and pronounced.

steps.m: to push, drive, or propel forward

steps.n: Added fatha (a) vowels between the root letters to make 'he' form of past tense verb meaning 'pushed'
Changed to present tense with يُ prefix. Added vowels to show it's happening now. The word الْفُلْكَ (the ships) is what's being driven/propelled.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added ك with damma to show it refers to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: for you all (connected to يُزْجِى - drives/propels)
steps.n: Added م with damma to complete the plural 'you' ending. This word connects to يُزْجِى (drives/propels) to show who the action is for.

steps.m: to move in a circular path
ship (because it moves through water in paths)
steps.n: The vowel pattern changes this from an action into a thing. A ship was named this way because it moves through water in paths.
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific ship rather than any ship
The fatha at the end shows this is the object being pushed/driven by Allah in the verse
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'the ships' (ٱلْفُلْكَ) with 'the sea' (ٱلْبَحْرِ), showing where the ships are.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to split open widely, to spread out

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic noun for a large body of water. The fatha and sukun pattern shows this is a physical thing rather than an action.
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after the word في (in)
Added ال at the beginning to show we're talking about a specific sea
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added ت after first letter to show doing something for your own benefit
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as subject
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something is done
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a basic particle that shows where something comes from or its source. Here it connects 'seek' (تَبْتَغُوا) with 'His bounty' (فَضْلِهِ), showing they are seeking from His bounty.

steps.m: to give extra or do more than required

steps.n: Root letters put together with vowels to make a noun meaning 'extra gift' or 'favor'
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after the word مِن (from)
Added هِ with a small alif (ۦٓ) to show the favor belongs to Him (Allah)
This particle adds emphasis to a statement

steps.m: indeed he (referring to رَبُّكُمُ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added هُ (hu) at the end to show we're talking about 'he/it' - in this case referring back to 'your Lord' from earlier in the verse

steps.m: indeed he (referring to رَبُّكُمُ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters. This is a theoretical form before the weak letter change.
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Starting with the preposition ب which shows how something is done

steps.m: with you all (connected to يُزْجِى - drives)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This word connects to يُزْجِى (drives) to show who is being driven in the ships.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy
Added ي to make an intensive adjective meaning 'constantly/intensely merciful'
Added tanween alif to show this word is describing the subject of كَانَ (he was)

steps.m: when (introduces the condition مَسَّكُمُ ٱلضُّرُّ - harm touches you)
steps.n: This is a word that shows when something happens, like saying 'at the time when' in English. Here it introduces what happens when harm touches people.

steps.m: and when (introduces the condition مَسَّكُمُ ٱلضُّرُّ - harm touches you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: to touch or affect (physically or emotionally)

steps.n: Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter with shadda because two identical letters come together. This word is connected to ٱلضُّرُّ (harm/distress) in this ayah, showing what affects you.

steps.m: he touched/affected you all (referring to how ٱلضُّرُّ affects people at sea)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show this affects multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to how harm/distress affects people when they're at sea.
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'harm'

steps.m: the harm (that touches you in وَإِذَا مَسَّكُمُ)
steps.n: Added 'the' to show this is referring to a specific harm mentioned in the verse. This harm is what touches or affects the people being addressed.
This is a basic preposition that connects two words by showing where something happens. Here it shows that the harm/distress (ٱلضُّرُّ) happens in the sea (ٱلْبَحْرِ).
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. A sea 'splits' or 'cuts through' the land, which is why this root came to mean sea.
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it follows the word في (in/on) in the ayah

steps.m: to lose the way, go off course

steps.n: Added vowels to make it 'he lost' in the past tense
When two identical letters come together, they combine with a shadda (ّ) mark to show doubling
This is a question word that asks about or refers to people. In this ayah, it refers to those being called upon when people are in trouble at sea.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and damma with و at end

steps.m: you all call out (to other gods when in danger)
steps.n: Added نَ to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to people calling out to false gods when they're in trouble at sea.
This is a basic exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. In this ayah, it excludes Allah (إِيَّاهُ) from those who are lost when harm touches people at sea.
The long alif (آ) is added at the end to add emphasis and extend the sound, showing the complete exclusion of everything except what follows.
This is a special word used before pronouns to emphasize them as the object of an action. In this ayah, it emphasizes who they call upon.

steps.m: Him alone (as the object being called upon)
steps.n: Attached pronoun هُ makes this refer specifically to Allah, who they call upon when in trouble at sea

steps.m: when (connects to نَجَّىٰكُمْ - He saved you)
steps.n: This is a time particle that shows something happened right after something else. Here it connects the rescue (نَجَّىٰكُمْ - He saved you) with what happened after.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or sequence - when one thing happened, another thing followed. Here it connects being saved (نَجَّىٰكُمْ) with turning away (أَعْرَضْتُمْ).

steps.m: to rescue or deliver from danger

steps.n: Added vowels to make it mean 'he rescued' in the past tense
The shadda makes the action stronger, showing a more complete or intense rescue
The alif at the end is just a spelling variation of the same word
Added كُمْ to show the rescue was done to multiple people
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects with ٱلْبَرِّ (the land) to show where people turned towards after being saved from the sea.
Connected the root letters to make the simple word for 'land'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific land rather than any land
The kasra ending shows this word follows the word إِلَى (to) in the ayah
Adding ال makes the word definite/specific
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning and changing the middle vowel to sukun makes the action more intentional

steps.m: you all turned away (from the truth after being saved)
steps.n: Added تُمْ at the end to show that many people (you all) did this action in the past. In this ayah, it refers to people turning away from Allah after He saved them from danger at sea.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.n: Added vowels and ي to show someone who has this quality strongly - like saying this person is so close they're like family

steps.m: a close friend (object of لَّٱتَّخَذُوكَ - they would have taken you as)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action - they would have taken you as a close friend
Starting with the basic conditional particle لو meaning 'if'
Adding لا changes the meaning from a simple 'if' to 'if not for', which introduces a preventing condition
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer
Adding و at the start connects this condition to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: that (connects to ثَبَّتْنٰكَ - We made you firm)
steps.n: This is a basic word that connects two ideas together, like saying 'If it weren't that We made you firm...' in English. It shows what could have happened.

steps.m: that (connects to ثَبَّتْنٰكَ - We made you firm)
steps.n: Added the fatha mark (َ) above أ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'an'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the action stronger and shows it's being done to something else
Added 'we' as the doers of the action and changed the last vowel to sukun
Changed ending from نا to كَ to show 'you' as the one being made steady, with alif elongated
This is a common particle in Arabic that emphasizes past actions. It shows that something definitely happened.
Adding لَ at the beginning adds extra emphasis. In this ayah, it emphasizes how Allah is telling the Prophet that he almost (كِدتَّ) inclined towards them.

steps.m: to almost do something/to be on the verge of

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'he almost did' in the past tense
The weak letter و is dropped and the first vowel changes to كِ following a common pattern with hollow verbs (verbs with و in the middle)
Added تَ to show 'you' (masculine singular) did the action in the past
Added shadda on the ت for emphasis and proper pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: you lean towards (addressing the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and ُ at end. The sukun on ر helps pronunciation. In this ayah, Allah is telling the Prophet ﷺ that he almost leaned towards the disbelievers.
This is a basic preposition showing direction
Changed ى to ي before adding a suffix - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning

steps.m: towards them (referring to the disbelievers being discussed in this passage)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'towards them' - specifically referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the passage
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a noun meaning 'thing'
Changed to show it's receiving the action - added tanween (double marks) and alif at the end to show something is being inclined towards

steps.m: to be small in amount or size

steps.n: Adds vowels to show this describes a quality of being small in amount
Adding ي makes it a strong adjective meaning 'little/few' as a fixed quality
Added ًا to show this describes how much of something. In this ayah, it describes شَيْـًٔا (something).
This is the basic form meaning 'when' or 'at that time'

steps.m: in that case/then (connects to لَّأَذَقْنٰكَ - We would have made you taste)
steps.n: Adding tanween alif (ًا) changes this from just 'when' to showing a result or consequence. In this ayah, it shows what would happen as a consequence - Allah would make them taste double punishment.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes it about causing someone else to do the action
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added كَ at the end to show who receives the action (you)

steps.m: surely we would make you taste/experience
steps.n: Added لَّ (la) at the start to mean 'surely/definitely' - makes it stronger and shows something would happen
Changes the verb concept into a noun meaning 'double amount'. This word is connected to ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ (life) in this ayah, showing double the punishment in life.
Adds fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the verb أَذَقْنَا (We would have made taste) earlier in the ayah
Added fatha vowels and changed last ي to و to make it into a noun
Added ة to change from the action of living to the noun meaning life itself
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'double amount'. This word is the object (thing receiving the action) of أَذَقْنٰكَ (We would have made you taste) in this ayah.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Adding the fatha mark changes this into a noun meaning 'death'
Changed the word to show death as a state or place rather than just the action

steps.m: the death (as a state or place)
steps.n: Added the definite article to specify this particular state/place of death

steps.m: of the death (as a state or place)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes before it in the ayah (ضِعْفَ)

This word connects two ideas, showing that the second thing happens after the first. In this ayah, it connects what would happen (receiving double punishment) with what would come after (having no helper).
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَجِدُ (you find) into 'you do not find'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding ي at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding final damma
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing ي with ت at the beginning
This is the basic particle that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for you (connected to تَجِدُ - you find)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is directed to 'you'. This word connects to تَجِدُ (you find) in the ayah, showing what you won't find against Allah.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'. It's a particle word that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
When adding a suffix to عَلَى, the ى changes to يْ. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding نَا at the end shows this applies to 'us'. In this ayah, it's used with negative meaning (against us) because of the context about punishment.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who does this action frequently or intensively

steps.m: a helper/supporter (as object of تَجِدُ - you find)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is the object of the verb تَجِدُ (you find) in this ayah
This is a compound particle made up of و (and) + إن (if)
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter).
Changed the word to show multiple people (they) instead of just one person (he). This word connects to لَيَسْتَفِزُّونَكَ (they were about to drive you out) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added Form X pattern است to create meaning of attempting/seeking to provoke
Added present tense prefix for 3rd person masculine singular
Added ون to make the subject plural masculine (they)
The ل prefix adds emphasis/certainty to the verb meaning (surely/indeed)

steps.m: they surely try to provoke you (acted on)
steps.n: Adds ك (you) at the end to show who is being provoked. The word shows someone is doing the provoking to you.
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْأَرْضِ (the earth). This combination means 'from the earth'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters properly
Added ٱلْ to make it specific. The hamza on the alif changes to a wasl mark because it comes at the start.
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after مِنَ (from) in the phrase مِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ (from the earth)
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Adding أ at start makes it mean causing someone else to do the action
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added 'you' as the object by adding كَ

steps.m: so that they make you exit (purpose of كَادُوا)
steps.n: The لِ shows this is the purpose of their attempt mentioned in كَادُوا (they were about to)
This is the basic preposition مِنْ which shows something coming from or out of something else

steps.m: from it/her (referring to الْأَرْضِ - the earth)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what is being referred to (the earth, which is feminine in Arabic)
Added tanween (ًا) to show this is specifically about a consequence or result

steps.m: and in that case (connecting to لَيَسْتَفِزُّونَكَ - they would drive you out)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this consequence to the previous statement about them trying to drive the Prophet out
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'
The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the negation. This happens because it comes after إِذًا (then/in that case) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to be behind or after something

steps.n: Changed from a basic root meaning 'behind/after' into a noun that means 'being behind/after'. Shows where they won't stay, connecting to يَلْبَثُونَ (they remain) in the verse.
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added كَ at the end to show this means 'after you'. The alif is written as ٰ which is just a different way to write it.
This is a basic particle that shows an exception. In this ayah, it shows that 'they would not remain' except for a little while.

steps.m: to be small in amount or time

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to make an adjective showing the quality of being small in amount

steps.m: a little while (describing how long they will stay)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to make the word describe how long the action of staying (يَلْبَثُونَ) will last
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (following) to a noun (way/practice) by adding ة and changing vowels. The doubled ن shows it's a strong rule.

This is a special word that refers to people. In this ayah, it's asking about which messengers were sent before. It connects to the verb أَرْسَلْنَا (we sent) to ask 'who did we send?'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to the past tense verb that follows it (أَرْسَلْنَا meaning 'We sent'). It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' before stating what happened.
Added a sukun (ْ) mark on the د to show where the word stops
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The أ prefix changes this from a simple action to making something happen - like the difference between 'go' and 'make (something) go'
The نا suffix indicates 'we' as the subject of the verb. This is a past tense verb, not affected by case endings.

steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this means 'before you'. This connects to the earlier verb أَرْسَلْنَا (We sent), showing who was sent before.

steps.m: from/of (connects رُّسُلِنَا to what comes before)

Root meaning 'send' becomes plural noun 'messengers' by adding u-vowels
Adds -i ending to show the word is affected by a preposition

This is a basic negation word that makes things negative, like adding 'not' in English

steps.m: and not (negates تَجِدُ - you will find)

Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to us
Added لِ at the beginning to mean 'for' or 'to'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb

Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb

Changes verb 'to pray' into the noun 'a prayer' by adding ة at the end

The middle letter ل gets doubled with a shadda, which adds emphasis to the word
The word 'prayer' gets a special mark at the end because it's receiving the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb

steps.m: at the setting (of الشَّمْسِ - the sun)

Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the next word, meaning 'of the night'

steps.m: to break through, burst forth (like light breaking through darkness)
dawn (the time when light breaks through darkness)

steps.m: to see or be present at something
something that is seen/witnessed (object of كَانَ)

steps.m: and from (connects to ٱلَّيْلِ - the night)

Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to night prayer)

steps.m: to send forth or raise up

steps.n: Added يَ at the start to show 'he' is doing the action. The sukun and fatha help connect the letters. This verb comes after عَسَىٰ (it may be that) to show what might happen.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares in a complete way

steps.m: your Lord (the one who will raise you)

Added tanween (ًا) to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يَبْعَثَكَ (raise you)
Changes from root to passive participle pattern showing who receives praise
Doubles the م to add emphasis/intensity to the meaning
Added -an ending (tanween) to show this is receiving the action - it's the special position that the Prophet will be raised to

The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter. This makes it a command form telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak. This command appears at the start of many Quranic verses where Allah instructs the Prophet to say something.

steps.m: and say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Adds و (and) to connect this command with what came before. Here it connects to the previous commands in the ayah.

Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to make it causative - meaning to make someone else do the action

steps.m: make me enter! (command to Allah in prayer)

Added tanwin kasratayn (ٍ) to show this word is indefinite and is being used to describe or modify another word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing someone else to do the action

Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show where/how something happens. This word connects to صِدْقٍ to mean 'a sincere/truthful way of exiting'

Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed from 'he made' to 'make!' by adding ا at start - turns it into a command

Basic preposition showing purpose or direction. Here it connects to سُلْطٰنًا (authority) to show what is being asked for.
Adding emphasis by doubling the ل letter. This emphasizes the request being made in the prayer.
Adds ى to show this is 'for me'. This is part of a prayer asking Allah to grant authority.

steps.m: from (connects لَّدُنكَ to what comes before)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects 'from Your presence' (لَّدُنكَ) to what comes before it.

steps.m: near, close by, in the presence of

The sukun (circle mark) shows where the word ends
Adds 'your' by attaching كَ. This word follows مِن (from/of) in the phrase 'from Your presence', showing where something comes from.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning authority/power
The ًا at the end shows this is the thing being granted in the phrase 'grant me an authority'. It's like saying 'Give me a strong backing or power.'
Connected the root letters with fathas to make a past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who does this action frequently
Added the two dots under at the end to show this is any helper, not a specific one

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by making it a command - the middle و drops out and the first letter gets a u-sound (damma)

Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to make a noun meaning the essence of being true or real
Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb جَآءَ (came) in 'the truth came'
Added ال to specify this is referring to the ultimate truth/reality, not just any truth

Adding وَ at the beginning shows this happened after and because of 'the truth came' (جَآءَ الْحَقُّ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to active participle pattern فاعِل to show something that does or embodies falseness
Added ُ to show this word is the subject of كَانَ (was)
Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific, like changing from 'falsehood' to 'the falsehood'

Changes verb into a noun describing something that is false. This word is what vanished/perished (زَهَقَ) in the ayah.
Makes it refer to a specific falsehood rather than falsehood in general
Shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence. The alif is written as ٰ but pronounced the same.
Added basic past tense vowels to show this happened in the past
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

Changed the word pattern to show something that happens a lot or is certain to happen

steps.m: to send down repeatedly or gradually
steps.n: Adding shadda makes the action more intense or repeated
Adding ن prefix changes verb to first person plural 'we'

steps.m: and we send down (repeatedly) (this Quran mentioned in مِنَ ٱلْقُرْءَانِ)
steps.n: Adding و connects this to the previous statement. This verb refers to Allah sending down the Quran mentioned later in the verse.
Basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere else
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْقُرْءَانِ (the Quran). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.

steps.m: that which is recited (the divine revelation)
steps.n: This special pattern creates a noun meaning 'that which is recited' and is specifically used for divine scripture
Adding the definite article makes it 'the recitation' rather than just 'a recitation'
The kasra at the end shows this word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah

steps.m: that which (connects شِفَآءٌ to the previous statement)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links what comes before with what comes after. In this ayah, it connects the Quran (mentioned before) with healing (شِفَآءٌ mentioned after), showing that the Quran contains that which is healing.
This is a basic pronoun that refers to someone or something previously mentioned. In this ayah, it refers back to the Quran mentioned earlier.
Added the vowel marks ُ (damma) on ه and َ (fatha) on و to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'huwa'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and change it from a verb to a noun
Adding اء makes it refer to healing as a general concept rather than a specific instance

steps.m: a healing, a cure (describes what القرآن provides)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite - it means 'a healing' rather than 'the healing'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form the base noun
Adding ة makes it refer to a specific instance of mercy
Adding و connects this word to the previous word (شِفَآءٌ) in the ayah
Adding tanween damma shows this is a subject like the word شِفَآءٌ before it

steps.m: to cause to believe, make someone trust
steps.n: Changed first letter to آ (alif with madd) to show making someone else believe or trust
Changed from a verb (to believe) to a noun (believer) by adding مُ at start and changing middle vowels
Added ين at the end to show more than one believer
The definite article ال makes the noun definite/specific
Adding the preposition ل meaning 'for/to'
Starting with the basic negation word لا that means 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous words شِفَآءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ (healing and mercy)
Connected the root letters and added kasra to show basic verb pattern

steps.m: he increases (the loss of ٱلظَّالِمِينَ/the wrongdoers)
steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and 'he' as subject, and ُ at end to show it's doing the action. This verb is acting on the word ٱلظَّالِمِينَ (the wrongdoers) in this ayah.

steps.m: to do wrong or harm to others
one who does wrong to others
steps.n: This pattern shows who does the action. The alif (ا) after the first letter makes it mean 'someone who does wrong' instead of just 'doing wrong'.

steps.m: those who do wrong to others
steps.n: ين makes it plural (more than one). This word is the object (accusative case) of the verb يزيد (increases), showing who is receiving the action of increasing.
Adding ال makes it specific/definite - we're talking about particular wrongdoers
The َ shows this word is receiving the action in the sentence

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was said before)
steps.n: This word creates an exception to what was said before

steps.m: except (excludes خَسَارًا/loss from the previous statement about the Quran)
steps.n: Added the vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce it. In this ayah, it's excluding 'loss' from what the Quran brings - while it brings healing and mercy to believers, it only brings loss to wrongdoers.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he lost' changes to the noun 'loss' - like changing 'ran' to 'running'. Shows what increases for the wrongdoers.
Adds an extra 'an' sound at the end to show this word is receiving the action of 'increases'

steps.m: when (introduces a condition about أَنْعَمْنَا - We bestow favors)
steps.n: This is a word that means 'when' and introduces a condition about what happens at a specific time. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when Allah bestows favors.

steps.m: and when (introduces a condition about أَنْعَمْنَا - We bestow favors)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it in the ayah

steps.m: and when (introduces a condition about أَنْعَمْنَا - We bestow favors)
steps.n: Changed the final alif to a long alif (آ). This is just a writing style used in the Quran and doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding أ at the beginning changes the verb from 'being blessed' to 'giving blessings to others' - like changing from receiving something good to giving something good to someone else
نا shows that 'we' did the action. This is a complete verb form and doesn't have a case relationship with other words in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else. In this ayah, it connects the blessing (أَنْعَمْنَا) to the human (ٱلْإِنسٰنِ).
Added the pattern that makes this word mean 'human' - someone who is naturally social and friendly with others

steps.m: the human being (subject of أَعْرَضَ - turned away)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the human') and ِ at the end because it comes after عَلَى (upon/on)
