Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: he turned away (from the truth mentioned in أَنْعَمْنَا)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action more intentional and directed. In this ayah, it shows how man deliberately turns away after Allah bestows favors (أَنْعَمْنَا) on him.

steps.m: to distance oneself or turn away

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to form past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
The أَى at the end changes to ـَٔا due to a common spelling variation in the Quran. This doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he turned) to a noun (one who turns)
Added هِ to show it belongs to him
Added بِ at the beginning to show how something is done
This is a basic particle that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects two events: when we favor someone (أَنْعَمْنَا) and when harm touches them (مَسَّهُ).
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to touch, feel, or affect something/someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Doubled the middle letter س with shadda to show the action is stronger. In this ayah, الشَّرُّ (evil/harm) is what's doing the touching/affecting
Added هُ at the end to show who was affected - in this case 'him' refers to الْإِنسٰنِ (the human/person) mentioned earlier in the ayah
The doubled letter (shadda) makes the meaning stronger, showing this is not just regular evil but intense evil
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific evil rather than evil in general

steps.m: the evil (subject of مَسَّهُ - touched him)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this word is doing the action in مَسَّهُ (touched him)
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows something that happened before.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.
Changed the middle letter to و with damma to show this is a quality of someone
Added tanween alif to show this describes how someone was. This word describes the state of the person in كَانَ (he was).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma (ُ) on first letter, and 3) adding sukun (ْ) at the end. This is telling one person to speak.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added tanween (ٌ) to show this word is the subject doing the action in يَعْمَلُ (works/acts)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb

steps.m: he works/acts/does (subject doing action according to his way in شَاكِلَتِهِ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and adding ُ at end. This word describes what each person does according to their way/manner mentioned later in شَاكِلَتِهِ (his way/manner).
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing position above something
The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is connected to the word after it (شَاكِلَتِهِۦ meaning 'his way/manner'). Together they mean 'according to his way'.
Added vowels to connect the letters into a past-tense verb
Changed pattern to show who does the action
Added feminine ending to create an abstract noun for the quality or state
Added -i sound at the end to show something belongs to or is connected to something else
Added pronoun suffix to show it belongs to 'him'. This word follows عَلَىٰ (upon) in the ayah.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop gradually

steps.n: Root letters combined with shadda to make 'lord/master' - someone who takes care of others
The word 'lord' gets a u-mark (damma) on top because it's doing the action of 'knowing'
Adding connective particle فَ to indicate sequence/result
Adding 2nd person masculine plural possessive pronoun suffix
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changes to comparative/superlative form meaning 'more/most knowing'. In this ayah, Allah is saying He knows best who is most guided.
This is a question word used to ask about people. It can mean either 'who' when asking about someone doing something, or 'whom' when asking about someone receiving an action.

steps.m: about who/whom (connected to أَعْلَمُ - more knowing)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is about or concerning someone. This connects to the word أَعْلَمُ (more knowing) in the ayah, showing what/who Allah knows about.
This is a pronoun that refers to a male person or thing. In this ayah, it's the subject of the verb يَعْمَلُ (works/does), showing who is doing the action.

steps.m: to cause to be guided, to guide someone
steps.n: Adding alif at start makes it mean 'to make someone do the action'

steps.m: to cause to be guided, to guide someone
steps.n: The alif maqsurah (curved alif) at the end is added because this is a weak-ending verb
Added ي between second and third letters to complete the word's standard form
Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being acted upon - in this case, what someone is being guided to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Adding ون shows multiple people are doing the action
Adding كَ shows who is being asked. This connects to the next word عَنِ which shows what they're asking about.
Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement
This is a basic preposition that shows what topic or subject we're talking about
Added vowel marks to connect this word to the next word (ٱلرُّوحِ meaning 'the soul/spirit'). The kasra (ِ) at the end helps it flow smoothly into the next word.
Added damma on first letter and connected letters to form the noun meaning 'spirit'
Added ٱل to specify 'the spirit' and doubled the ر with shadda because it comes after ٱل
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَنِ (about/concerning) which requires the following word to end in kasra
The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter in a command form. The ُ (damma) and ْ (sukun) make it a command.
The ِ (kasra) is added because this word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah
Connected the root letters and added damma to create a noun for an abstract concept
Adding ٱل makes it refer to the specific spirit being asked about in this ayah

steps.m: the spirit/soul (subject of the question being asked)
steps.n: Adding damma case ending shows this is the subject being asked about in وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلرُّوحِ (and they ask you about the spirit)
Adding vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of this preposition
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. This preposition connects 'the soul' (ٱلرُّوحُ) with 'command' (أَمْرِ) to show the soul is from the command.
Changed from a basic root meaning 'command' into a noun meaning 'a command/matter' by adding marks (fatha and sukun)
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to رَبِّى (my Lord) in the ayah

steps.m: to nurture, raise, sustain, care for

steps.n: Root letters arranged with vowels to make a basic noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Adding shadda to intensify meaning
The kasra shows this word is connected to أَمْرِ (command/matter) in the ayah
Adding ي makes it 'my lord' - first person singular possession
This is a question word that asks about something or refers to something general. In this ayah, it's asking about how much knowledge was given.
Adding وَ connects this word to the previous statement about the spirit/soul
The alif is lengthened to show a pause in recitation, but this doesn't change the meaning
Changed to passive form to show receiving something. The و appears because of the weak letter ي.
Added ت to show 'you' as the receiver of the action
Added م to show this refers to multiple people being given something
This is the basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added kasra under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added shadda on م because it comes after the word ٱلرُّوحُ which ends in a similar sound
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word ٱلْعِلْمِ (knowledge)
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This pattern turns actions into abstract concepts.
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to specific knowledge rather than knowledge in general
The kasra shows this word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
This is a particle (حرف) used for making exceptions. It has no root as it's not derived from a trilateral root.
Added kasra under the إ to show how it's pronounced (i-lla)
Added shadda on ل and alif with fatha to complete pronunciation (il-laa). In this ayah, it's making an exception of قَلِيلًا (little) from what came before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed vowels to make an adjective

steps.m: a little (of the knowledge that was given)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how much of something. In this ayah, it tells us how much knowledge (العِلْمِ) was given (أُوتِيتُم).
This is the basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else happening
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
The إِ changes to ئِ when it comes after لَ - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The vowel changed to كسرة (ِ) at the start and the middle weak letter changed to ء with سكون (ْ) because of this pattern.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The ن at the start shows that 'we' are doing the action
The لَ shows this is happening for a reason or purpose

steps.m: we will most certainly go away
steps.n: Adding emphatic نّ (nūn with shaddah) to create strong emphasis
This is the basic relative pronoun meaning 'that' or 'which'. It connects descriptions to what they're describing.
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being used with something. In this ayah, it connects to أَوْحَيْنَآ (we inspired/revealed).
Changed the final ي to ى with a long vowel mark (ٓ) - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to send a message directly to someone's heart or mind
he sent a message (to someone's heart/mind)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to cause' something to happen. The vowel marks change to match this pattern.
Adding نَا at the end shows that 'we' did the action
The alif at the end is written long to show proper pronunciation
Starting with the basic preposition meaning 'to'
Added sukun before attaching pronoun

steps.m: to you (object of أَوْحَيْنَا - we revealed)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this means 'to you'. This word is connected to أَوْحَيْنَا (we revealed) - showing what was revealed to.

steps.m: then (connects to لَنَذْهَبَنَّ - we would take away)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another with a gap in between. Here it connects 'we would take away' with what comes next, showing these events happen in order with some time between them.
This word makes the verb تَجِدُ (you will find) negative, showing that something won't happen
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The weak letter و is dropped in present tense. This is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in the context of the ayah.
This is a particle that shows direction or purpose
Added كَ to show this is directed to 'you'
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning with/by/in

steps.m: with it (referring to ٱلَّذِىٓ - that which)
steps.n: Added هِ (it) to show what is being referred to. This connects back to ٱلَّذِىٓ (that which) in the ayah.
Added small و mark (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Starting with the basic preposition عَلَى which shows something being on top of or upon something else
When adding a pronoun after عَلَى, the ى changes to يْ to make it easier to connect
Added نَا at the end to show this applies to 'us'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to pattern فَعِيل to make it mean 'someone who does the action a lot' (like protector/guardian)
The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon by the verb تَجِدُ (you find) in the phrase 'you will not find a guardian'
This is a basic exception particle that introduces something excluded from a previous statement. It is not derived from a root.
Adding the shadda and alif strengthens and completes the exception meaning. In this ayah, it's excluding something from the mercy (رَحْمَةً) mentioned right after it.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun instead of an action
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of mercy
Added ً (tanween) to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. It has no root since it's a helper word.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a voweled letter at the end of the previous word (ةً in رَحْمَةً). This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

steps.m: to care for, raise, nurture to completion

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
The extra mark (shadda) at the start shows this word is connected to the word before it, making it flow together smoothly
The kasra shows this word is connected to something after it
The كَ at the end shows this is your Lord. This word comes after مِّن (from) in the ayah.
Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphasis word that makes the following statement stronger. Here it emphasizes that Allah's favor upon the Prophet was truly great.

steps.m: to give extra or do more than required

steps.n: The root letters that mean 'to give extra' are arranged in a simple pattern (fa-'l) to make a noun meaning 'a favor/blessing' - something extra that is given. Like changing 'give' to 'gift'.
Adds هُ (his) at the end to show the favor belongs to Allah. This word is doing the action of كَانَ (was) in this ayah.
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he was' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word is describing how Allah's favor (فَضْلَهُۥ) was upon the Prophet.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun suffix
Added كَ at the end to show this applies to 'you' (singular)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (action) to a descriptive word by adding ي and changing vowels
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing His favor (فَضْلَهُۥ) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
This is a command telling one person to speak. The و is dropped because it's between two different vowels. The command is from Allah to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the people.
Starting with the basic conditional particle إِنْ (if). The نْ loses its sukun when connected to what follows.
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'
Added shadda on ل to make the emphasis even stronger
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ا at start and ت after first letter to show they gathered themselves together, not someone gathering them

steps.m: gathered together (referring to الإنس والجن - mankind and jinn)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show it's talking about a group (الإنس والجن), like saying 'they' instead of 'he'

steps.m: to be close or familiar with others

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun that refers to humans as social beings who live together in communities

steps.m: the humans (subject of يَأْتُونَ - they bring)
steps.n: Added 'the' to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action in the sentence with the jinn

steps.m: to hide or be hidden from sight

steps.n: The ِ (kasra) and ّ (shadda) transform the concept of 'being hidden' into the beings that are hidden from human sight
Adds ٱلْ to specify these particular jinn mentioned in the ayah. The ُ shows this is the subject doing the action in يَأْتُوا (they come/bring)
Adds وَ to connect this word with ٱلْإِنسُ (humans) from the previous part, showing both groups together cannot match the Quran
Basic form of the preposition showing position or responsibility
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) which is a spelling variation of ى at the end of words
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show this connects to the next word أَن يَأْتُوا۟ (that they bring)
This is a special word that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what the humans and jinn would try to do - which is 'to bring' (يَأْتُوا۟) something like the Quran.
Adds يَ at start to make it 'he' in present tense
Changes ending to make it mean 'they' instead of 'he'
Adds ا to show this is in the subjunctive mood (after words like 'that', 'so that', etc.)

steps.m: to be similar or equal to something

steps.n: Changed the idea of being similar into a noun meaning 'a similar thing'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to something after it

steps.m: with/by a similar thing (of هٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ)
steps.n: Added bi- at the beginning to show this is talking about doing something with or by means of something similar. This word is connected to 'this Quran' in the ayah.
Basic pointing word that shows something is near
The alif is written as a small mark above the ه to show it's stretched when pronounced
Added ذ with fatha to make it specifically point to something
Added alif at the end to show we're pointing to something masculine and singular. In this ayah, it's pointing to the Quran.
Changes from 'to read' into 'something that is read' by adding -aan ending
Makes it refer specifically to the Quran as THE recitation
Shows this word follows بِمِثْلِ (like/similar to) in the ayah
This is a negative word that tells us the action after it won't happen. In this ayah, it's saying humans and jinn will not be able to produce something like the Quran.
Added يَ at start to show 'he' in present tense, changed root letters to make a complete word with vowel marks

steps.m: they come (referring to humans and jinn together)
steps.n: Added ون with fatha to show multiple subjects (humans and jinn mentioned in ayah). Changed kasra to damma before the plural ending.
Added vowels to make a noun meaning 'something similar'
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with'. This word relates to القُرْءَانِ (the Quran) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added ِ at end to show this word is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: with something similar to it (referring to القُرْءَانِ)
steps.n: Added هِ at end meaning 'it' which refers back to the Quran mentioned earlier
This is a connecting particle that joins this phrase to what came before
Added fatha to show how the و is pronounced

steps.m: and even if (connects to يَأْتُونَ - they bring)
steps.n: Added لَوْ which means 'if/even if' to create a conditional phrase. This combination introduces a hypothetical situation that follows.
Added fathas to make it past tense before the weak letter change

steps.m: he/it was (describing the state of بَعْضُهُمْ - some of them)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb

steps.m: to divide into parts or portions

steps.n: The root letters get vowel marks (fatHa, sukun, Damma) to make the word mean 'a part/portion'. The Damma ending shows it's doing the action in the sentence.
Adds the pronoun 'them' to show who the part refers to. This word is still the subject of كَانَ (was) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be part of something, to divide into portions

steps.n: Forms a noun meaning 'part' or 'some' by adding vowel marks to the root letters
Added لِ at beginning to mean 'to/for' and tanwin at end to show it's indefinite. This word helps explain بَعْضُهُمْ (some of them) in the ayah.
Added ي between the last two root letters and vowels to create a word for someone who does this action intensively

steps.m: a helper/supporter (for others against this Quran)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence. In this ayah, it refers to someone being a helper for others against the Quran.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced
Added ل at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'
Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he explained thoroughly (by turning things different ways)
steps.n: The doubled ر shows doing something repeatedly or thoroughly in different ways

steps.m: we explained thoroughly (in this Quran for mankind)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This connects to لِلنَّاسِ (for mankind) in the ayah, showing who the explanation was for.
Changes the concept of being close to others into a word for the group that is close to each other - people
Adding ال makes it specific people we're talking about. The ن gets doubled (shadda) because it's a special letter that combines with ال
Adding ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is connected to another word, often meaning 'of the people'

steps.m: for/to the people (in هٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ - this Quran)
steps.n: Adding لِ at the start means 'for' or 'to'. In this ayah, it's talking about what was explained for people in the Quran
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to 'this Quran' to show where the examples are found.
This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to something nearby. In this ayah, it's specifically pointing to the Quran (القُرْءَانِ) which comes right after it.
Changed the root into a noun form that shows the result of the action of reading/reciting
Adding ال makes it refer specifically to the Quran as THE recitation
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word في (in) in the ayah

steps.m: from (showing the source of كُلِّ مَثَلٍ - every example)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects to كُلِّ مَثَلٍ (every example) to show the source or type.

steps.m: from (showing the source of كُلِّ مَثَلٍ - every example)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word connects to what comes after it
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to مَثَلٍ (example) that comes after it, showing it means 'every example' or 'all examples'

steps.m: to compare or be like something

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'compare' into a noun meaning 'example' by adding the vowels a-a
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is any general example, not a specific one. This word follows كُلِّ (every) in the ayah.
Added fathas to show past tense. The ى at the end is because this is a weak verb (the root ends with ي).
Added فَ at the beginning to show this refusal happened as a result of what came before in the verse
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) which are spelling variations that don't change the meaning
Base form showing the trilateral root with appropriate vowelization for this meaning
Added vowels to make a past tense verb showing something increased
Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'most'. Changed the middle vowels to make it work as a word meaning 'most' that describes 'the people'

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: The root 'to be social' changes to mean 'people' by adding نَا at the start. When we add لِ before it, it means 'for the people'
Adds ال which means 'the'. The ل in ال combines with the ن to make a stronger ن sound (shown by ّ)
This is the basic form of the exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before it.
Added shadda and alif to strengthen the exception meaning. In this ayah, it excludes 'rejection' (كُفُورًا) from 'refused' (أَبَىٰٓ), showing that most people chose nothing but rejection.
Basic past tense verb 'he did' pattern (fa3ala) from root meaning 'reject/deny'
Changed vowels to damma and added و to show this is a strong form of rejection
Added ًا to show this word is being acted upon - it's what the people refused in the phrase 'they refused except total rejection'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. Added fatha vowels to show past tense.
Adding وا at the end makes it refer to a group (they) instead of just one person (he)

steps.m: and they said (subject of لَن نُّؤْمِنَ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to what comes after it, where they say 'we will not believe'.
This is a special word that makes something impossible in the future. Here it makes the verb نُّؤْمِنَ (we will believe) into 'we will never believe'.
Changes to first person plural (we) form
Changed the ending vowel from 'a' to 'u' to show it's a statement rather than a wish/command
Changed to fatha ending after لَن (which means 'will not'). The shadda on ن is because two ن sounds came together

steps.m: for you/to you (object of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: This combines two parts: ل (for/to) with ك (you). The word لَكَ is being used to show who they are speaking to in the phrase 'they said to you'.
This is a preposition that shows when something continues up to a certain point in time or place, like saying 'up to' or 'until' in English.
Added the basic vowel marks and shadda that are always part of this word. The shadda shows we say the 't' sound twice.

Added the small alif (ٰ) above the final ى. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to تَفْجُرَ (you cause to gush forth) to show the limit of what they're waiting for.

steps.m: to make something burst forth/gush out

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he made burst' to present tense 'make burst' by changing the vowels

steps.m: you make (something) burst forth (in response to لَن نُّؤْمِنَ لَكَ)
steps.n: Added تَ prefix to show 'you' as the subject. This is in response to the previous statement 'we will not believe you'
First person plural pronoun meaning 'us', 'our', or 'we' when attached to words. Here it's receiving the action of تَفْجُرَ (you cause to gush forth)
Added لَ at the beginning to show the meaning 'for' or 'to'. This shows who will benefit from the action تَفْجُرَ (you cause to gush forth)
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from or out of something else. Here it connects to 'the earth' (الْأَرْضِ) to show where the spring should come from.
Added a fatha (َ) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الْأَرْضِ (the earth). This is like adding a small bridge between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Adding ال makes it specific - the earth we know
The kasra (ِ) shows this word comes after مِنَ (from) in the ayah

steps.m: to gush forth or spring up (like water)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The و after ب changes it from a verb (to gush) into a noun meaning 'lots of gushing'
Adding ي changes it from just 'gushing' to 'a place where water gushes out'
The ًا at the end shows this is 'a' spring rather than 'the' spring

steps.m: a spring/fountain (object of تَفْجُرَ - you cause to gush forth)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb تَفْجُرَ (you cause to gush forth) in the ayah
This is the basic form of the word that gives a choice between options

steps.m: or (connecting تَكُونَ with what came before)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word. In this ayah, it connects two possible things being requested.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Added ت at start to make it mean 'you'
The َ at the end shows this is after 'that' or similar words
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added ك with fatha at the end to show this is directed to one person (you). This word connects to جَنَّةٌ (garden) in this ayah, showing the garden would belong to you.

steps.m: to cover or protect something by surrounding it
steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make them pronounceable
The doubled ن (with shadda) and ة ending transform it from a verb into a noun meaning a protected/covered place
Adding the double damma makes it indefinite (a garden vs. the garden)
Basic preposition showing source or origin

steps.m: from/of (describing what جَنَّةٌ 'garden' is made of)
steps.n: Added shadda because this word comes after a word ending in a vowel (لَكَ). The shadda helps connect the words smoothly in pronunciation. This word introduces what type of garden is being discussed - one of palm trees (نَّخِيلٍ) and grapes (وَعِنَبٍ).

steps.m: to extract or select (like picking fruit)

steps.n: Changed from basic root to noun meaning 'many palm trees'
The ن gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after مِن, and gets tanween kasra (-in sound) at the end to show it belongs to something
Adding basic vowel marks to make the word pronounceable
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous word
Adds a mark at the end to show it's receiving the action

steps.m: to burst forth or make something flow

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled ج shows the action is done with force or intensity
Changed to show 'you' doing the action instead of 'he'

steps.m: and then you make (something) burst forth forcefully (connected to تَكُونَ)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this action with the previous verb تَكُونَ (you have)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun form
This is the broken plural form showing multiple rivers
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to specific rivers rather than just any rivers

steps.m: the rivers (object of فَتُفَجِّرَ - 'then you cause to gush forth')
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object being acted upon by the verb فَتُفَجِّرَ (then you cause to gush forth)
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters
Changes from singular to plural form
Added fatha at the end to prepare for the attached pronoun
Added ها meaning 'it/her' to show what is being passed through (the garden). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this form.

steps.m: to burst forth or flow forcefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun showing repeated or intensive action

steps.m: with a forceful bursting forth (describing how the rivers flow in فَتُفَجِّرَ الْأَنْهٰرَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the rivers burst forth in the phrase 'so you make the rivers burst forth'
Basic particle that connects two choices or alternatives. In this ayah, it connects two demands being made: dropping the sky in pieces OR bringing Allah and the angels.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و
Added alif at beginning to show causing something else to fall
Changed to 'you' form by adding تُ at start and changing vowel marks. This word is acting on السَّمَآءَ (the sky) in this ayah.
Basic concept of height or elevation
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding fatha marks (َ) above each letter, meaning 'it rose up'
Changed from a verb meaning 'rise up' to a noun meaning 'sky' by adding a long 'aa' sound and hamza at the end
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the sky' - the س gets doubled (shadda) when ال is added

steps.m: the sky (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence (the thing that will be made to fall)
Basic comparison particle that shows similarity
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: just as, like what (connects to زَعَمْتَ - you claimed)
steps.n: Added ما to make the comparison more specific. This word connects to زَعَمْتَ (you claimed) to say 'just as you claimed'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added sukun to prepare for adding the 'you' ending
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' (masculine singular) did this action in the past. In this ayah, the disbelievers are addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ about his claims.
Starting with the basic preposition على which means 'upon' or 'on'
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation
Changed the ending to prepare for connecting with نا

steps.m: upon us (object of تُسْقِطَ - you cause to fall)
steps.n: Added نا at the end which means 'us'. In this ayah, this word shows who the sky would fall upon, connecting to the verb تُسْقِطَ (you cause to fall).
Basic concept of breaking or splitting
Changed the word pattern to show multiple pieces or fragments. This form is often used for parts that come from breaking something whole.

steps.m: pieces (object of تُسْقِطَ - to make fall)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action. In this ayah, it refers to the pieces of sky that would fall, as mentioned in تُسْقِطَ ٱلسَّمَآءَ (make the sky fall).

steps.m: or (connects تُسْقِطَ 'you cause to fall' with تَأْتِىَ 'you bring')
steps.n: This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects two things being demanded: causing the sky to fall and bringing Allah and angels.
Connected the root letters together
Added ت at beginning to make it present tense and show who is doing it (you/she)
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Added fatha at end to show subjunctive mood
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one true God, not just any deity
Added fatha on the shadda for proper pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added بِ at the beginning to show doing something with or by means of Allah. Also added the connecting alif (ٱ) that comes between بِ and ال

steps.m: to control or have power over something

steps.n: Changed the root 'to control/have power' into a noun meaning 'angel' by adding the short 'a' sounds (fatha marks)
Changed the middle of the word to make it plural - one angel becomes many angels
Added ة to complete the plural form
Added ٱلْ to make it specific - these particular angels
Added وَ meaning 'and' to connect this word to what came before it
Added ِ to show this word follows something else in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'faced' to noun meaning 'group/tribe'

steps.m: as a group/tribe (describing how Allah and the angels would come)
steps.n: This word describes how Allah and the angels would come - as a unified group. It's the object of the verb تَأْتِىَ (you come with)
This is a connecting word that gives two options in this ayah: either 'you have a house' or 'you climb'. It's like saying 'Would you like chocolate OR vanilla?'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added يَ at start and changed middle vowel to show present/future tense
Added fatha at end to show subjunctive mood
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: for you/to you (addressed to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha at the end to show this is directed to a single male person being spoken to
Root letters ب-ي-ت form the simple noun 'house' by adding vowels. Like changing 'live' to 'home'.
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of يَكُونَ (to be/have). The meaning is 'a house' rather than 'the house' because of the tanween.
The base form مِن gets a shadda when followed by certain letters. This is a phonetic change for easier pronunciation.
Changed the word from an action (decorating) to a thing (gold/decoration)
Added tanween kasra to show this describes what kind of house it is (a house of gold/decoration). This happens because it comes after the word مِن (from/of).
This is a basic conjunction that connects two options: 'having a house of gold' or 'ascending into the sky'. It shows these are alternative demands being made.

steps.m: to rise, ascend, or go up

steps.n: Basic past tense verb made by adding fathas (small a sounds) to the root letters. The ي becomes ى at the end, which is common in Arabic verbs.
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed first vowel to sukun to show present tense
Added alif maksura with superscript alif to show this is connected to 'into the sky' in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it shows being 'in' the sky.
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning. The ل merges with س creating a doubled sound (shown by ّ)
Added ِ at the end because it comes after في (in), which makes it 'of the sky'. The آ is just another way to write the long aa sound.
The sky/heaven is owned by someone (shown by the kasra/i sound at the end). This happens when something owns or possesses something else.

steps.m: never/will not (negates نُّؤْمِنَ - we will believe)
steps.n: This is a particle that strongly negates future actions. Here it's negating the verb نُّؤْمِنَ (we will believe), telling us they will never believe.

steps.m: and never/will not (negates نُّؤْمِنَ - we will believe)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English.

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure with

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed from 'he believed' to 'we believe' by adding نُ (nu) at start. The أ (hamza) moves after the prefix.
Changed from 'we believe' to 'that we believe' by adding َ (fatha) at the end
Added shadda on ن for pronunciation when connecting with previous word
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he went up'
Changes from a verb (he went up) to a noun meaning the act of going up
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose (related to نُّؤْمِنَ - we will believe)

steps.m: for your going up (connected to نُّؤْمِنَ - we will believe)
steps.n: Added كَ to show it's your going up. This whole phrase explains what they won't believe in.
Added vowel marks to the basic particle form
Added shadda to strengthen the middle letter, which is common in particles
Added alif maqsura with superscript alif at the end. This particle connects to the verb تُنَزِّلَ (you bring down) to show the endpoint of the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Adding shadda to middle radical creates intensive/repetitive meaning

steps.m: you send down repeatedly (addressing the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he sent down' to 'you send down' by adding تُ prefix - not passive voice
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun suffix
Added نا at the end to show this means 'us'
Changed to a noun meaning the thing that results from writing

steps.m: a book (that is being sent down)
steps.n: Added ًا (an alif with tanween) at the end to show this is the object - the thing that the action is happening to
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Added ن at beginning to show 'we' and changed vowels for present tense
Added emphasis with shadda on ن
Added هُ (hu) at the end to mean 'it' - shows what we are reading
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma (ُ) on first letter, and 3) adding sukun (ْ) on last letter. This is telling one person to speak.
Changed the verb into a noun showing the concept of glory/perfection
The ان ending makes it stronger, like saying 'extremely glorified'
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end to show it belongs to or connects with another word, like saying 'my Lord' or 'Lord of'
Added ى at the end to show it belongs to me (like saying 'my' in English)
Basic question word that turns a statement into a yes/no question. Here it's asking about كُنتُ (I was) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
The middle weak letter و drops out and the first vowel changes to damma due to a phonetic rule with hollow verbs
Added تُ at the end to show that 'I' did this action in the past

steps.m: except (connecting to بَشَرًا رَّسُولًا - a human messenger)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows an exception - it connects what comes before it with what comes after it. Here it's saying that the Prophet ﷺ is nothing except/but a human messenger.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun

steps.m: a human being (object of كُنتُ - I was)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon. This word is what the speaker was (object of كُنتُ - I was) in this ayah.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Makes the word indefinite and shows it's being used as an object
Basic particle ما which can mean either 'what' or act as a negation. In this ayah, it negates the verb مَنَعَ (prevented), making the meaning 'did not prevent'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to prevent or stop something from happening
he prevented (subject of prevented الناس/the people from believing)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is followed by الناس (the people) who are being prevented from something.

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: Changes the basic meaning into a word for those who do this action - people are social beings who live together
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
Adding shadda makes the ن sound stronger, emphasizing the word

steps.m: the people (as the ones being prevented from believing)
steps.n: The fatha shows this word is receiving the action of منَعَ (prevented) in this ayah
This is a connecting word that joins يُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ (they believe) to what follows. It works like 'that' in English when you say 'I know that he came.'

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure with

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to form past tense verb
Changed first letter to make it mean causing others to believe/have faith
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he believes' to 'they believe' by adding وا at the end

steps.m: when (connects to جَآءَهُمُ - came to them)
steps.n: This is a word that tells when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects to جَآءَهُمُ (came to them) to show when the guidance came.
The middle letter changes to alif due to weak letter rules
Added هُمُ at the end to show who the action was done to - in this case, 'them' (a group of people)

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the first vowel to damma to make it passive (something was guided)
Added 'ال' (the) at the start to make it 'the guidance' instead of just 'guidance', with a long sound at the end
Added shadda (ّ) to emphasize the meaning. This makes the 'l' sound stronger.
Added long alif (آ) at the end. This elongated sound helps connect it smoothly to the next phrase 'that they said' (أَن قَالُوٓا۟).
This is a connecting word that joins what comes before with what comes after. Here it connects 'what prevented the people' with 'they believe', showing what the people were prevented from doing.
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to make 'they said' instead of 'he said'
Added optional marks (ٓ and ۟) that don't change the meaning but help with proper recitation

steps.m: to send forth or raise up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it past tense
Added hamza at beginning to make this a question. This is asking in disbelief about Allah sending a human messenger.
Changed the verb concept into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Root letters connected with short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the word for 'human'

steps.m: a human being (object of أَبَعَثَ - did He send)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being sent in the question 'Did Allah send a human as a messenger?'
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Adds two small diagonal lines (tanween) to show this is indefinite and in accusative case
Connected the root letters together with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by dropping the first vowel and changing middle vowel to 'oo' sound to make it a command

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to tell the disbelievers)
steps.n: The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter. This is common in Arabic when certain vowel patterns meet weak letters.
This is a basic conditional particle used for unlikely or impossible situations. It introduces a hypothetical scenario that didn't happen.
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda. This happens because the word comes after قُل (Say), making the statement stronger.
Added vowels to make it past tense and show who did the action

steps.m: he was (describing what مَلٰٓئِكَةٌ/angels were like)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic for words with و in the middle.
This is a basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows the angels would be in/on the earth.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make it mean 'earth' as a specific thing you can touch
Added 'al' to make it definite and kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِي (in)
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make the word for 'angel' - like turning a verb into a person/being who does that action
Added alif and changed the middle vowel to make it plural, showing there are many angels
Added ة at the end to show this is a complete group of angels
Added tanween damma (two dots on top) to show these angels are the main doers of the action
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with sukun on first root letter

steps.m: they walk/are walking (referring to angels in مَلٰٓئِكَةٌ)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to the angels (مَلٰٓئِكَةٌ) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to feel safe and at peace
to make someone feel safe and at peace
steps.n: Adds vowel marks to make it a complete verb

steps.m: one who feels safe and at peace
steps.n: Added مُ at the start to show who does the action, and changed the middle vowels to show an ongoing state

steps.m: those who are feeling safe and at peace (walking on earth)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This describes the angels (ملائكة) in the verse if they were walking on earth.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this happens multiple times or with extra force
The لَ at the beginning shows this is a response to a condition (if this happened, then we would surely do this)
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. The ْ (sukun) is added before نَا to help pronunciation.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is just a spelling change.
Added هِم which means 'them' (referring to a group)
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added shadda to emphasize the 'm' sound, which happens when certain letters come before مِن
Added fatha at the end to connect this word to the next word السَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven), showing what we're coming from
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added ال to make it definite. The shadda appears on the س because it comes after ال
The kasra shows this word comes after مِنَ which means 'from'
The alif is written longer (with a madda) but pronounced the same way - it's still 'the sky'

steps.m: to have power or authority over something

steps.n: Changed the root 'to rule/have power' into 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a

steps.m: an angel (object of لَنَزَّلْنَا - we would have sent down)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show this is receiving the action (something being sent down)
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Added shadda for emphasis

steps.m: a messenger (object of لَنَزَّلْنَا - we would have sent down)
steps.n: Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object being sent down in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it a command - damma on first letter and sukun on last letter
The weak letter و is removed to make the command form easier to say
Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he was sufficient (subject of قُلْ - say)
steps.n: Added the special ending ىٰ (alif maqsurah) because this word ends with the weak letter ي. This word is what Allah commands the Prophet to say in this verse.
This is the base word - the proper name of God in Arabic. It's not derived from a root like other words.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

steps.m: by Allah (as a witness in شَهِيدًۢا)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show Allah is being used as a means or instrument. In this ayah, Allah is being called upon as a witness.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb 'witnessed' into 'witness' by adding ي to make it a noun meaning 'one who witnesses'

steps.m: as a witness (between me and you)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object of كَفَىٰ (is sufficient). The word describes Allah being witness between the Prophet and those he's speaking to.
Changed the verb into a word meaning the space or relationship between things. This appears with بَيْنَكُمْ (between you) in this ayah to show separation between two parties.
Added ِى at the end to show this means 'between me'. The kasra (ِ) comes before the ى because this word follows another word in the ayah.

steps.m: between (connected to بَيْنِى in the ayah)
steps.n: The root meaning of separation becomes a word meaning 'between' - the space that separates things. This word pairs with بَيْنِى (between me) to show the two sides that Allah is witness between.

steps.m: and between (connected to بَيْنِى in the ayah)
steps.n: Adding the coordinating conjunction 'and'

steps.m: and between you all (paired with بَيْنِى meaning 'between me and you all')
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this refers to multiple people being addressed. Together with بَيْنِى (between me) from earlier in the ayah, it forms the complete meaning 'between me and you all' that Allah is witness to.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' at the start of a sentence
Adding هُ shows who the word is talking about (in this case 'he' or 'it'). This word connects to كَانَ (was) later in the sentence, telling us who 'was'.

steps.m: to be or exist in a state/way

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'. Shows Allah being All-Aware and All-Seeing.
The و (waw) changes to ا (alif) because it's a hollow verb - this is a common pattern for verbs with و in the middle
Changes the word from an action (to serve) into people who do that action (servants). The ِ and ا pattern makes it plural.
The بِ at the beginning shows how something is done - in this case, it means 'with' or 'by'
Adding هِ shows these are His servants (referring to Allah). The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: to be aware/informed of something through experience
one who is thoroughly aware/informed (describing Allah)
steps.n: Forms an intensive adjective describing Allah's complete knowledge and awareness

steps.m: All-Aware (describing Allah as the object of كَانَ - He was)
steps.n: Adds -an ending to show this word describes what Allah was (connects to كَانَ before it)
Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who sees in a complete and perfect way. When used for Allah, it means He sees everything completely.

steps.m: All-Seeing (describing how Allah was with His servants in كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this describes how Allah was with His servants. This word connects to كَانَ (was) earlier in the verse.
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + من (who/whoever)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The final ي changes to ى when it has a fatha.
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels - like changing 'guided' to 'guides'

steps.m: he guides (after مَن in a conditional phrase)
steps.n: The final ي is dropped because this word comes after مَن (whoever) in a conditional phrase

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who guides)
steps.n: Added ال to specify THE one true deity, doubled the ل with shadda, and added ُ to show this is the doer of guidance in this verse. The hamza from إِلَه is dropped when ال is added.
This is a basic pronoun that means 'he' or 'it' and often implies 'is' in Arabic
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about guidance. This word connects back to يَهْدِ (guides) showing the result of being guided.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way
steps.n: This root is about guiding or directing someone to the right path
Added vowels (َ) to make it mean 'he guided' in the past tense
Added مُ at start and ت in middle to change from 'guiding' to 'being guided' - like changing from teacher to student
Added ال to make it specific and dropped the final ي because it comes at the end of the phrase
This is a question word that can also mean 'whoever'. It's used here to start a conditional phrase meaning 'whoever/anyone who'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word starts a conditional phrase about 'whoever Allah guides'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show causing someone else to do the action

steps.m: he causes (someone) to lose the way (subject: اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Changed from past to present tense. The subject is Allah (اللَّهُ) who is mentioned earlier in the verse. The verb means 'to make someone go astray/lose their way'.
Added fatha to show this is a particle that makes the verb after it negative and changes it to future tense. It negates the verb يَجِدَ (find) in this ayah.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before. The فَ here connects this statement to the previous part about those who are misguided.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding ي at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the start.

steps.m: you will find (after فَلَن meaning 'will never')
steps.n: Changed ي to ت to show 'you' instead of 'he', and final vowel changed to fatha because it comes after فَلَن (will never) in the ayah
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ which means 'him/his'
Changed from singular 'him' to plural 'them' by adding م with sukun
Connected the root letters and added vowels to create the singular form meaning one who protects or supports
Changed to broken plural form by adding hamza at start, changing vowel pattern, and adding اء at end

steps.m: protectors/guardians (object of تَجِدَ - you will find)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the verb تَجِدَ (you will find) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing separation, distance, or origin. Here it combines with دُونِهِۦ (besides/other than Him) to mean 'from besides Him'
This is an adverb particle that doesn't come from a root
The damma and fatha marks help show this word's role in the sentence
Changed ending to -i because it's connected to the next word (like saying 'besides the...')
Added هِۦ at the end to show this refers to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is the basic concept of gathering or assembling things or people together
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added نَ at start to show 'we' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it

steps.m: and we will gather them (on يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ - Day of Resurrection)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being gathered. The context shows this will happen in the future on the Day of Resurrection.

steps.m: day (object of وَنَحْشُرُهُمْ - We will gather them)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the word 'day'. Shows when the gathering will happen.

steps.m: on the day (object of وَنَحْشُرُهُمْ - We will gather them)
steps.n: Adding a small diagonal line (fatha) at the end shows this word is receiving the action 'We will gather them'
The root word changes to show the action of standing/rising happening now, and the middle letter و becomes ي
Adding ة makes this a specific type of rising - the resurrection
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is being possessed by another word

steps.m: the resurrection (يَوْمَ الْقِيٰمَةِ - Day of Resurrection)
steps.n: Adding definite article makes this specific resurrection. This word appears in the phrase 'Day of Resurrection' (يَوْمَ الْقِيٰمَةِ) where it describes which day.
Basic preposition showing position above something or superiority. In this ayah, it connects to وُجُوهِهِمْ (their faces) to show they will be gathered on their faces.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is the end of the word. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the physical face
Changes from one face to many faces by changing the vowels inside the word
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added the suffix هِمْ to show these faces belong to them
The ُ (damma) changes it from singular to plural, like saying 'blind people' instead of 'a blind person'

steps.m: blind (describing how they will be gathered)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this describes their state when being gathered (connected to نَحْشُرُهُمْ - We will gather them)
Added vowels to make it mean 'he was mute/unable to speak' in the past tense
Changed vowels to make it a plural noun meaning 'those who are mute'
Added tanween alif to show these people are objects of the verb نَحْشُرُهُمْ (We will gather them)

steps.m: and mute ones (as objects of نَحْشُرُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this with the previous description عُمْيًا (blind ones)
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to make it a plural noun meaning 'deaf ones'
Added tanween alif to show these deaf ones are being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: and deaf ones (as objects of نَحْشُرُهُمْ - we will gather them)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word to the previous words عُمْيًا وَبُكْمًا (blind and mute), listing the conditions in which they will be gathered
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens. The ى at the end is because the last root letter is weak.

steps.m: their place of shelter/refuge (referring to those mentioned in previous ayah)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this belongs to them. This refers back to the people mentioned in the previous ayah who were led astray.

steps.m: their place of shelter/refuge (emphasized because it refers to Hell)
steps.n: The shadda on م adds emphasis because this word is referring to Hell (جَهَنَّمُ) in the next ayah.
Base form of the word. This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew 'Gehinnom', referring to Hell. It has no Arabic root.
Added vowel marks and shadda to show pronunciation. The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject of the sentence, specifically relating to مَّأْوَىٰهُمْ (their refuge).
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
The fatha shows this connects to the word after it
Adding ما changes this from just 'every' to 'whenever' - it connects to the verb خَبَتْ (died down) in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: it (the fire) died down (subject of جَهَنَّمُ)
steps.n: Changed the last letter to ت to show it's talking about Hell-fire (which is like 'she' in Arabic), like saying 'she died down'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it a command to one person
Added نا to make it 'us' - asking for increase for a group
Changed from a command (increase us!) to past tense (we increased) and added هُمْ (them) at the end to show who received the increase
Adding ي between the last two root letters with the vowels makes this word mean something that burns intensely

steps.m: a blazing fire (object of زِدْنٰهُمْ - We increased them)
steps.n: Adding ًا at the end shows this is what Allah increased them in, connecting it to the verb زِدْنٰهُمْ (We increased them) in the ayah
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far
Adding كَ shows that the thing being pointed to is far away

steps.m: to compensate or repay (with good or bad)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he repaid' (action) to 'repayment' (thing) by adding alif and hamza at the end
Adds tanween (double damma) to show this is any repayment, not a specific one
Changed from 'a repayment' to 'the repayment' by adding damma (u sound) at the end to show it's the main subject being talked about
Added هُم to show this belongs to them (refers to the disbelievers mentioned in the context)
This is the basic emphatic particle that confirms something
Adding بِ at the beginning changes 'that' to 'because' - it shows the reason for something
Adding هُمْ at the end shows who the word is talking about - in this case, it refers to those who disbelieved, mentioned in the next word كَفَرُوا۟
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. The small mark (۟) above وا is just to help with pronunciation.
Root meaning 'show/appear' became a noun meaning 'a sign' by adding alif and ta marbuta
Made the word plural to show multiple signs
Added نَا ('our') at the end to show these are Allah's signs and proofs
Added 'by/in' at start to show how these signs are being used
Connected the root letters and added vowels. The middle root letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Adding وا makes it plural masculine - meaning more than one man did the action
Adding و connects this with the previous statement about them disbelieving in Allah's signs. The ٓا۟ marks are pronunciation helpers.
This is a word that refers to a future time, like saying 'when' something will happen
The initial إِ changes to ءِ when it comes after another letter
Added أَ at the beginning to make it into a question, like adding 'is it?' in English
The middle weak letter و drops out and the vowels adjust according to regular rules for hollow verbs

steps.m: we were (subject of أَءِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ - 'will we be resurrected?')
steps.n: Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is part of the disbelievers' question about whether they will be resurrected.
Changed from a root meaning 'big/great' to mean 'bones' by using the plural pattern فِعَال (fi'aal)
Added ًا at the end to show these bones are what's being talked about in the sentence. This word comes after كُنَّا (we were) to tell us what state they were in.

steps.m: to crumble or break into tiny pieces
dust or tiny fragments (especially of bones)
steps.n: Forms a noun meaning the result of something being completely broken down. In this ayah, it's specifically referring to bones being broken down into dust, connected to the word عِظٰمًا (bones).
Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous word عِظٰمًا (bones), showing disbelievers questioning if they will be restored after becoming bones and dust

steps.m: and (into) dust/tiny fragments (object of كُنَّا - we became)
steps.n: Adds accusative case ending to show this is what they became, along with bones. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this Quranic spelling.
This is the basic word that shows strong emphasis
Added نا at the end to show this is about 'we/us'
Added أَء at the beginning to turn this into a question expressing disbelief or surprise

steps.m: to send forth, to raise up

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding the vowels 'a' between letters
Added مَ at start and و before last letter to show this describes who the action happens to

steps.m: ones who are sent forth/raised up
steps.n: Added plural ending to show multiple people

steps.m: indeed/surely ones who are raised up
steps.n: Added لَ to emphasize that this will definitely happen

steps.m: to create or form something from nothing

steps.n: Added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun meaning 'creation'. This is what they will be resurrected as - a new creation.
Added ًا (an) at the end to make it mean 'a creation' instead of just 'creation' - shows something new is being created

steps.m: to make something new or renew

steps.n: Added vowels and ي to make an adjective describing something that is new
Added tanween alif to show this word is describing the word خَلْقًا (creation) in the ayah
The base particle لَ combined with مْ creates a negation particle for past actions
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added أَ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question. This is often used for rhetorical questions to emphasize a point.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to make it present tense. The middle and end of the word changed to make it easier to say (رَأَيَ became رَى)

steps.m: they see (referring to the disbelievers in أَفَأَبَى ٱلظَّٰلِمُونَ)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. This refers back to the disbelievers (الظالمون) mentioned later in the verse

Adding shadda (ّ) makes the word stronger, changing it from just 'that' to 'indeed/certainly that'. This particle connects what came before (يَرَوْا - they see) with what comes after (ٱللَّهَ - Allah).
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of أَنَّ - that)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a shadda mark on the ل and an ending mark to show it's being affected by the word 'that' before it
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to what they describe, like saying 'the book which I read'

steps.m: the one who (connects خَلَقَ with اللَّهَ)
steps.n: Adding ال and doubling the ذ makes it point to something specific. Here it connects Allah (اللَّهَ) with what He did (خَلَقَ/created).

steps.m: to create or bring into existence from nothing

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes what Allah did - He created.
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Changed to plural form by adding وات
Makes the word definite by adding ال

steps.m: of the heavens/skies (object of خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Changed to show it's the object being created (with kasra ending), with slight spelling change in middle of word
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific earth/ground rather than any earth/ground
وَ means 'and', ٱلْ means 'the', and َ shows it's the object of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action
Added double damma to show this describes Allah in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows Allah's power and ability over something. In this ayah, it connects to the word قَادِرٌ (capable/able) to show what Allah has power over.
Added an alif that looks like ى (called alif maqsurah) at the end to show this is how the word is written in the Quran
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word
This is a particle that connects two ideas by introducing what someone sees, thinks, or knows. Here it connects 'they see' (يَرَوْا) with what follows.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to damma
Changed last vowel to fatha to show subjunctive mood (like 'to create' or 'that he creates')

steps.m: to resemble or be like something
similar to (describes how Allah can create others like the disbelievers)
steps.n: This form creates a noun meaning 'likeness' that is used here to describe Allah's power to create others similar to the disbelievers

steps.m: like them (refers to creating others similar to the disbelievers)
steps.n: Added 'them' at the end to show who is being made similar. The fatha (َ) before هُمْ connects it smoothly to the word.

steps.m: to make, set, or place something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense. This word shows what Allah did, connecting to how He is قَادِرٌ (capable/able) in this ayah.
Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous statement
Added fatha to the letter ل to show its pronunciation
Added هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to
Added م with sukun to change the pronoun from singular (him) to plural (them)
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make it a noun meaning the time itself rather than the action of setting it
Added tanwin alif to show this is the thing being set/appointed in the sentence. This word is what receives the action of جَعَلَ (he set/made).
Negative particle with emphasis (shown by shadda)
Changed the action of doubting into the noun 'doubt' itself
Added fatha at the end because this word is being negated by لَّا (no/not) in the ayah
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show it's a preposition meaning 'in'

steps.m: in it (referring to أَجَلًا/the appointed term)
steps.n: Added هِ (hi) at the end to mean 'it', referring back to the appointed term (أَجَلًا) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to not want, refuse, or reject
he refused/rejected (response to seeing Allah's power)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, this refusal comes after seeing Allah's power to create.

steps.m: and so he refused/rejected (as a result of seeing Allah's power)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this refusal happened as a direct result of what came before. In this ayah, it shows the disbelievers refused despite seeing clear signs.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from a past tense verb (he was unfair) to an emphasized noun (very unfair person) by adding shadda and changing vowels
Regular masculine plural
Adding ال makes the noun definite
Basic particle form used for exception
Added kasra vowel mark to help pronunciation
Added shadda to emphasize the ل sound
Added alif at the end to complete the word. This word restricts the meaning of كُفُورًا (rejection/disbelief) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show the concept of rejection itself, making it stronger

steps.m: total rejection (as the thing being rejected in أَبَى)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is what's being rejected in the phrase. It connects to the verb أَبَى (they refused) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by making it a command. The middle letter و is removed to make it shorter and easier to say as a command. This is Allah telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ what to say.
This is the basic form of the word that introduces hypothetical situations - things that didn't actually happen. It's like saying 'what if' in English.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after قُل (say), making the 'l' sound stronger. This is a common change in Arabic when certain letters come together.
This is the basic form of the pronoun used when speaking to one person
Added fatha mark on first letter to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: you all (when talking to a group)
steps.n: Added م with sukun at the end to make it plural, and damma before it to connect the sounds
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding تَ at start, adjusting vowels, and adding ُ at end
Suffix ونَ changes verb to plural form
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to show who does the action
Changed to show the place or thing that stores
Changed to broken plural form. This word is what is being possessed in the phrase 'you possess treasuries'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form the base noun
Added ة to make it a specific instance of mercy
Added kasra to show this mercy belongs to 'my Lord' (رَبِّى) in this ayah
The doubled letter shows intensity in the meaning
The word now shows it's connected to something after it (like 'Lord of...')
The ي suffix indicates first person possession (my)
The long vowel mark (ٓ) shows the word is being stretched when read, adding emphasis
Starting with the basic particle إذ which refers to time. Added kasra to show it's connected to what follows.

steps.m: then, in that case (result of تَمْلِكُونَ - you possess)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif (ًا) at the end to change the meaning from just time to showing a result or consequence. This connects what would happen (you would withhold) as a result of the condition (if you possessed).
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أ (hamza) at the start changes the meaning to 'made someone/something do the action' - like changing 'hold' to 'made hold'
Adding تُ changes subject to 'I' and keeps past tense
Adding م changes subject from 'I' to 'you all'

steps.m: surely you all would have withheld
steps.n: Adding لَّ (with shadda) at beginning adds emphasis and shows this is part of a conditional statement (after لو)
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form a basic noun
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of fear
Added fatha at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (spending). This construction means 'fear of spending'

steps.m: to spend or use up resources

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that means the action itself
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes before it in the ayah

steps.m: to be or exist in a certain state

steps.n: Added past tense vowels. This verb connects the subject (الْإِنسٰنُ - mankind) to its description (قَتُورًا - stingy).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about mankind's stinginess

steps.m: to be familiar, sociable with others

steps.n: Root means 'to be social' - changed to noun meaning 'humans' since we live in society
The suffix ان shows this is about a being with human qualities
Added ال to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the doer in the sentence

steps.m: to hold back or restrict spending

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added و after second root letter to show this is a strong quality in someone's character
Added -an ending to show this word is describing 'mankind' as an adjective
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'definitely' instead of just 'indeed'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters and added basic past tense vowels
Changed the word to make it mean 'causing something to come/bring' which gives us the meaning of 'giving'
Changed the final ى to ي with sukun to prepare for the suffix
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the giving. In this ayah, Allah is giving to Moses.
This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian, meaning 'born of water' or 'drawn out'. It does not come from Arabic root letters.
Added the elongated alif (ىٰ) at the end. This word is the subject of the verb جَآءَ (he came) in this ayah.
The root letters that represent the concept of nine
Changed vowels to make it a number word

steps.m: to show, to be clear, to be apparent

steps.n: Changes the root into a noun meaning something that shows or demonstrates truth
Base form of the word before adding plural ending

steps.m: signs, miracles, verses (acted on by تِسْعَ)
steps.n: Adds the feminine plural ending with tanwin kasra to show it's being acted upon by the word تِسْعَ (nine) before it
Shows something being temporarily clear or evident
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger
Adding the long alif makes it feminine

steps.m: clear signs/proofs (referring to the nine signs given to Moses)
steps.n: Adding ات and tanwin (تٍ) makes it plural - meaning multiple clear signs/proofs
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Removing the first vowel and adding two sukun marks changes it from 'he asked' to 'ask!' (a command)
Adding فَ shows this command follows from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it's telling Moses to ask the Children of Israel about the signs he was given.

steps.m: to build, establish, or create offspring

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowel marks
These are two different words - not a transformation. 'Bana' means built, while 'bani' means sons/children
Added a long vowel mark (ي with ـٓ) to connect it with the next word, showing it means 'children of'
First part of the compound name meaning 'servant' or 'one who struggles'

steps.m: Israel (servant of God) - name of Prophet Jacob (يَعْقُوب)
steps.n: Added the second part 'il' meaning God, making the complete name 'servant of God'. This is the name given to Prophet Jacob, and his descendants are called Children of Israel (بَنِي إِسْرٰٓءِيلَ)
