steps.m: when (referring to جَآءَهُمْ - he came to them)
steps.n: This is a word that points to when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects 'when' to the next part that says 'he came to them' (جَآءَهُمْ).
Added fatha marks to show this is a past-tense verb. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
The long alif with madda (آ) makes the word stronger, like saying 'definitely came' instead of just 'came'

steps.m: he definitely came to them (referring to the Children of Israel mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُم which means 'them' to show who he came to. This refers back to the Children of Israel (بَنِىٓ إِسْرٰٓءِيلَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to connect the letters together. This is the basic past tense form before the weak letter change.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes between two fathas. This makes it easier to pronounce. The word connects to فِرْعَوْنُ (Pharaoh) who is being spoken to.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else. In this case, it shows Pharaoh's response came after Moses brought the signs.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the action is directed towards
Added the small و mark (ۥ) after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper - it doesn't change the meaning
This is a title for ancient Egyptian kings, borrowed into Arabic as a proper noun. In this ayah, Pharaoh is speaking to Prophet Musa (Moses).
The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Pharaoh is the one doing the action (speaking) in this sentence
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed I (subject of لَأَظُنُّكَ - I surely think)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is about 'me/I'. This word connects to لَأَظُنُّكَ (I surely think) later in the verse, where Pharaoh is speaking to Moses.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he thought' to 'I think' by adding أَ at start and changing vowels. Shows uncertainty.
Added لَ prefix for emphasis. This word is part of Pharaoh's speech to Moses.

steps.m: surely I think you (speaking to مُوسَىٰ)
steps.n: Added كَ (ka) at the end to mean 'you', showing Firawn is speaking directly to Musa, saying 'I think you...'
This is the proper name Moses (مُوسَىٰ). The ىٰ at the end with the small alif shows it's a special type of name.
The يٰ at the beginning is used when calling out to someone directly. Here, Pharaoh (فِرْعَوْنُ) is speaking directly to Moses.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed the word to show someone who magic was done to, instead of someone doing magic

steps.m: bewitched/enchanted (object of لَأَظُنُّكَ - I surely think you are)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this describes what Pharaoh thinks Moses is in the phrase 'I surely think you are bewitched'

steps.m: he said (introducing what he said next)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word introduces what was said next in the ayah.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is a basic particle that shows certainty or completion of an action
Added لَ at the start to make 'indeed' even stronger - like saying 'most definitely' or 'without any doubt'. It's a way to be extra firm when speaking.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to change 'he' to 'you', since Moses is speaking to Pharaoh in this verse
Added fatha to show this is a question word. In this ayah, it asks 'what' was sent down.
The alif is elongated to add emphasis to the question. This is a stylistic change that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ changes the meaning from something happening on its own to someone causing it to happen. This word is what was known in عَلِمْتَ (you knew).
This is the attention-getting part that starts many pointing words. The alif is written as a small mark above the ه to show it's connected to what comes next.
The small mark (ٓ) shows we should stretch the sound a bit for emphasis

steps.m: these ones (pointing to things mentioned in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ؤُلَآءِ which makes it point to multiple things. In this ayah, it's pointing to the clear signs (بَصَآئِرَ) that were sent down.
This is the base form of the exception particle. It introduces something that is excluded from what was mentioned before.
The shadda and alif strengthen and complete the meaning of exception. In this ayah, it's excluding who sent down the signs - saying it was none other than the Lord of heavens and earth.
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of the meaning
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb أَنزَلَ (sent down) in this ayah
Adds اء ending to make it a noun referring to something high/elevated
Changed sky/heaven into plural by adding وات (waat) at the end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by رَبُّ (Lord) in the phrase 'Lord of the heavens'. Also changed alif to alif maqsurah for writing style.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into the basic noun meaning 'earth'
Added the definite article ال to specify a particular earth/land
Adding kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to رَبُّ (Lord) from the previous ayah

steps.m: and of the earth (possessed by رَبُّ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this word with السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) from the previous ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who does this action intensively
Added ة to make it into a specific instance or concrete example of insight
Changes from single insight to multiple insights by adding ا and changing vowels
This is a particle of emphasis that makes things more certain
The ي at the end means 'I/me/my'
The وَ at the start connects this statement to what came before. Here, Moses is speaking to Pharaoh.

steps.m: to think or believe something based on evidence

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense first person by adding أَ at start and changing vowels. The ّ (shadda) shows emphasis on the ن.
Added لَ at beginning to show emphasis or certainty in the statement

steps.m: surely I think you (are) (speaking to Pharaoh)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this action is done to 'you' (Pharaoh in this case)
Added vowel marks to the proper noun. The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject of the sentence.
Added يٰ at the beginning to show we are calling out to Pharaoh directly. This is used when speaking to someone important or in formal speech.

steps.m: to destroy or bring to ruin

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to show something that was destroyed rather than someone doing the destroying
This is what Musa says about Pharaoh, describing him as someone who will be completely destroyed. It's the second object of the verb أَظُنُّكَ (I think you).
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added alif at beginning to show purposeful wanting. This word is connected to يَسْتَفِزَّهُم (drive them out) showing what Pharaoh intended to do.

steps.m: so/then he intended/wanted (with purpose) (subject of يَسْتَفِزَّهُم)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows as a consequence of previous events. Shows Pharaoh's response to what came before.
This particle connects two actions, showing that Pharaoh 'wanted' (أَرَادَ) something, which was 'to drive them out' (يَسْتَفِزَّهُم). It's like saying 'he wanted TO do something' in English.
Connected the root letters with vowels and doubled the last letter to show intensity
Added است at the beginning to create Form X, showing someone trying to make something happen. The root's final letter is doubled.
Adding يَ at the start changes it from past tense to present/future tense

steps.m: he tries to drive them out (of الْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: Adds هم (them) at the end to show who is being driven out
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from' in English
The م gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes right after a word ending in ن or م - this is a common rule in Arabic pronunciation
Added a fatha (َ) at the end to connect it with the next word الْأَرْضِ (the earth)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from). In Arabic, words after 'from' get this ِ ending.

steps.m: to drown or sink in water

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to show causing someone else to drown
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show who was drowned

steps.m: so we drowned him (after فَأَرَادَ - he wanted)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of the previous action (he wanted)
This word asks about or refers to people
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with what came before, showing these events happened one after another
Added a shadda and fatha on the م to show emphasis and connect with previous word
Added fatha at the end to connect with the next part
Added هُۥ at the end to show 'him' - the person being referred to. The ۥ mark shows this is a long vowel sound.

steps.m: to join or bring things together

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters with vowels to show completeness

steps.m: all together (describing how something was done)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how something happened in the sentence
Added damma and sukun to make the basic command form
Adding نَا changes it from a command to 'we said' in past tense
Adding وَ at the start connects this with what came before it
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or someone
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added a small ن mark (called tanwin) after the ن to show proper pronunciation when connecting to the next word بَعْدِهِۦ (after him)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic noun meaning 'after'
The kasra (ِ) shows this word is owned by something or follows certain words like مِن (from/after)
Added هِ (him) to show who/what comes after. This whole phrase follows the word مِنْ (from/after) in the ayah.
Added ۦ after هِ which is just a pronunciation helper mark, doesn't change meaning
Adding vowel marks to create basic past tense verb form
Adding لِ changes it from 'children' to 'for/to the children'
This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew meaning Israel (Prophet Jacob's other name). No Arabic morphological analysis applies since it's a borrowed name.

Added vowel marks and spelling variations common in Quranic text: 1) Added kasra (إِ) and sukun (سْ), 2) Changed alif (ا) to alif with overbar (ٰٓ), 3) Added kasra under hamza (ءِ), and 4) Added fatha on final letter (لَ). These are pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he settled' to 'you settle!' by adding u- at start and changing middle vowel - makes it a command to one person
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added ٱ to mark this as a command
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun

steps.m: the earth (object of اسْكُنُوا - dwell in)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show this is the object being dwelled in
This is a word that introduces a future condition. In this ayah, it connects to the verb جَآءَ (came) to show 'when something came'
The prefix فَ shows this happens as a result of something before it. Here it connects what happened after they were told to live in the land.
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun by adding sukun on middle letter and removing final vowel
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb جَآءَ (came) in this ayah
Changes the verb into a word describing someone/something that comes after others
Adds feminine ending to specify this refers to the final life/world (as opposed to this worldly life)
Adds the definite article to make it refer to THE final life specifically
Adds kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by something else in the sentence
Changed vowels and removed the final vowel to prepare for adding 'we'. The ا changes back to ئ with a sukun.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is a pronoun that refers to multiple people being spoken to
The preposition بِ is added to show that something is being done with these people. In this ayah, it connects to the verb جِئْنَا (we came), showing how we came with them.
Changes from basic meaning to describe something with that quality

steps.m: all together (describing how جِئْنَا 'we brought' was done)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this word describes how the action was done. It's connected to the verb جِئْنَا (we brought) in the ayah, explaining that Allah brought them all gathered together.
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'
Added 'al' to point to a specific truth rather than truth in general
Added 'bi' to show this truth is being used as a means or tool

steps.m: and with/by the truth (connected to نَزَلَ - it descended)
steps.n: Added 'wa' to connect this to the verb نَزَلَ (it descended), showing that truth was both the means and manner of its descent
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start changes the meaning from 'came down' to 'made something come down' - like changing 'fell' to 'dropped'
Adding 'na' at the end changes 'he sent down' to 'we sent down'
We added 'hu' (meaning 'it') at the end to show what was sent down. The 'na' in the middle got shorter because of this addition.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'
Added ال to make it specific - 'the truth' instead of just any truth
Added بِ at the beginning to show this truth is being used as a tool or method (with/by the truth)

steps.m: and with the truth (connected to أَنزَلْنٰهُ - we sent it down)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this phrase to what comes before it, and added the final kasra mark to show this word's connection to the verb أَنزَلْنٰهُ (we sent it down)

steps.m: he descended (connected to أَنزَلْنٰهُ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is related to أَنزَلْنٰهُ (we sent it down) in the same verse, showing two forms of the same root being used.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb that comes after it (أَرْسَلْنٰكَ - 'We sent you'). In this context, it's saying 'We did not send you'
The alif is elongated to show emphasis in pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: and not (connects to previous verse while negating أَرْسَلْنٰكَ)
steps.n: Adding و connects this statement to the previous verse, showing this is an additional thought about how the Quran was sent down with truth
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters together
Adding أ (hamza) at the start makes it mean 'to cause/make someone send something'
Adding 'na' at the end changes it from 'he sent' to 'we sent'
The ك at the end shows you are the one being sent. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's long.
This is a basic particle that restricts or makes exceptions. It's like saying 'but only' or 'nothing but' in English.

Added alif at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it's making an exception after the phrase 'And We have not sent you' (وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنٰكَ), showing the only purpose for which the Prophet was sent.
Adding shadda makes the action more intensive - showing it happens multiple times or with more effort

steps.m: one who spreads good news (as their role)
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle creates a noun meaning 'someone who does this action as their role'

steps.m: as a bearer of good news (object of أَرْسَلْنٰكَ - We sent you)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon by the verb أَرْسَلْنٰكَ (We sent you) in this ayah

steps.m: to warn or give notice of danger
warner (one who warns others of danger)
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'someone who warns' by adding ي between the last two root letters and vowel marks

steps.m: a warner (object of أَرْسَلْنٰكَ - We sent you)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb أَرْسَلْنٰكَ (We sent you) in this ayah

steps.m: and a warner (connected to مُبَشِّرًا - bearer of good news)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this word with مُبَشِّرًا (bearer of good news) that came before it in the ayah, showing two roles: giving good news and warning
Changed from basic root 'read' into noun meaning 'reading/recitation' by adding vowel marks
Adding ان makes it mean 'intensive/complete recitation'
Added conjunction 'and' at the beginning

steps.m: and a recitation (object of فَرَقْنٰهُ - we divided it)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being divided in the phrase 'we divided it'

steps.m: to split or separate into parts

steps.n: Added fathas to make it a past tense verb
Changed final vowel to sukun to prepare for attachments
Added نٰ to show 'we' did the action. This refers to Allah's action of revealing the Quran in parts.

steps.m: we divided/separated it (the Quran) into parts (for gradual revelation)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what was divided (referring to the Quran mentioned as قُرْءَانًا earlier in the verse)
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it past tense
The ت at the start shows 'you' are doing the action
The ه (hu) at the end means 'it' - showing what is being read. Like adding 'it' in English to 'you read it'

steps.m: so that you read/recite it (purpose of فَرَقْنٰهُ - We divided it)
steps.n: The لِ at the start shows purpose - 'in order to' or 'so that'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. Here it connects to the word مُكْثٍ (intervals) to show the Quran was revealed 'upon intervals'.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word - 'a' sound (َ) after ع and after ل

steps.m: to socialize or be familiar with others

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'those who do the action'
Added 'the' at the beginning and kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَلَى (upon/to) in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that exists in its primitive form and does not undergo derivational transformations
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation - fatha on ع and ل

steps.m: upon/over (indicates position above something in relation to ٱلنَّاسِ)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) after the alif maksura to show it's being lengthened. In this ayah, it shows the Quran being revealed upon/to the people.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to create a noun expressing the concept of staying or doing something slowly

steps.m: with deliberation/slowly (describing how the Quran was revealed)
steps.n: Added tanwin (two dots under) to show it's describing 'how' something happened - like saying 'slowly' or 'carefully'
The doubled ز (shadda) transforms this into Form II, indicating gradual or repeated sending down rather than a single action
The نا at the end shows 'we' are doing the action of sending down. The word changed from 'sent down' to 'we sent down'
Added ه at the end to indicate 'it' as the object being sent down. This is a verb phrase, not a noun that would have case endings.

steps.m: and we sent it down gradually (referring to the Quran)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous statement about the Quran being divided into parts. The هُ refers back to the Quran.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: act of sending down (related to نَزَّلْنٰهُ - we sent it down)
steps.n: This form creates a noun that describes the process of sending down

steps.m: a sending down (object of وَنَزَّلْنٰهُ - and We sent it down)
steps.n: Adds -an ending to show this is the object (what was sent down)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on second letter, 3) Dropping the weak middle letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers, as shown by the next word ءَامِنُوا۟ (believe)

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure

steps.n: Added vowels to make basic past tense form
Added a long 'aa' sound at the start to mean 'really believed' or 'had strong faith'
Added وا to show many people (men) did the action
Starting with the basic pronoun that refers back to the Quran mentioned earlier
Added the preposition بِ which shows how something is done or used
Added pronunciation helpers ۦٓ after هِ to show how to read it correctly

steps.m: or (connecting 'believe in it' with 'do not believe')
steps.n: This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects the command to believe (ءَامِنُوا۟) with its opposite (لَا تُؤْمِنُوٓا۟), showing that people have a choice between believing or not believing.

steps.m: or (connecting 'believe in it' with 'do not believe')
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و to show correct pronunciation
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. Here it changes 'believe' to 'do not believe'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to trust or feel secure in something/someone

steps.n: Adding alif makes it about having faith rather than just feeling secure
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding تُ at start and changing vowels
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to show how it's pronounced
Added shadda (ّ) to strengthen the word. This makes it emphasize what comes after it more strongly. In this ayah, it emphasizes that those who were given knowledge before fall in prostration.
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the ذ letter to connect it smoothly

steps.m: those who (as the subject of يَخِرُّونَ - they fall)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in يَخِرُّونَ (they fall)
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and kasra (ِ) on middle letter to show passive voice - meaning something was done to someone rather than someone doing something
Changed the weak letter to و because of the passive voice pattern

steps.m: they were given (subject of يَخِرُّونَ - they fall)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to change from singular (he) to plural (they). This word is connected to يَخِرُّونَ (they fall) later in the ayah - both are talking about the same group of people.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter

steps.m: the knowledge (object of أُوتُوا - were given)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and fatha at the end because it's the object of the verb أُوتُوا (were given) in this ayah

steps.m: from (connects قَبْلِهِۦٓ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'before it' (قَبْلِهِۦٓ) to the previous statement about those who were given knowledge.
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make a word meaning 'before' in time or place
Added kasra at the end because it follows مِن (from) in the ayah
Added هِۦٓ to show this refers to something mentioned before (the Quran)
This is the basic form of the word that points to a specific time or moment
Adding ا at the end makes it refer to future events. In this ayah, it connects to يُتْلَىٰ (is recited) to show what happens when the Quran is recited to them.

steps.m: to recite, to read out loud

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this is happening to something rather than someone doing it
Added يُ at the start to show this is happening now. The و at the end changed to ى because it's a weak letter at the end of a passive verb.
Added the small alif (ٰ) to show this is the final form. This word connects to عَلَيْهِمْ (upon them) in the ayah.
The base word عَلَى (upon) changes its final ى to ي when connecting to pronouns

steps.m: upon them (subject of يَخِرُّونَ - they fall)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them'. This word connects to يَخِرُّونَ (they fall) in the next ayah, showing who is falling.

steps.m: to fall down suddenly or heavily

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Combined the repeated ر with a shadda to show emphasis
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the learned people mentioned before falling in prostration when hearing the Quran.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic noun
Changed from singular 'chin' to plural 'chins' by adding أَ at the start and ان at the end
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon

steps.m: to the chins (they fall upon in يَخِرُّونَ)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show direction - they fall upon their chins in prostration

steps.m: to prostrate, to bow down with forehead touching ground

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed to plural form showing people who do a lot of prostrating

steps.m: falling into prostration (describing how they fall in يَخِرُّونَ)
steps.n: Changed to show HOW they fall - with tanween alif at the end to connect it to 'they fall' (يَخِرُّونَ)
Connected the root letters together with fatha. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا changes back to و.
Added ونَ to show multiple people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
Added وَ to connect this with what comes before it. This word introduces what they actually say: 'Glory be to our Lord!'

steps.m: to be free from any flaw or imperfection

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'perfection' by adding vowel marks (damma and sukun)
Adds ان to make it a special word used for praising Allah

steps.m: absolutely perfect and free from all flaws (describing رَبِّنَآ - our Lord)
steps.n: The alif (ٰ) was added to show this is a special word praising Allah's perfection, and the fatha (َ) at the end connects it to 'our Lord'

steps.m: to care for, nurture, help grow and develop
one who cares for and nurtures (subject of يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha and doubled the middle letter to show this is someone who really takes care of others. This word is part of what people are saying in يَقُولُونَ (they say).

steps.m: one who cares for and nurtures
steps.n: Changed the vowel under the last letter to kasra to prepare for adding 'our'

steps.m: our Lord (the one who cares for us)
steps.n: Added نَآ at the end to show this is our Lord. The آ makes the sound longer when speaking.
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif. This particle emphasizes the truth of what follows it. Here it emphasizes that the promise (وَعْدُ) of our Lord was certain to be fulfilled.
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he was' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in past tense. This word is connected to وَعْدُ (promise) in this ayah.
Changed from verb 'to promise' into the noun 'a promise' by joining the root letters together
Added ُ at the end to show this word is the subject of كَانَ (was) in the ayah

steps.m: to care for, nurture, help grow and develop
one who cares for and nurtures
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does a lot of caring and nurturing

steps.m: one who cares for and nurtures (possessed)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Adding نَا shows this is 'our' Lord. The word comes after سُبْحٰنَ (glory) in the ayah, showing whose glory it is.
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to show receiving the action instead of doing it
Adds emphasis - like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
Added ًا (tanween) at the end to show this word is the predicate (describes what something is) after كَانَ
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
The doubled ر shows the intensity and completeness of falling down in prostration
Adding يَ at the start and changing the vowel to ِ makes it happen in the present/future
Adding ونَ shows it's many people doing the action together

steps.m: and they fall down prostrate (to their chins يَبْكُونَ while they cry)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this action to what comes before it. In this ayah, it shows they fall down prostrate while crying.
Connected the root letters with basic vowels
Made the word plural using the pattern أَفْعَال which is common for body parts
Added 'the' (ال) at the start and changed the ending to show it's receiving the action

steps.m: to the chins (they fall upon them in يَخِرُّونَ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show direction - they fall upon their chins in prostration
Added vowel marks to make the past tense 'he wept' from the root meaning 'to weep'
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra)
Changes from singular (he weeps) to plural (they weep) by adding ونَ at the end
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start
هم shows who receives the increase

steps.m: and he increases them (connected to يَبْكُونَ - they cry)
steps.n: Adds conjunction 'and' to connect to previous phrase
Added vowel marks and و to turn the action into a noun meaning the state of being humble

steps.m: in humble submission (describing how they fall down)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how they fall down in the ayah (وَيَخِرُّونَ - and they fall down)
Changed from basic 'say' root to a command by adding dots (vowel marks) and dropping middle letter
Added ِ at the end because the next word (ٱدْعُوا۟) starts with a hamza. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

steps.m: to call out to someone or invite them

steps.n: Removed last letter and added marks to make it a command to one person
Added helper alif at start because Arabic words can't begin with a sukun
Added وا at the end to make it a command for a group. The small circle shows it's a command.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam

steps.m: Allah (as the object being called upon)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (shadda) to make the L sound stronger, and an 'a' sound at the end to show we are calling upon Allah
This is the basic form of the word that shows a choice between options. In this ayah, it connects between calling upon Allah or calling upon Ar-Rahman (both are permitted).
The kasra (ِ) is added at the end because this word connects to the verb ٱدْعُوا۟ (call upon) that comes after it
Added vowels to connect the root letters into past tense form
Changed to command form by adding alif at start and changing vowels. The final weak letter و drops in command form.
Changed to plural command by adding وا at the end. The initial alif changes to hamzat wasl (ٱ).
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Adds ان at end to make it stronger, like saying 'very very merciful'
Added ال to make it specific. The ر gets doubled (shadda) because it's a sun letter that comes after ال
Added the alif mark (ٰ) and fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb 'call upon' (ٱدْعُوا۟)
Adding the fatha mark makes it ready for use in speech
Adding an alif and tanween at the end makes this word the object (the thing being acted upon) instead of the subject
Basic form of the word that makes something general or non-specific. Here it connects to تَدْعُوا (you call) to mean 'whatever you call'
Added shadda to emphasize the م, making the word stronger. This connects to تَدْعُوا (you call) to mean 'whichever name you call'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed vowels to prepare for present tense form. The و at the end is from the root.
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' or 'she' is doing the action

steps.m: you all call upon (command to multiple people)
steps.n: Added ا (alif) at the end to change from 'you' (singular) to 'you all' (plural) - like changing from 'you call' to 'you all call'
Starting with the connecting particle that shows this follows from the previous statement about calling upon Allah or Al-Rahman
Added the preposition ل which shows ownership or belonging

steps.m: so for Him (referring to اللَّهَ or الرَّحْمٰنَ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه which refers back to Allah or Al-Rahman mentioned in the previous part of the verse
Changes from verb 'to name' into the noun 'a name'
Changed from singular to plural form
Added 'the' to make it specific and lengthened the alif
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence فَلَهُ (then to Him belong)
Changes the verb concept into a noun meaning the quality itself
Adds feminine ending and changes meaning to express the highest degree of the quality

steps.m: the most beautiful, the best (feminine) (describing الْأَسْمَآءُ - the names)
steps.n: Makes the word definite and shows it's describing 'the names' mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added fatha to show this is a complete word that means 'not'. This word negates تَجْهَرْ (be loud) in this ayah.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject. The sukun at the end shows this is a command form telling you not to do something (because of لا before it in the ayah). This word refers to speaking loudly in prayer specifically.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Changed the verb 'he prayed' into the noun 'prayer' by adding ا after ل
Changed the و (waw) at the end to ة (ta marbuta) to make it mean 'one prayer' instead of just 'prayer'

steps.m: prayer (ready to be connected to something)
steps.n: Added -i sound at the end to link it to the next word (like saying 'prayer-of')
Added -ka at the end to show it belongs to 'you' (masculine singular)
Added bi- at start to mean 'with/by your prayer'
This is the basic negative particle لا that makes something 'not' happen. Here it's negating the verb يَجْهَرْ (be loud).
Added و at the beginning to connect this command with the previous one, like saying 'do this AND don't do that'
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Adding alif makes the action more deliberate/intense
ت at start shows this is about 'she' doing the action

steps.m: she was made to lower her voice
steps.n: Changing vowel to ُ makes this passive - something was done to her
This is a preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with it/her (referring to صَلَاتِكَ - your prayer)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to 'your prayer' mentioned earlier in the verse. This connects the word to what it's talking about.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added اِبْتَ to make the meaning stronger - like really trying hard to seek something for yourself
Changed from 'he sought' to 'you seek!' by dropping the last letter to make it a command to one person
Added وَ at the beginning to connect it to the previous phrase
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a preposition. The fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter create a pattern used for some prepositions.
Added a small diagonal line (fatha) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it
This is the basic pointing word meaning 'this'
Added an overbar (ٰ) which is just a writing variation

steps.m: that (pointing to something not very close)
steps.n: Added لِكَ to change the meaning from 'this' (close) to 'that' (far). The كَ part shows how far away it is - like pointing to something you can see but isn't right next to you.
Added ي between second and third letters to make it a noun meaning 'path'
The ًا ending shows this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is the object of the verb ابْتَغِ (seek/take). When something is the object of a verb, it takes the accusative case.
Changed to command form by adding damma on first letter and dropping the weak middle letter و. This tells one person to do something.
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. Added kasra at end because this command is connected to what follows it in the ayah.
Changed the basic idea of praising into a noun that means the act or concept of praise itself

steps.m: all praise (subject of belongs to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning which in this context means 'all praise'. The ُ ending shows this word is the subject that belongs to Allah in the phrase 'all praise belongs to Allah'
This is the base word Allah, with shadda on ل to show emphasis
Added لِ at beginning to show the praise belongs to Allah. Added ِ at end to connect it with الْحَمْدُ (the praise).
This is the base demonstrative form that points to something
Adding ٱلَّ changes this from a simple pointing word to a connecting word. Here it introduces the description that Allah did not take a child.
This is the basic form of the negative particle
Adding sukun (ْ) at the end shows this particle changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَتَّخِذْ) from present to past tense and makes it negative. It's like adding 'did not' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to show doing the action for oneself. The initial أ is dropped when ت is added.
Added يَ to show present tense action

steps.m: he takes for himself (negated by لَمْ)
steps.n: Added sukun at the end because this verb comes after لَمْ (did not)
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
The ًا ending shows this word is the object being acted upon by the verb يَتَّخِذْ (take/adopt). Allah did not take any child.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced. This particle changes verbs after it from present to past tense and negates them.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it. This connects the negation to the previous statement.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Added يَ at start to make it 'he' in present tense, and changed middle vowels to make it ongoing action
The word follows لَمْ (not) which requires the jussive form. This drops the final vowel and weak letter و
Starting with the basic particle ل which shows direction or ownership
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: surely for him (connected to يَكُن - was)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows who the action is for - in this case referring back to Allah in the ayah

steps.m: surely for him (connected to يَكُن - was)
steps.n: The small و (ۥ) is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to share or join with others

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this into a verb meaning 'to partner with'
Added ي to show this describes someone who regularly shares or partners with others
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is talking about any general partner. This word relates to فِى ٱلْمُلْكِ (in the dominion/power) that comes right after it.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to the word ٱلْمُلْكِ to show 'in the dominion/kingdom'
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning or pronunciation.

steps.m: to own or rule over something

steps.n: Changed the word from an action (owning/ruling) into the thing itself (ownership/kingdom) by adding ُ on first letter and ْ on middle letter
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific kingdom, and ِ to show it belongs to Allah (mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. لَمْ is a particle that makes verbs after it refer to the past and negates them, like saying 'did not' in English.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. In this ayah, it connects two things that Allah does not have: a son (وَلَدًا) and a partner (شَرِيكٌ).
This is the root meaning existence or being
The prefix يَ shows this is happening now. The ُ (damma) on ك shows it's active voice.
The و is dropped and the word becomes يَكُن because it comes after لَمْ (meaning 'did not'). This is like saying 'he did not exist'.
Starting with the basic particle ل that shows direction or ownership
Added هُ to show who the word is referring to (him)
Added shadda because this word connects to the previous word يَكُن (was). The ۥ is just a pronunciation helper mark that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be close to and take care of
steps.n: Root indicating closeness or authority

steps.m: he was close to/took care of
steps.n: Changed to past tense verb showing state of being close/in authority
Changed from a past tense verb (he protected) to a noun meaning protector, with shadda showing intensity. This word describes Allah as a strong guardian.
Basic form of the word meaning 'from'. This particle shows the source or origin of something.
The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the م sound because it comes after the word لَّهُۥ in the ayah. This is a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed vowels to make it passive - something happened to the subject rather than them doing it
Changed from a verb (was made lowly) to a noun (humiliation) and added ال to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of)
Root indicating greatness or largeness
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he' form meaning 'he became great/old'
Doubling the middle letter changes it from 'become great' to 'make great'
Added attached pronoun هُ as direct object meaning 'him/His'
Added conjunction وَ to connect with previous phrase
Connected the root letters with basic past tense voweling
Forms a verbal noun showing the action itself. This word is related to the command كَبِّرْ (declare His greatness) in the ayah.

steps.m: with great declaration of His greatness (object of كَبِّرْ)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this describes how Allah should be declared great, in response to the command كَبِّرْ (declare His greatness)
